op_000002.statement_formalization_notes: Used the `Tp` type and `‚áí` notation already opened in the theory context; no extra prelude declarations are needed.

op_000002.proof_sketch: Instantiate the statement at A = #"p" and B = #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"). The sequent [#"p"] ‚áí #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p") is derivable by decidability of sequents. The claimed equivalence then forces the impossible equality #"p" = #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"), contradicting constructor disjointness.
op_000002.proof_text:
intro h
have hseq : ([#"p"] ‚áí (#"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"))) := by
  decide
have heq : (#"p" : Tp) = (#"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p")) :=
  (h (A := #"p") (B := (#"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p")))).mp hseq
cases heq
op_000002.counterexample_text: Take A = #"p" and B = #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"). Then [#"p"] ‚áí #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p") holds (Lean closes it by `decide`), while (#"p" : Tp) and #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p") are different constructors, so they are not equal.

op_000005.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí tp_degree A ‚Č§ list_degree őď
op_000005.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí tp_degree A ‚Č§ list_degree őď
op_000005.statement_formalization_notes: The statement is already a valid Lean proposition in the repository's `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree` notation.

op_000005.proof_sketch: Assume the claimed degree bound holds for every derivable sequent. Instantiate it with the derivable sequent `[Tp.atom "p"] ‚áí Tp.rdiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.atom "p"))`, built from `Sequent.ax`, one use of `Sequent.ldiv_l`, and then `Sequent.rdiv_r`. The bound then specializes to `5 ‚Č§ 1`, which is impossible.
op_000005.proof_text:
intro h
let p : Tp := Tp.atom "p"
let bad : Tp := Tp.rdiv p (Tp.ldiv p p)
have hp : [p] ‚áí p := Sequent.ax
have hldiv : [p, Tp.ldiv p p] ‚áí p := by
  simpa [p] using
    (Sequent.ldiv_l (őĒ := [p]) (A := p) (őď := []) (B := p) (őõ := []) (C := p) hp hp)
have hseq : [p] ‚áí bad := by
  have hne : [p] ‚Č† [] := by simp
  simpa [bad, p] using
    (Sequent.rdiv_r (őď := [p]) (A := Tp.ldiv p p) (B := p) hne hldiv)
have hle : tp_degree bad ‚Č§ list_degree [p] := h hseq
simpa [bad, p, tp_degree, list_degree] using hle
op_000005.counterexample_text: Take `őď = [Tp.atom "p"]` and `A = Tp.rdiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.atom "p"))`. This sequent is derivable, but `tp_degree A = 5` while `list_degree őď = 1`, so the proposed inequality fails.

op_000009.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {s : String} {A : Tp}, ([# s] ‚áí A) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ őĒ : List Tp, őĒ ‚áí # s ‚ąß A = (# s ‚ßł foldl Tp.ldiv (# s) őĒ) ‚ą® A = (foldr Tp.rdiv (# s) őĒ)
op_000009.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {s : String} {A : Tp}, ([# s] ‚áí A) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ őĒ : List Tp, őĒ ‚áí # s ‚ąß (A = (# s ‚ßł List.foldl Tp.ldiv (# s) őĒ) ‚ą® A = List.foldr Tp.rdiv (# s) őĒ)
op_000009.statement_formalization_notes: Interpreted the existential clause as requiring both `őĒ ‚áí # s` and one of the two displayed equalities; used `List.foldl` and `List.foldr` explicitly for reliable parsing.

op_000009.proof_sketch: Instantiate the statement with `s := "x"` and `A := # "x"`. The premise `[ # "x" ] ‚áí # "x"` is `Sequent.ax`. If the claimed conclusion held, we would get some `őĒ` with `őĒ ‚áí # "x"`, hence `őĒ ‚Č† []` by `nonempty_premises`. After splitting on the disjunction and then on `őĒ`, the empty case contradicts `őĒ ‚Č† []`, while the nonempty case makes each candidate expression definitionally a `Tp.rdiv` term, so `simp` rules out equality with the atom `# "x"`.
op_000009.proof_text:
intro h
have hax : ([# "x"] : List Tp) ‚áí # "x" := Sequent.ax
rcases h (s := "x") (A := # "x") hax with ‚ü®őĒ, hőĒ, hEq | hEq‚ü©
¬∑ have hne : őĒ ‚Č† [] := nonempty_premises hőĒ
  cases őĒ with
  | nil => exact hne rfl
  | cons A őď =>
      simp at hEq
¬∑ have hne : őĒ ‚Č† [] := nonempty_premises hőĒ
  cases őĒ with
  | nil => exact hne rfl
  | cons A őď =>
      simp at hEq
op_000009.counterexample_text: Take `s := "x"` and `A := # "x"`. Then `[ # "x" ] ‚áí A` holds by `Sequent.ax`. But any witness `őĒ` in the conclusion must satisfy `őĒ ‚áí # "x"`, so `őĒ` is nonempty. For nonempty `őĒ`, both `# "x" ‚ßł List.foldl Tp.ldiv (# "x") őĒ` and `List.foldr Tp.rdiv (# "x") őĒ` are `Tp.rdiv` terms, hence cannot equal the atom `# "x"`. Thus the existential conclusion fails for this instance.

op_000010.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí atom_count A ‚Č§ atom_count_list őď
op_000010.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí atom_count A ‚Č§ atom_count_list őď
op_000010.statement_formalization_notes: The target names were not present in the provided context, so I added minimal recursive prelude definitions counting atomic leaves of a type and summing them over a context.

op_000010.proof_sketch: Assume the claimed atom-count bound for every derivable sequent. Instantiate it with őď = [#"p"] and A = #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"). Build the sequent [#"p", #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] ‚áí #"p" from two axioms using `Sequent.ldiv_l`, then derive [#"p"] ‚áí #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p") via `Sequent.rdiv_r`. The bound then specializes to 3 ‚Č§ 1, which `norm_num` refutes.
op_000010.prelude_code:
def atom_count : Tp ‚Üí Nat
  | Tp.atom _ => 1
  | Tp.ldiv A B => atom_count A + atom_count B
  | Tp.rdiv A B => atom_count A + atom_count B

def atom_count_list : List Tp ‚Üí Nat
  | [] => 0
  | A :: őď => atom_count A + atom_count_list őď
op_000010.proof_text:
intro h
let p : Tp := #"p"
let bad : Tp := p ‚ßł (p ‚ßĻ p)
have hp : [p] ‚áí p := Sequent.ax
have hldiv : [p, p ‚ßĻ p] ‚áí p := by
  simpa [p] using
    (Sequent.ldiv_l (őĒ := [p]) (A := p) (őď := []) (B := p) (őõ := []) (C := p) hp hp)
have hseq : [p] ‚áí bad := by
  have hne : [p] ‚Č† [] := by simp
  simpa [bad, p] using
    (Sequent.rdiv_r (őď := [p]) (A := p ‚ßĻ p) (B := p) hne hldiv)
have hle : atom_count bad ‚Č§ atom_count_list [p] := h hseq
norm_num [bad, p, atom_count, atom_count_list] at hle
op_000010.counterexample_text: Take őď = [#"p"] and A = (#"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p")). First, [#"p"] ‚áí #"p" by `Sequent.ax`. Using `Sequent.ldiv_l` with two copies of this axiom, we get [#"p", #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] ‚áí #"p"; then `Sequent.rdiv_r` yields [#"p"] ‚áí (#"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p")). For the leaf-count definitions above, `atom_count A = 3` and `atom_count_list őď = 1`, so the claimed inequality becomes `3 ‚Č§ 1`, impossible.

op_000011.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the given statement using the ProductFree `Tp`, `‚áí`, and `#` notation from the theory context.

op_000011.proof_sketch: Use `prove1_iff_sequent` to convert `[A] ‚áí # s` into `prove1 [A] (# s)`. Then split on `A`: simplifying `prove1` on a singleton context yields `name = s` in the atomic case, while both division cases simplify to `False`. For the reverse implication, rewrite by the equality and use `Sequent.ax`.
op_000011.proof_text:
intro A s
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  have hp : prove1 [A] (# s) := (prove1_iff_sequent).2 h
  cases A with
  | atom name =>
      have hname : name = s := by
        simpa [prove1, candidates, picks, splits] using hp
      simpa using congrArg Tp.atom hname
  | ldiv A B =>
      have : False := by
        simpa [prove1, candidates, picks, splits] using hp
      exact False.elim this
  | rdiv B A =>
      have : False := by
        simpa [prove1, candidates, picks, splits] using hp
      exact False.elim this
¬∑ rintro rfl
  exact Sequent.ax

op_000011.proof_sketch: Split on `A`. In the atomic case, use `atom_generation` on the singleton atomic context to obtain `[Tp.atom name] = [Tp.atom s]`, hence `Tp.atom name = Tp.atom s`. In the division cases, convert the sequent derivation to `prove1 [A] (# s)`, unfold `prove1` only at the outermost call, simplify `candidates/picks/splits` for a singleton context, and obtain an impossible premise `prove1 [] _`; turn that into a contradiction using `prove1_iff_sequent` and `nonempty_premises`.
op_000011.proof_text:
intro A s
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  have hp : prove1 [A] (# s) := (prove1_iff_sequent).2 h
  cases A with
  | atom name =>
      have hctx : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą [Tp.atom name], is_atom x := by
        intro x hx
        simp at hx
        rcases hx with rfl
        simp [is_atom]
      have hsingle : [Tp.atom name] = [Tp.atom s] := atom_generation hctx h
      simpa using List.singleton_inj.mp hsingle
  | ldiv A B =>
      unfold prove1 at hp
      simp [candidates, picks, splits] at hp
      have hnil : ¬¨ prove1 [] A := by
        intro hA
        have hs : ([] : List Tp) ‚áí A := (prove1_iff_sequent).1 hA
        exact (nonempty_premises hs) rfl
      exact False.elim (hnil hp.1)
  | rdiv B A =>
      unfold prove1 at hp
      simp [candidates, picks, splits] at hp
      have hnil : ¬¨ prove1 [] A := by
        intro hA
        have hs : ([] : List Tp) ‚áí A := (prove1_iff_sequent).1 hA
        exact (nonempty_premises hs) rfl
      exact False.elim (hnil hp.1)
¬∑ rintro rfl
  exact Sequent.ax

op_000013.statement_formalization_notes: Used the repository's `Tp`, `‚áí`, `‚ßĻ`, `‚ßł`, and `is_atom` notation directly; no extra prelude is needed.

op_000013.proof_sketch: Split the hypothesis, then case on `A`. The atomic case contradicts `¬¨ is_atom A`. The `ldiv` and `rdiv` cases are witnessed directly using `ldiv_invertible` and `rdiv_invertible`.
op_000013.proof_text:
intro s A h
rcases h with ‚ü®hA, hnot‚ü©
cases A with
| atom t =>
    exfalso
    exact hnot trivial
| ldiv B C =>
    left
    refine ‚ü®B, C, rfl, ?_‚ü©
    simpa using ldiv_invertible hA
| rdiv C B =>
    right
    refine ‚ü®B, C, rfl, ?_‚ü©
    simpa using rdiv_invertible hA

op_000006.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)
op_000006.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)
op_000006.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized exactly as stated, using the repository's open Lambek notation and Bool-to-Prop coercion for `proveAux`.

op_000006.proof_sketch: The forward direction is immediate: if `proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A` holds, then `proveAux_sound` gives `prove1 őď A`, and `prove1_sound` yields `(őď ‚áí A)`. For the converse, do strong induction on `n = list_degree őď + tp_degree A`, following the same case split as `prove1_complete`. If the goal type is `A' ‚ßĻ B` or `B ‚ßł A'`, a derivation of `őď ‚áí A` must end with `ldiv_r` or `rdiv_r`, and the recursive premise has measure exactly `n - 1`, so the induction hypothesis matches the recursive `proveAux` call after unfolding. If the conclusion is atomic, the only derivation cases are `ax`, `rdiv_l`, or `ldiv_l`; in the latter two cases `candidates_rdiv_mem` or `candidates_ldiv_mem` places the principal candidate in `candidates őď`, both premise sequents have strictly smaller measure, and `proveAux_mono_le` lifts those recursive proofs to the budget `n - 1` required by the unfolded atom branch. Hence `őď ‚áí A` implies `proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A`, establishing the equivalence.

op_000006.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)
op_000006.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)
op_000006.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly with the existing `Tp`, `proveAux`, `list_degree`, `tp_degree`, and `‚áí` notation from the ProductFree context.

op_000006.proof_sketch: For the forward direction, `proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A` implies `prove1 őď A` by `proveAux_sound`, and then `őď ‚áí A` by `prove1_sound`. For the converse, do strong induction on `n = list_degree őď + tp_degree A`; the base case is vacuous because any derivable sequent has nonempty context, so measure `0` cannot occur. If `A` is `A‚āĀ ‚ßĻ B` or `B ‚ßł A‚āĀ`, the derivation must end with `Sequent.ldiv_r` or `Sequent.rdiv_r`, and unfolding `proveAux (n+1)` reduces to the single premise with recursive budget `n`, whose measure is exactly `n`, so the induction hypothesis applies directly. If `A` is atomic, the derivation is either `ax`, `Sequent.rdiv_l`, or `Sequent.ldiv_l`; in the axiom case the head equality test is true, and in the left-rule cases the needed candidate is present by `candidates_rdiv_mem` or `candidates_ldiv_mem`. The premise sequents in those candidate branches have strictly smaller measure than the conclusion, so the induction hypothesis gives `proveAux` at their own measures, and `proveAux_mono_le` raises each to the common recursive budget required by the unfolded atom branch. Hence `őď ‚áí A` implies `proveAux (list_degree őď + tp_degree A) őď A`, giving the stated equivalence.

op_000018.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {s : String} {A : Tp}, ([# s] ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ A = # s ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (B ‚ßĻ C) ‚ąß ([B, # s] ‚áí C)) ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (C ‚ßł B) ‚ąß ([# s, B] ‚áí C))
op_000018.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {s : String} {A : Tp}, ([# s] ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ A = # s ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (B ‚ßĻ C) ‚ąß ([B, # s] ‚áí C)) ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (C ‚ßł B) ‚ąß ([# s, B] ‚áí C))
op_000018.statement_formalization_notes: Used the statement essentially verbatim in the repository's ProductFree notation; the right-hand side is grouped as a three-way disjunction.

op_000018.proof_sketch: Split on the succedent `A`. If `A` is atomic, `atom_generation` on the singleton atomic context `[ # s ]` forces that atom to be `# s`. If `A` is a left or right division, use `ldiv_invertible` or `rdiv_invertible` for the forward implication, and `Sequent.ldiv_r` or `Sequent.rdiv_r` for the converse.
op_000018.proof_text:
intro s A
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  cases A with
  | atom name =>
      left
      have hctx : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą [Tp.atom s], is_atom x := by
        intro x hx
        simp at hx
        rcases hx with rfl
        simp [is_atom]
      have hsingle : [Tp.atom s] = [Tp.atom name] := atom_generation hctx h
      simpa using (List.singleton_inj.mp hsingle).symm
  | ldiv B C =>
      right
      left
      refine ‚ü®B, C, rfl, ?_‚ü©
      simpa using ldiv_invertible h
  | rdiv C B =>
      right
      right
      refine ‚ü®B, C, rfl, ?_‚ü©
      simpa using rdiv_invertible h
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with rfl | h | h
  ¬∑ exact Sequent.ax
  ¬∑ rcases h with ‚ü®B, C, rfl, hBC‚ü©
    have hne : ([# s] : List Tp) ‚Č† [] := by simp
    simpa using Sequent.ldiv_r hne hBC
  ¬∑ rcases h with ‚ü®B, C, rfl, hCB‚ü©
    have hne : ([# s] : List Tp) ‚Č† [] := by simp
    simpa using Sequent.rdiv_r hne hCB

op_000022.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {A C : Tp}, ([A] ‚áí C) ‚ÜĒ A = C ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B D : Tp, C = (B ‚ßĻ D) ‚ąß ([B, A] ‚áí D)) ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B D : Tp, C = (D ‚ßł B) ‚ąß ([A, B] ‚áí D))
op_000022.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {A C : Tp}, ([A] ‚áí C) ‚ÜĒ A = C ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B D : Tp, C = (B ‚ßĻ D) ‚ąß ([B, A] ‚áí D)) ‚ą® (‚ąÉ B D : Tp, C = (D ‚ßł B) ‚ąß ([A, B] ‚áí D))
op_000022.statement_formalization_notes: Direct notation-first formalization of the given singleton-context iff classification in `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree`.

op_000022.proof_sketch: Split on the succedent C. If C is atomic, use the verified singleton-atomic iff to get A = C. If C is a left or right division, use ldiv_invertible or rdiv_invertible to obtain the required premise. The converse direction is by ax, ldiv_r, and rdiv_r.
op_000022.proof_text:
intro A C
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  cases C with
  | atom s =>
      left
      exact (thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011 (A := A) (s := s)).mp h
  | ldiv B D =>
      right
      left
      refine ‚ü®B, D, rfl, ?_‚ü©
      simpa using ldiv_invertible h
  | rdiv D B =>
      right
      right
      refine ‚ü®B, D, rfl, ?_‚ü©
      simpa using rdiv_invertible h
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with rfl | h | h
  ¬∑ exact Sequent.ax
  ¬∑ rcases h with ‚ü®B, D, rfl, hBD‚ü©
    have hne : ([A] : List Tp) ‚Č† [] := by simp
    simpa using Sequent.ldiv_r hne hBD
  ¬∑ rcases h with ‚ü®B, D, rfl, hDB‚ü©
    have hne : ([A] : List Tp) ‚Č† [] := by simp
    simpa using Sequent.rdiv_r hne hDB

op_000023.source_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, is_atom x) ‚Üí ((őď ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ (‚ąÉ s : String, A = # s ‚ąß őď = [# s]) ‚ą® (őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß ‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (B ‚ßĻ C) ‚ąß ([B] ++ őď ‚áí C)) ‚ą® (őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß ‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (C ‚ßł B) ‚ąß (őď ++ [B] ‚áí C)))
op_000023.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, is_atom x) ‚Üí ((őď ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ (‚ąÉ s : String, A = # s ‚ąß őď = [# s]) ‚ą® (őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß ‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (B ‚ßĻ C) ‚ąß ([B] ++ őď ‚áí C)) ‚ą® (őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß ‚ąÉ B C : Tp, A = (C ‚ßł B) ‚ąß (őď ++ [B] ‚áí C)))
op_000023.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a reliable Lean proposition in the repository's notation, so I formalized it directly.

op_000023.proof_sketch: Split on `A`. In the atom case, `atom_generation` forces `őď = [# s]`. In the division cases, use `nonempty_premises` and `ldiv_invertible`/`rdiv_invertible` forward, and reconstruct with `Sequent.ldiv_r`/`Sequent.rdiv_r` backward.
op_000023.proof_text:
intro őď A hőď
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  cases A with
  | atom s =>
      left
      exact ‚ü®s, rfl, atom_generation hőď h‚ü©
  | ldiv B C =>

      left

op_000024.proof_sketch: Prove the forward direction by induction on a derivation of `[A] ‚áí C`. The `ax`, `ldiv_r`, and `rdiv_r` cases map directly to the corresponding constructors of `LeftRuleFreeDerivation`. In the `ldiv_l` and `rdiv_l` cases, the conclusion context has the form `őď ++ őĒ ++ [..] ++ őõ` or `őď ++ [..] ++ őĒ ++ őõ`, yet it is equal to the singleton list `[A]`; therefore the premise context `őĒ` must be `[]`. But `d_arg : őĒ ‚áí _` then contradicts `nonempty_premises d_arg`, so no left rule can produce a singleton conclusion. The reverse direction is immediate by induction on `LeftRuleFreeDerivation`, since its constructors are exactly the corresponding `Sequent.ax`, `Sequent.ldiv_r`, and `Sequent.rdiv_r` rules.

op_000032.proof_sketch: Proceed by induction on the derivation of `őď ‚áí A`. The `ldiv_r` and `rdiv_r` cases match the recursive clauses of `support_ok` directly, with the required nonemptiness supplied by the rule hypotheses (equivalently by `nonempty_premises`). In the atomic singleton base case, `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011` shows that from `[A] ‚áí # s` we get `A = # s`, so `support_ok [A] (# s)` holds by choosing the unique hypothesis as witness. The only nontrivial bridge is a pair of context-replacement lemmas for left rules: from `support_ok (őď ++ [B] ++ őõ) C`, derive `support_ok (őď ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ őõ) C`, and similarly for `őď ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ`; for atomic `C`, either keep the old witness if it is elsewhere, or replace witness `B` by the compound formula using the recursive definition of `occurs_atom`, and the division cases follow by unfolding `support_ok`. With those helper lemmas, the `ldiv_l` and `rdiv_l` cases close from the induction hypothesis on the main premise.

lemma support_ok_replace
    {őď L R őõ : List Tp} {B B' C : Tp}

    (h : support_ok (őď ++ [B] ++ őõ) C) :
    support_ok (őď ++ L ++ [B'] ++ R ++ őõ) C := by
  induction C generalizing őď L R őõ with
  | atom s =>
      rcases h with ‚ü®X, hX, hXocc‚ü©
      simp at hX
      rcases hX with hX | rfl | hX
      ¬∑ refine ‚ü®X, ?_, hXocc‚ü©
        simp [hX]
      ¬∑ refine ‚ü®B', ?_, hocc s hXocc‚ü©
        simp
      ¬∑ refine ‚ü®X, ?_, hXocc‚ü©
        simp [hX]
  | ldiv D C ihD ihC =>
      rcases h with ‚ü®_, hC‚ü©
      constructor
      ¬∑ simp
      ¬∑ simpa [List.append_assoc] using
          ihC (őď := [D] ++ őď) (L := L) (R := R) (őõ := őõ) (by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hC)
  | rdiv C D ihC ihD =>
      rcases h with ‚ü®_, hC‚ü©
      constructor
      ¬∑ simp
      ¬∑ simpa [List.append_assoc] using
          ihC (őď := őď) (L := L) (R := R) (őõ := őõ ++ [D]) (by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hC)

      constructor

      ¬∑ simpa using
          (support_ok_replace
            (őď := []) (L := [B]) (R := []) (őõ := [])
            (B := C) (B' := B ‚ßĻ C) (C := C)
            (fun s hs => by simp [occurs_atom, hs]) ihC)
  | rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
      constructor
      ¬∑ simp
      ¬∑ simpa using
          (support_ok_replace
            (őď := []) (L := []) (R := [B]) (őõ := [])

op_000027.source_statement: Introduce an inductive proof predicate for sequents from atom-only contexts using only `ax`, `ldiv_r`, and `rdiv_r`, and prove: for all `{őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}`, `(‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, is_atom x) ‚Üí ((őď ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ AtomOnlyNormalDerivation őď A)`.
op_000027.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, is_atom x) ‚Üí ((őď ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ AtomOnlyNormalDerivation őď A)
op_000027.statement_formalization_notes: The prelude defines the intended left-rule-free derivation predicate with exactly the constructors `ax`, `ldiv_r`, and `rdiv_r`; the atom-only restriction is kept as the theorem hypothesis on the initial context.

op_000027.proof_sketch: Instantiate the statement at őď = [#"p"] and A = #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"). The context hypothesis is immediate. The sequent [#"p"] ‚áí #"p" ‚ßł (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p") is derivable in the full calculus: first derive [#"p", #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] ‚áí #"p" by `ldiv_l` from two copies of `ax`, then apply `rdiv_r`. If the claimed equivalence held, this would give an `AtomOnlyNormalDerivation` of the same sequent. But since the succedent is a right division, such a derivation must end with `rdiv_r`, leaving a premise `AtomOnlyNormalDerivation [#"p", #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] (#"p")`. Case analysis on that premise shows only `ax` could match the atomic succedent, and `ax` would force a singleton context, contradiction.
op_000027.proof_text:
intro h
let p : Tp := # "p"
let A : Tp := p ‚ßł (p ‚ßĻ p)
have hctx : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą [p], is_atom x := by
  intro x hx
  rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hx with rfl
  simp [p, is_atom]
have hiff : ([p] ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ AtomOnlyNormalDerivation [p] A :=
  h (őď := [p]) (A := A) hctx
have hseq : [p] ‚áí A := by
  apply Sequent.rdiv_r
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ simpa [A, p] using
      (Sequent.ldiv_l
        (őĒ := [p]) (A := p) (őď := []) (B := p) (őõ := []) (C := p)
        (Sequent.ax : [p] ‚áí p)
        (Sequent.ax : [p] ‚áí p))
have hnorm : AtomOnlyNormalDerivation [p] A := hiff.mp hseq
cases hnorm with
| rdiv_r _ hinner =>
    cases hinner with
    | ax =>
        simp [A, p] at *
op_000027.counterexample_text: Take őď = [# "p"] and A = # "p" ‚ßł (# "p" ‚ßĻ # "p"). Then őď is atom-only, and őď ‚áí A holds in the full sequent calculus by one `ldiv_l` followed by `rdiv_r`. However, `AtomOnlyNormalDerivation őď A` is impossible: any such derivation must end with `rdiv_r`, so it would require an atomic-succedent derivation on context [# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "p"], and case analysis shows that only `ax` could produce an atomic succedent, forcing a singleton context. Therefore the claimed equivalence fails.

op_000031.source_statement: Define what it means for a type and a context to lie entirely in the left-only fragment or entirely in the right-only fragment, and prove: if every formula in `őď` lies in the left-only fragment, then `(őď ‚áí # s)` in the full product-free calculus iff the corresponding translated context derives `# s` in the left-only calculus; likewise for the right-only fragment.
op_000031.formalized_statement:
(‚ąÄ {őď : List PFTp} {s : String},
  IsLeftOnlyCtx őď ‚Üí
    ((őď ‚áí # s) ‚ÜĒ
      match leftCtx? őď with
      | some őďL => Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent őďL (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.atom s)
      | none => False)) ‚ąß
(‚ąÄ {őď : List PFTp} {s : String},
  IsRightOnlyCtx őď ‚Üí
    ((őď ‚áí # s) ‚ÜĒ
      match rightCtx? őď with
      | some őďR => Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Sequent őďR (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Tp.atom s)
      | none => False))
op_000031.statement_formalization_notes: Used recursive one-sided fragment predicates and option-valued translations so the translated context is explicit and deterministic.

op_000031.proof_sketch: Prove two bridge lemmas by structural induction: `IsLeftOnlyTp A` gives some `A_L` with `leftTp? A = some A_L` and `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.toProductFree A_L = A`, and similarly on lists `IsLeftOnlyCtx őď` gives some `őď_L` with `leftCtx? őď = some őď_L` and `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.ctxToProductFree őď_L = őď`. Then unfold `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent`; by definition it is just the full sequent on `Left.ctxToProductFree őď_L`, so rewriting by that equality yields `(őď ‚áí # s) ‚ÜĒ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent őď_L (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.atom s)`. Because `IsLeftOnlyCtx őď` forces the translation to succeed, the `match leftCtx? őď` expression cannot be `none`, so the stated left conjunct follows. The right conjunct is the same argument with `rightTp?`, `rightCtx?`, and the definition of `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Sequent`.

op_000030.source_statement: Introduce an inductive predicate `AtomicCandidateTree : List Tp ‚Üí String ‚Üí Prop` with a base case for `[# s]` and recursive cases indexed by `c ‚ąą candidates őď`, requiring exactly the two recursive premises attached to that candidate, and prove: `AtomicCandidateTree őď s ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí # s)`.
op_000030.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (s : String), AtomicCandidateTree őď s ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí # s)
op_000030.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired the declaration by splitting the recursive candidate step into separate `rdiv` and `ldiv` constructors. This avoids invalid nested recursive occurrences through `And` or `match` while preserving the intended two attached premises for each candidate.

op_000030.proof_sketch: Forward direction is induction on `AtomicCandidateTree`. For completeness, use strong induction on `list_degree őď`; decompose an atomic sequent with `thm_atom_goal_candidate_cases_000007`, then recurse on the strictly smaller recursive context coming from the selected candidate.
op_000030.prelude_code:
inductive AtomicCandidateTree : List Tp ‚Üí String ‚Üí Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      AtomicCandidateTree [# s] s
  | step_rdiv (őď L őĒ őõ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A)
      (hrec : AtomicCandidateTree (L ++ [B] ++ őõ) s) :
      AtomicCandidateTree őď s
  | step_ldiv (őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A)
      (hrec : AtomicCandidateTree (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R) s) :
      AtomicCandidateTree őď s
op_000030.proof_text:
intro őď s
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  induction h with
  | base s =>
      exact Sequent.ax
  | step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hőď : őď = L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ) hc)
      subst őď
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.rdiv_l (őď := L) (őĒ := őĒ) (A := A) (B := B) (őõ := őõ) (C := # s) harg ih)
  | step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hőď : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
      subst őď
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.ldiv_l (őď := őď‚āĀ) (őĒ := őĒ) (A := A) (B := B) (őõ := R) (C := # s) harg ih)
¬∑ have hcomplete : ‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (s : String), (őď ‚áí # s) ‚Üí AtomicCandidateTree őď s := by
    intro őď s
    let n := list_degree őď
    have hmain : ‚ąÄ n (őď : List Tp) (s : String), list_degree őď = n ‚Üí (őď ‚áí # s) ‚Üí AtomicCandidateTree őď s := by
      intro n
      refine Nat.strong_induction_on n ?_
      intro n ih őď s hdeg h
      rcases thm_atom_goal_candidate_cases_000007 őď s h with rfl | ‚ü®c, hc, hcases‚ü©
      ¬∑ simpa using AtomicCandidateTree.base s
      ¬∑ cases c with
        | rdiv L B A őĒ őõ =>
            rcases hcases with ‚ü®harg, hrec‚ü©
            have hlt : list_degree (L ++ [B] ++ őõ) < n := by
              have hőď : őď = L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ := by
                symm
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ) hc)
              rw [hőď] at hdeg
              rw [‚Üź hdeg]
              simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
              omega
            exact
              AtomicCandidateTree.step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg
                (ih (list_degree (L ++ [B] ++ őõ)) hlt (L ++ [B] ++ őõ) s rfl hrec)
        | ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R =>
            rcases hcases with ‚ü®harg, hrec‚ü©
            have hlt : list_degree (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R) < n := by
              have hőď : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R := by
                symm
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
              rw [hőď] at hdeg
              rw [‚Üź hdeg]
              simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
              omega
            exact
              AtomicCandidateTree.step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg
                (ih (list_degree (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R)) hlt (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R) s rfl hrec)
    exact hmain n őď s rfl
  exact hcomplete őď s

op_000036.source_statement: Define the least context-succedent closure generated by singleton self-support and the three sequent rules, and prove that it coincides with support_ok.
op_000036.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ support_ok őď A
op_000036.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the least closure as an inductive context-succedent predicate with singleton self-support, the two right-introduction propagation rules, and the occurrence-preserving replacement rule that subsumes the left rules.

op_000036.proof_sketch: Induct on `SupportClosure` for the forward implication, using `support_ok_self_singleton` and `support_ok_replace`. For the reverse implication, induct on the structure of `A`; the atomic case splits the witness membership in `őď` as `L ++ [B] ++ R` and rebuilds the closure from `SupportClosure.self (# s)` via `replace`, while the division cases follow directly from the recursive clauses of `support_ok`.
op_000036.prelude_code:
inductive SupportClosure : List Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Prop where
  | self (A : Tp) :
      SupportClosure [A] A
  | ldiv_r (őď : List Tp) (B C : Tp)
      (hőď : őď ‚Č† [])
      (h : SupportClosure ([B] ++ őď) C) :
      SupportClosure őď (B ‚ßĻ C)
  | rdiv_r (őď : List Tp) (C B : Tp)
      (hőď : őď ‚Č† [])
      (h : SupportClosure (őď ++ [B]) C) :
      SupportClosure őď (C ‚ßł B)
  | replace (őď L R őõ : List Tp) (B B' C : Tp)
      (hocc : ‚ąÄ s : String, occurs_atom s B ‚Üí occurs_atom s B')
      (h : SupportClosure (őď ++ [B] ++ őõ) C) :
      SupportClosure (őď ++ L ++ [B'] ++ R ++ őõ) C
op_000036.proof_text:
intro őď A
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  induction h with
  | self A =>
      simpa using support_ok_self_singleton A
  | ldiv_r őď B C hőď h ih =>
      exact ‚ü®hőď, by simpa using ih‚ü©
  | rdiv_r őď C B hőď h ih =>
      exact ‚ü®hőď, by simpa using ih‚ü©
  | replace őď L R őõ B B' C hocc h ih =>
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using support_ok_replace hocc ih
¬∑ revert őď
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      intro őď h
      rcases h with ‚ü®B, hB, hocc‚ü©
      have hsplit : ‚ąÉ L R, őď = L ++ [B] ++ R := by
        induction őď with
        | nil =>
            cases hB
        | cons X őď ih =>
            simp at hB
            rcases hB with rfl | hB
            ¬∑ exact ‚ü®[], őď, by simp‚ü©
            ¬∑ rcases ih hB with ‚ü®L, R, hLR‚ü©
              exact ‚ü®X :: L, R, by simp [hLR]‚ü©
      rcases hsplit with ‚ü®L, R, rfl‚ü©
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (SupportClosure.replace
          (őď := []) (L := L) (R := R) (őõ := [])
          (B := # s) (B' := B) (C := # s)
          (fun t ht => by
            simp [occurs_atom] at ht
            simpa [ht] using hocc)
          (SupportClosure.self (# s)))
  | ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
      intro őď h
      rcases h with ‚ü®hőď, hC‚ü©
      exact SupportClosure.ldiv_r őď B C hőď (ihC ([B] ++ őď) hC)
  | rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
      intro őď h
      rcases h with ‚ü®hőď, hC‚ü©
      exact SupportClosure.rdiv_r őď C B hőď (ihC (őď ++ [B]) hC)

op_000038.source_statement: Define an inductive predicate `CandidateTree : List Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Prop` by combining the new atomic-case constructors with one constructor for each right-introduction rule, and prove `CandidateTree őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)` for all `őď` and `A`.
op_000038.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp), CandidateTree őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)
op_000038.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized `CandidateTree` by reusing the atomic constructors from `AtomicCandidateTree` and adding recursive constructors mirroring `ldiv_r` and `rdiv_r`.

op_000038.proof_sketch: Forward by induction on `CandidateTree`. Reverse by converting atomic sequents through `thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030`, then inducting on the succedent and using `ldiv_invertible`/`rdiv_invertible` for division cases.
op_000038.prelude_code:
inductive CandidateTree : List Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      CandidateTree [# s] (# s)
  | step_rdiv (őď L őĒ őõ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A)
      (hrec : CandidateTree (L ++ [B] ++ őõ) (# s)) :
      CandidateTree őď (# s)
  | step_ldiv (őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A)
      (hrec : CandidateTree (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R) (# s)) :
      CandidateTree őď (# s)
  | ldiv_r (őď : List Tp) (A B : Tp)
      (hne : őď ‚Č† [])
      (hrec : CandidateTree ([A] ++ őď) B) :
      CandidateTree őď (A ‚ßĻ B)
  | rdiv_r (őď : List Tp) (A B : Tp)
      (hne : őď ‚Č† [])
      (hrec : CandidateTree (őď ++ [A]) B) :
      CandidateTree őď (B ‚ßł A)
op_000038.proof_text:
intro őď A
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  induction h with
  | base s =>
      exact Sequent.ax
  | step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hőď : őď = L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ) hc)
      subst őď
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.rdiv_l (őď := L) (őĒ := őĒ) (A := A) (B := B) (őõ := őõ) (C := # s) harg ih)
  | step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hőď : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
      subst őď
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.ldiv_l (őď := őď‚āĀ) (őĒ := őĒ) (A := A) (B := B) (őõ := R) (C := # s) harg ih)
  | ldiv_r őď A B hne hrec ih =>
      exact Sequent.ldiv_r hne ih
  | rdiv_r őď A B hne hrec ih =>
      exact Sequent.rdiv_r hne ih
¬∑ have hatom : ‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (s : String), AtomicCandidateTree őď s ‚Üí CandidateTree őď (# s) := by
    intro őď s h
    induction h with
    | base s =>
        simpa using CandidateTree.base s
    | step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
        exact CandidateTree.step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg ih
    | step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
        exact CandidateTree.step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg ih
  have hcomplete : ‚ąÄ (A : Tp) (őď : List Tp), (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí CandidateTree őď A := by
    intro A
    induction A with
    | atom s =>
        intro őď h
        exact hatom őď s ((thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 őď s).2 h)
    | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
        intro őď h
        have hne : őď ‚Č† [] := nonempty_premises h
        have hinner : [A] ++ őď ‚áí B := ldiv_invertible h
        exact CandidateTree.ldiv_r őď A B hne (ihB ([A] ++ őď) hinner)
    | rdiv B A ihB ihA =>
        intro őď h
        have hne : őď ‚Č† [] := nonempty_premises h
        have hinner : őď ++ [A] ‚áí B := rdiv_invertible h
        exact CandidateTree.rdiv_r őď A B hne (ihB (őď ++ [A]) hinner)
  exact hcomplete A őď

op_000041.source_statement: Prove exact completeness of the support closure by showing `‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)`.
op_000041.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A)
op_000041.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the stated exact-completeness theorem, using the repository's existing `Tp`, `SupportClosure`, and `‚áí` notation.

op_000041.proof_sketch: Refute the universal equivalence by specializing to őď = [#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] and A = #"p". The `SupportClosure` side holds by starting from `SupportClosure.self (#"p")` and applying `SupportClosure.replace` to swap `#"p"` for `#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"`, since every occurrence of `"p"` in `#"p"` also occurs in `#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"`. If the claimed equivalence held, we would get `[#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] ‚áí #"p"`. Then `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011` forces `#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p" = #"p"`, contradiction.
op_000041.proof_text:
intro h
let bad : Tp := #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"
have hsc : SupportClosure [bad] (#"p") := by
  simpa [bad] using
    (SupportClosure.replace
      (őď := []) (L := []) (R := []) (őõ := [])
      (B := #"p") (B' := bad) (C := #"p")
      (fun s hs => by
        simp [occurs_atom, bad] at hs ‚äĘ
        exact hs)
      (SupportClosure.self (#"p")))
have hseq : [bad] ‚áí #"p" := (h [bad] (#"p")).mp hsc
have heq : bad = #"p" :=
  (thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011 (A := bad) (s := "p")).mp hseq
simp [bad] at heq
op_000041.counterexample_text: Take őď = [# "p" ‚ßĻ # "p"] and A = # "p". Then `SupportClosure őď A` holds via `SupportClosure.self (# "p")` followed by `SupportClosure.replace`, because any occurrence of `"p"` in `# "p"` also occurs in `# "p" ‚ßĻ # "p"`. But `őď ‚áí A` is impossible: by `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011`, a derivation `([# "p" ‚ßĻ # "p"] ‚áí # "p")` would imply `# "p" ‚ßĻ # "p" = # "p"`, contradiction.

op_000044.source_statement: Define what it means for an arbitrary type to lie entirely in the left-only fragment or entirely in the right-only fragment, and prove: if every formula in őď and A is left-only, then őď ‚áí A in the full product-free calculus iff the corresponding translated sequent is derivable in the left-only calculus; dually, if every formula in őď and A is right-only, then full derivability is equivalent to derivability in the right-only calculus.
op_000044.formalized_statement:
(‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isLeftOnly x) ‚Üí
    isLeftOnly A ‚Üí
    (őď ‚áí A ‚ÜĒ leftTranslatedSequent őď A))
  ‚ąß
  (‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isRightOnly x) ‚Üí
    isRightOnly A ‚Üí
    (őď ‚áí A ‚ÜĒ rightTranslatedSequent őď A))
op_000044.statement_formalization_notes: Minimal repair of the previous attempt: removed the redundant `Tp.rdiv` match branch and factored the translated sequents into helper definitions to keep the proposition parser-stable.

op_000044.proof_sketch: Prove the conjunction by handling the left and right halves symmetrically. The smallest missing bridge step is an induction lemma saying `isLeftOnly A` gives a witness `AL` with `toLeftTp? A = some AL` and `AL.toProductFree = A`, plus the list version saying `(forall x in őď, isLeftOnly x)` gives `őďL` with `toLeftCtx? őď = some őďL` and `Left.ctxToProductFree őďL = őď`. After choosing these witnesses, `leftTranslatedSequent őď A` reduces by unfolding to `Left.Sequent őďL AL`, and by the definition of `Left.Sequent` this is exactly the full sequent `őď ‚áí A` after rewriting by the recovered equalities. The right-only part is identical, using the corresponding witness lemmas for `toRightTp?`, `toRightCtx?`, and the definition of `Right.Sequent`.

op_000042.source_statement: Prove full one-sided conservativity: if every formula in `őď` and `A` lies in the left-only fragment, then `őď ‚áí A` in the full product-free calculus iff the translated sequent is derivable in the left-only calculus; dually for the right-only fragment.
op_000042.formalized_statement:
(‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isLeftOnly x) ‚Üí
    isLeftOnly A ‚Üí
    (őď ‚áí A ‚ÜĒ leftTranslatedSequent őď A))
  ‚ąß
  (‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),

lemma exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly {A : Tp} (hA : isLeftOnly A) :
    ‚ąÉ AL, toLeftTp? A = some AL ‚ąß Left.Tp.toProductFree AL = A := by
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      refine ‚ü®Left.Tp.atom s, ?_, ?_‚ü© <;> rfl
  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
      rcases hA with ‚ü®hA, hB‚ü©
      rcases ihA hA with ‚ü®AL, hAL, hAL_pf‚ü©
      rcases ihB hB with ‚ü®BL, hBL, hBL_pf‚ü©
      refine ‚ü®Left.Tp.ldiv AL BL, ?_, ?_‚ü©
      ¬∑ simp [toLeftTp?, hAL, hBL]
      ¬∑ simp [Left.Tp.toProductFree, hAL_pf, hBL_pf]
  | rdiv B A =>
      cases hA

lemma exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly {A : Tp} (hA : isRightOnly A) :
    ‚ąÉ AR, toRightTp? A = some AR ‚ąß Right.Tp.toProductFree AR = A := by
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      refine ‚ü®Right.Tp.atom s, ?_, ?_‚ü© <;> rfl
  | ldiv A B =>
      cases hA
  | rdiv B A ihB ihA =>
      rcases hA with ‚ü®hB, hA‚ü©
      rcases ihB hB with ‚ü®BR, hBR, hBR_pf‚ü©
      rcases ihA hA with ‚ü®AR, hAR, hAR_pf‚ü©
      refine ‚ü®Right.Tp.rdiv BR AR, ?_, ?_‚ü©
      ¬∑ simp [toRightTp?, hBR, hAR]
      ¬∑ simp [Right.Tp.toProductFree, hBR_pf, hAR_pf]

lemma exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly {őď : List Tp}
    (hőď : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isLeftOnly x) :
    ‚ąÉ őďL, toLeftCtx? őď = some őďL ‚ąß Left.ctxToProductFree őďL = őď := by
  induction őď with
  | nil =>
      refine ‚ü®[], ?_, ?_‚ü© <;> rfl
  | cons x xs ih =>
      have hx : isLeftOnly x := hőď x (by simp)
      have hxs : ‚ąÄ y ‚ąą xs, isLeftOnly y := by
        intro y hy
        exact hőď y (by simp [hy])
      rcases exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly hx with ‚ü®xL, hxL, hx_pf‚ü©
      rcases ih hxs with ‚ü®xsL, hxsL, hxs_pf‚ü©
      refine ‚ü®xL :: xsL, ?_, ?_‚ü©
      ¬∑ simpa [toLeftCtx?, hxL] using
          congrArg (fun t => t.bind fun ys => some (xL :: ys)) hxsL
      ¬∑ simp [Left.ctxToProductFree, hx_pf]
        simpa [Left.ctxToProductFree] using hxs_pf

lemma exists_right_ctx_of_allRightOnly {őď : List Tp}
    (hőď : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isRightOnly x) :
    ‚ąÉ őďR, toRightCtx? őď = some őďR ‚ąß Right.ctxToProductFree őďR = őď := by

  | nil =>

      have hxs : ‚ąÄ y ‚ąą xs, isRightOnly y := by
        intro y hy
        exact hőď y (by simp [hy])
      rcases exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly hx with ‚ü®xR, hxR, hx_pf‚ü©
      rcases ih hxs with ‚ü®xsR, hxsR, hxs_pf‚ü©
      refine ‚ü®xR :: xsR, ?_, ?_‚ü©
      ¬∑ simpa [toRightCtx?, hxR] using
          congrArg (fun t => t.bind fun ys => some (xR :: ys)) hxsR
      ¬∑ simp [Right.ctxToProductFree, hx_pf]
        simpa [Right.ctxToProductFree] using hxs_pf

Then it proves by structural induction that a left-only or right-only formula translates successfully and round-trips back to the original full formula, and lifts this to contexts by list induction. def isLeftOnly : Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | Tp.atom _ => True
  | Tp.ldiv A B => isLeftOnly A ‚ąß isLeftOnly B
  | Tp.rdiv _ _ => False

def isRightOnly : Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | Tp.atom _ => True
  | Tp.ldiv _ _ => False
  | Tp.rdiv B A => isRightOnly B ‚ąß isRightOnly A

def toLeftTp? : Tp ‚Üí Option Left.Tp
  | Tp.atom s => some (Left.Tp.atom s)
  | Tp.ldiv A B => do
      let A' ‚Üź toLeftTp? A
      let B' ‚Üź toLeftTp? B
      pure (Left.Tp.ldiv A' B')
  | Tp.rdiv _ _ => none

def toRightTp? : Tp ‚Üí Option Right.Tp
  | Tp.atom s => some (Right.Tp.atom s)
  | Tp.ldiv _ _ => none
  | Tp.rdiv B A => do
      let B' ‚Üź toRightTp? B
      let A' ‚Üź toRightTp? A
      pure (Right.Tp.rdiv B' A')

def toLeftCtx? (őď : List Tp) : Option (List Left.Tp) :=
  List.mapM toLeftTp? őď

def toRightCtx? (őď : List Tp) : Option (List Right.Tp) :=
  List.mapM toRightTp? őď

def leftTranslatedSequent (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match (toLeftCtx? őď, toLeftTp? A) with
  | (some őďL, some AL) => Left.Sequent őďL AL
  | _ => False

def rightTranslatedSequent (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match (toRightCtx? őď, toRightTp? A) with
  | (some őďR, some AR) => Right.Sequent őďR AR
  | _ => False

      ¬∑ simp [toRightTp?, hBR, hAR]
      ¬∑ simp [Right.Tp.toProductFree, hBR_pf, hAR_pf]

op_000046.source_statement: Construct the finite state graph generated by all subformula-bounded candidate-tree decompositions of a fixed sequent, and prove that őď ‚áí A holds exactly when the root state (őď, A) is accepted by that graph.
op_000046.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  ‚ąÉ G : Finset CandidateGraphState,
    (őď, A) ‚ąą G ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ q ‚ąą G, RootBoundedState őď A q) ‚ąß
    (CandidateGraphAccepts (FiniteCandidateGraphStep őď A G) (őď, A) ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A))
op_000046.statement_formalization_notes: I encoded the graph as a finite state set together with a branching transition relation on lists of successor states, since candidate-tree decomposition is not unary. Subformula-boundedness is captured by RootBoundedState using both root-subformula containment and the root degree bound.

op_000046.proof_sketch: A plausible Lean route is to prove soundness of `CandidateGraphAccepts` by induction on `CandidateGraphAcceptsWithin`, then prove a stronger root-parameterized completeness statement by induction on a derivation, using helper lemmas that each decomposition step preserves `RootBoundedState` and that acceptance is monotone under enlarging `G`. From that one could choose the recursively collected finite graph in the derivable case and a singleton root graph in the non-derivable case. In the current repo, however, only `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038` is available; the needed boundedness, finitary-support, and monotonicity lemmas are all absent, so I do not have a defensible short Lean formalization for this theorem.

op_000046.source_statement: Construct the finite state graph generated by all subformula-bounded candidate-tree decompositions of a fixed sequent, and prove that őď ‚áí A holds exactly when the root state (őď, A) is accepted by that graph.
op_000046.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  ‚ąÉ G : Finset CandidateGraphState,
    (őď, A) ‚ąą G ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ q ‚ąą G, RootBoundedState őď A q) ‚ąß
    (CandidateGraphAccepts (FiniteCandidateGraphStep őď A G) (őď, A) ‚ÜĒ (őď ‚áí A))
op_000046.statement_formalization_notes: Interpreted the graph as a finite set of sequent states with branching decomposition transitions, since candidate-tree decomposition is not unary; root-boundedness uses root-subformula containment plus a root degree bound.

op_000046.proof_sketch: Start from `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038`: if `őď ‚áí A`, choose a witness `h : CandidateTree őď A`. Let `G` be the finite `Finset` of node labels `(őĒ, C)` occurring in `h`; finiteness is by structural recursion on the tree. The only real bridge is to show every node state is `RootBoundedState őď A`, and that follows by induction on node occurrence: `candidates_list_degree` identifies the principal context formula at each atomic expansion, the degree bound is preserved on children, and transitivity of `CandidateSubformula` lifts every child formula back to the root sequent. Then a second induction on `h` shows the root is accepted by `FiniteCandidateGraphStep őď A G`, since each constructor of `CandidateTree` matches one clause of `CandidateGraphStepCore` and its children stay inside `G`. For the converse, prove soundness by induction on `CandidateGraphAcceptsWithin`: each accepted step reconstructs the corresponding sequent rule, so acceptance of `(őď, A)` implies `őď ‚áí A`. In the non-derivable case, take `G = {(őď, A)}`; by the same soundness lemma that singleton graph cannot accept the root, so the displayed equivalence still holds.

op_000048.proof_sketch: Induct on `A`. The atomic case is exactly `thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030`. For `ldiv` and `rdiv`, unfold `HandedSupportClosure`; the forward direction uses the induction hypothesis with `Sequent.ldiv_r` or `Sequent.rdiv_r`, and the reverse direction uses `nonempty_premises` together with `ldiv_invertible` or `rdiv_invertible`.
op_000048.prelude_code:
def HandedSupportClosure (őď : List Tp) : Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | Tp.atom s => AtomicCandidateTree őď s
  | Tp.ldiv B C => őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß HandedSupportClosure ([B] ++ őď) C
  | Tp.rdiv C B => őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß HandedSupportClosure (őď ++ [B]) C
op_000048.proof_text:
intro őď A
induction A generalizing őď with
| atom s =>
    simpa [HandedSupportClosure] using
      (thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 őď s)
| ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
    constructor
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®hőď, hC‚ü©
      exact Sequent.ldiv_r hőď ((ihC (őď := [B] ++ őď)).1 hC)
    ¬∑ intro h
      refine ‚ü®nonempty_premises h, ?_‚ü©
      exact (ihC (őď := [B] ++ őď)).2 (ldiv_invertible h)
| rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
    constructor
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®hőď, hC‚ü©
      exact Sequent.rdiv_r hőď ((ihC (őď := őď ++ [B])).1 hC)
    ¬∑ intro h
      refine ‚ü®nonempty_premises h, ?_‚ü©
      exact (ihC (őď := őď ++ [B])).2 (rdiv_invertible h)

op_000045.source_statement: Define the collection of formulas occurring anywhere in a candidate tree, and prove a global subformula property: whenever őď ‚áí A, there exists a candidate tree for őď and A in which every intermediate formula is a subformula of some formula in őď or of A.
op_000045.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp), (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ h : CandidateTree őď A, CandidateTreeFormulas h ‚äÜ {B | RootBoundedFormula őď A B}
op_000045.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized formulas occurring anywhere via explicit node labels in a `CandidateTree`, and bounded them by a root-relative subformula predicate over the original context formulas and succedent.

op_000045.proof_sketch: Use `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038` to obtain some `h : CandidateTree őď A` from the given derivation. It is enough to prove the stronger lemma that every tree is root-bounded: `CandidateTreeFormulas h ‚äÜ {B | RootBoundedFormula őď A B}` for every `h`; the smallest missing bridge is a transitivity lemma for `CandidateSubformula`, together with lifting lemmas from `c ‚ąą candidates őď` using `candidates_list_degree`. Then induct on a node witness `CandidateTreeNode h őĒ C`: the `root` case is immediate, and the `step_rdiv_arg`/`step_ldiv_arg` cases show each formula in `őĒ` already lies in `őď` while the new succedent `A` is a subformula of the principal formula in `őď`. For `step_rdiv_rec`/`step_ldiv_rec`, apply the induction hypothesis to the recursive subtree and lift its root-bounded formulas back to the original root by transitivity, since the only new child-context formula is `B`, a subformula of the principal formula in `őď`. The `ldiv_r_rec` and `rdiv_r_rec` cases are analogous: formulas inherited from the recursive tree remain bounded because `A` and `B` are subformulas of the new root succedent `A ‚ßĻ B` or `B ‚ßł A`, and the rest of the context is unchanged. Finally, apply this stronger lemma to the tree returned by `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038`.

It is enough to prove the stronger statement that every `CandidateTree őď A` is root-bounded: for any `h`, every formula in `CandidateTreeFormulas h` satisfies `RootBoundedFormula őď A`. Prove this by taking `B ‚ąą CandidateTreeFormulas h`, unpacking a witness `CandidateTreeNode h őĒ C`, and inducting on that node witness. The `root` case is immediate; in the `step_rdiv_arg` and `step_ldiv_arg` cases, `candidates_list_degree` shows the context formulas are literally in the root context `őď`, while the child succedent is a direct subformula of the principal formula picked from `őď`. For the recursive `step_rdiv_rec`, `step_ldiv_rec`, `ldiv_r_rec`, and `rdiv_r_rec` cases, apply the induction hypothesis to the smaller node and transport root-boundedness to the original root using a transitivity lemma for `CandidateSubformula` plus the obvious one-step embeddings `B ‚äĎ (B ‚ßł A)`, `A ‚äĎ (B ‚ßł A)`, `A ‚äĎ (A ‚ßĻ B)`, and `B ‚äĎ (A ‚ßĻ B)`; then conclude the required existential statement with the chosen `h`.

op_000054.source_statement: Define intrinsic candidate-tree notions for the left-only and right-only calculi, and prove that on one-sided sequents the translation to the full calculus induces a size-preserving bijection between fragment candidate trees and full candidate trees.
op_000054.formalized_statement:
(‚ąÄ (őď : List Left.Tp) (A : Left.Tp),
    ‚ąÉ e : LeftCandidateTree őď A ‚ČÉ CandidateTreeObj (Left.ctxToProductFree őď) (Left.Tp.toProductFree A),
      (‚ąÄ h, leftCandidateTreeSize h = candidateTreeObjSize (e h)) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ h, candidateTreeObjSize h = leftCandidateTreeSize (e.symm h)))
  ‚ąß
  (‚ąÄ (őď : List Right.Tp) (A : Right.Tp),
    ‚ąÉ e : RightCandidateTree őď A ‚ČÉ CandidateTreeObj (Right.ctxToProductFree őď) (Right.Tp.toProductFree A),
      (‚ąÄ h, rightCandidateTreeSize h = candidateTreeObjSize (e h)) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ h, candidateTreeObjSize h = rightCandidateTreeSize (e.symm h)))
op_000054.statement_formalization_notes: Used Type-valued witness trees, since the existing `CandidateTree : Prop` is proof-irrelevant and cannot support literal size functions or nontrivial proof-object bijections.

op_000054.proof_sketch: Define the forward maps by recursion on fragment trees: left `base/step_ldiv/ldiv_r` go to full `base/step_ldiv/ldiv_r` using `candidates_ldiv_mem` and `simpa [Left.Sequent]`, and symmetrically on the right with `candidates_rdiv_mem`. For the inverse, recurse on a full `CandidateTreeObj` whose indices are `Left.ctxToProductFree őď` and `Left.Tp.toProductFree A` (or the right analogue). The smallest missing bridge is to show that every context piece and principal formula appearing in such a full-tree node is still one-sided; `candidates_list_degree` gives the exact decomposition of the translated context, and `thm_full_one_sided_conservativity_000042` together with `exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly` / `exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly` (or the right-hand versions) converts the stored full sequent proof back to the fragment sequent proof. The foreign constructors are impossible: `step_rdiv` and `rdiv_r` cannot occur over left-only translated indices, and `step_ldiv` and `ldiv_r` cannot occur over right-only translated indices, because they would force a forbidden division symbol inside a one-sided translated context or goal. The size equalities follow by the same recursion, since each corresponding constructor contributes exactly one `Nat.succ` on both sides.

op_000050.source_statement: Define the deterministic residual atomic state obtained from a sequent `(őď, A)` by recursively peeling the outer succedent divisions of `A`, pushing each left- or right-division argument into the context and recording the required nonemptiness side conditions. Prove that `HandedSupportClosure őď A` holds exactly when this peeling process ends in a residual state `(őĒ, #s)` with `AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s`, and that `SupportClosure őď A` holds exactly when the same residual state has `‚ąÉ B ‚ąą őĒ, occurs_atom s B`.
op_000050.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  (HandedSupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ őĒ s, residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚ąß AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s) ‚ąß
  (SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ őĒ s, residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚ąß ‚ąÉ B ‚ąą őĒ, occurs_atom s B)
op_000050.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the residual peeling as a recursive function returning `none` exactly when a required nonempty-context peel fails, and `some (őĒ, s)` when the succedent reduces to the atom `#s` over residual context `őĒ`.

op_000050.proof_sketch: Induct on `A`. In the atomic case everything reduces definitionally. In the division cases, split on whether `őď` is empty; in the nonempty branch, `residualAtomicState` and `HandedSupportClosure` recurse on the same residual context, while the `SupportClosure` clause is transferred through `thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036`.
op_000050.prelude_code:
def residualAtomicState : List Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Option (List Tp √ó String)
  | őď, Tp.atom s => some (őď, s)
  | [], Tp.ldiv _ _ => none
  | őď, Tp.ldiv B C => residualAtomicState ([B] ++ őď) C
  | [], Tp.rdiv _ _ => none
  | őď, Tp.rdiv C B => residualAtomicState (őď ++ [B]) C
op_000050.proof_text:
intro őď A
induction A generalizing őď with
| atom s =>
    constructor
    ¬∑ simp [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState]
    ¬∑ simpa [support_ok, residualAtomicState] using
        (thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 őď (# s))
| ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
    cases őď with
    | nil =>
        constructor
        ¬∑ simp [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState]
        ¬∑ simpa [support_ok, residualAtomicState] using
            (thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 [] (B ‚ßĻ C))
    | cons A őď =>
        constructor
        ¬∑ simpa [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState] using
            (ihC (őď := [B] ++ (A :: őď))).1
        ¬∑ rw [thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036]
          simp [support_ok, residualAtomicState]
          rw [‚Üź thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 (B :: A :: őď) C]
          exact (ihC (őď := [B] ++ (A :: őď))).2
| rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
    cases őď with
    | nil =>
        constructor
        ¬∑ simp [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState]
        ¬∑ simpa [support_ok, residualAtomicState] using
            (thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 [] (C ‚ßł B))
    | cons A őď =>
        constructor
        ¬∑ simpa [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState] using
            (ihC (őď := (A :: őď) ++ [B])).1
        ¬∑ rw [thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036]
          simp [support_ok, residualAtomicState]
          rw [‚Üź thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 (A :: (őď ++ [B])) C]
          exact (ihC (őď := (A :: őď) ++ [B])).2

op_000056.source_statement: Define a subformula relation on `Tp`, and prove that whenever `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` and `AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s`, every formula appearing anywhere in that atomic search tree lies in the subformula closure of the original sequent `(őď, A)`.
op_000056.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
  ‚ąÄ (h : AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s),
    AtomicCandidateTreeFormulas h ‚äÜ {B | ResidualRootBoundedFormula őď A B}
op_000056.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized ‚Äúappearing anywhere‚ÄĚ via node labels of an `AtomicCandidateTree`, including explicit nodes for the auxiliary premise sequents `őĒ ‚áí A` carried by the step constructors.

op_000056.proof_sketch: First prove transitivity of `TpSubformula`; this is the smallest missing bridge, and it immediately yields a lifting lemma saying that any subformula of a `ResidualRootBoundedFormula őď A` is again `ResidualRootBoundedFormula őď A`. Next, induct on the equation `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` to show that every formula in `őĒ` is root-bounded and that `# s` is root-bounded: in the `ldiv` and `rdiv` cases, formulas inherited from the recursive residual stay bounded, while the peeled argument becomes bounded via `TpSubformula.ldiv_left`/`TpSubformula.ldiv_right` or `TpSubformula.rdiv_left`/`TpSubformula.rdiv_right`. Then prove a stronger tree lemma by induction on `h : AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s`: assuming all formulas in the root context `őĒ` and the succedent `# s` are root-bounded, every formula in `AtomicCandidateTreeFormulas h` is root-bounded. In the step cases, `candidates_list_degree` identifies the parent context as `L ++ [B ‚ßł A'] ++ őĒ ++ őõ` or `őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A' ‚ßĻ B] ++ R`, so formulas from the argument context are already formulas of the parent context, while the new formulas `A'` and `B` are subformulas of the principal formula and hence root-bounded by the lifting lemma. Unfold `AtomicCandidateTreeFormulas`, case-split on the node witness, and combine the residual-state lemma with the tree lemma to obtain the required subset inclusion.

op_000056.proof_sketch: First prove transitivity of `TpSubformula`; this gives the key lifting lemma that any subformula of a `ResidualRootBoundedFormula őď A` is again `ResidualRootBoundedFormula őď A`. Then prove by induction on `A` and case-splitting on `őď` that from `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)`, every formula in `őĒ` and also `# s` are root-bounded relative to `(őď, A)`: the atomic case is immediate, and the `ldiv`/`rdiv` cases lift the recursive bounds while the peeled argument is bounded via `TpSubformula.ldiv_left`/`ldiv_right` or `TpSubformula.rdiv_left`/`rdiv_right`. Applying this tree lemma to the bounds obtained from the residual-state lemma yields `AtomicCandidateTreeFormulas h ‚äÜ {B | ResidualRootBoundedFormula őď A B}`.

op_000055.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  ‚ąÉ reconstruct :
      { p : List Tp √ó String //
          residualAtomicState őď A = some p ‚ąß AtomicCandidateTree p.1 p.2 } ‚Üí

op_000055.proof_sketch: Define `reconstruct` by turning a residual witness into `HandedSupportClosure őď A`, then into a sequent, then into `CandidateTree őď A`. The residual-witness subtype is subsingleton because `residualAtomicState őď A` is deterministic, and surjectivity comes from extracting a residual witness from any candidate tree via the same equivalence chain.
op_000055.proof_text:
intro őď A
let S :=

      residualAtomicState őď A = some p ‚ąß AtomicCandidateTree p.1 p.2 }
have hS : Subsingleton S :=
  ‚ü®fun x y => Subtype.ext (Option.some.inj <| x.2.1.symm.trans y.2.1)‚ü©
let reconstruct : S ‚Üí CandidateTree őď A := fun x =>
  (thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038 őď A).mpr
    ((thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 őď A).mp
      (((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 őď A).1).mpr
        ‚ü®x.1.1, x.1.2, x.2.1, x.2.2‚ü©))
refine ‚ü®reconstruct, ?_‚ü©
constructor
¬∑ intro x y _
  exact hS.elim x y
¬∑ intro htree
  have hseq : őď ‚áí A :=
    (thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038 őď A).mp htree
  have hhand : HandedSupportClosure őď A :=
    (thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 őď A).mpr hseq
  rcases ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 őď A).1).mp hhand with
    ‚ü®őĒ, s, hres, hatom‚ü©
  let x : S := ‚ü®(őĒ, s), ‚ü®hres, hatom‚ü©‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®x, ?_‚ü©
  exact Subsingleton.elim _ _

op_000051.source_statement: Define left-only and right-only analogues of `HandedSupportClosure` inside the one-sided fragment calculi, and prove that for any left-only sequent, respectively right-only sequent, the obvious translation preserves and reflects the handed closure.
op_000051.formalized_statement:
(‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isLeftOnly x) ‚Üí
    isLeftOnly A ‚Üí
    (HandedSupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure őď A))
  ‚ąß
  (‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isRightOnly x) ‚Üí
    isRightOnly A ‚Üí
    (HandedSupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure őď A))
op_000051.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced fragment-level handed closures on `Left.Tp` and `Right.Tp`, with translated predicates mirroring `leftTranslatedSequent` and `rightTranslatedSequent`.

op_000051.proof_sketch: Define fragment handed closures by recursion on `Left.Tp` and `Right.Tp`, prove each is equivalent to the corresponding fragment sequent by induction, then combine `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` with `thm_full_one_sided_conservativity_000042` and the left/right witness extraction lemmas.
op_000051.prelude_code:
def leftHandedSupportClosure (őď : List Left.Tp) : Left.Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | Left.Tp.atom s => AtomicCandidateTree (Left.ctxToProductFree őď) s
  | Left.Tp.ldiv B C => őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß leftHandedSupportClosure ([B] ++ őď) C

def rightHandedSupportClosure (őď : List Right.Tp) : Right.Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | Right.Tp.atom s => AtomicCandidateTree (Right.ctxToProductFree őď) s
  | Right.Tp.rdiv C B => őď ‚Č† [] ‚ąß rightHandedSupportClosure (őď ++ [B]) C

def leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match toLeftCtx? őď, toLeftTp? A with
  | some őďL, some AL => leftHandedSupportClosure őďL AL
  | _, _ => False

def rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match toRightCtx? őď, toRightTp? A with
  | some őďR, some AR => rightHandedSupportClosure őďR AR
  | _, _ => False

lemma leftHandedSupportClosure_iff_sequent :
    ‚ąÄ (őď : List Left.Tp) (A : Left.Tp),
      leftHandedSupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ Left.Sequent őď A := by
  intro őď A
  induction A generalizing őď with
  | atom s =>
      simpa [leftHandedSupportClosure, Left.Sequent, Left.Tp.toProductFree] using
        (thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 (Left.ctxToProductFree őď) s)
  | ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
      constructor
      ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®hőď, hC‚ü©
        exact Left.Sequent.ldiv_r hőď ((ihC (őď := [B] ++ őď)).1 hC)
      ¬∑ intro h
        refine ‚ü®Left.nonempty_premises h, ?_‚ü©
        exact (ihC (őď := [B] ++ őď)).2 (Left.ldiv_invertible h)

lemma rightHandedSupportClosure_iff_sequent :
    ‚ąÄ (őď : List Right.Tp) (A : Right.Tp),
      rightHandedSupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ Right.Sequent őď A := by
  intro őď A
  induction A generalizing őď with
  | atom s =>
      simpa [rightHandedSupportClosure, Right.Sequent, Right.Tp.toProductFree] using
        (thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 (Right.ctxToProductFree őď) s)
  | rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
      constructor
      ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®hőď, hC‚ü©
        exact Right.Sequent.rdiv_r hőď ((ihC (őď := őď ++ [B])).1 hC)
      ¬∑ intro h
        refine ‚ü®Right.nonempty_premises h, ?_‚ü©
        exact (ihC (őď := őď ++ [B])).2 (Right.rdiv_invertible h)

¬∑ intro őď A hőď hA
  rcases exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly hőď with ‚ü®őďL, hőďL, _‚ü©
  rcases exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly hA with ‚ü®AL, hAL, _‚ü©
  calc
    HandedSupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ őď ‚áí A :=
      thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 őď A
    _ ‚ÜĒ leftTranslatedSequent őď A :=
      (thm_full_one_sided_conservativity_000042.1 őď A hőď hA)
    _ ‚ÜĒ leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure őď A := by
      simp [leftTranslatedSequent, leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure, hőďL, hAL,
        leftHandedSupportClosure_iff_sequent]
¬∑ intro őď A hőď hA
  rcases exists_right_ctx_of_allRightOnly hőď with ‚ü®őďR, hőďR, _‚ü©
  rcases exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly hA with ‚ü®AR, hAR, _‚ü©

    HandedSupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ őď ‚áí A :=
      thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 őď A
    _ ‚ÜĒ rightTranslatedSequent őď A :=

    _ ‚ÜĒ rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure őď A := by

op_000053.source_statement: Prove a cross-fragment rigidity theorem: if every formula in őď is left-only and A is right-only, then őď ‚áí A implies A is atomic; dually, if every formula in őď is right-only and A is left-only, then őď ‚áí A implies A is atomic.
op_000053.formalized_statement:
(‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isLeftOnly x) ‚Üí
    isRightOnly A ‚Üí
    (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí
    is_atom A)
  ‚ąß
  (‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isRightOnly x) ‚Üí
    isLeftOnly A ‚Üí
    (őď ‚áí A) ‚Üí
    is_atom A)
op_000053.statement_formalization_notes: Uses the existing fragment predicates `isLeftOnly` and `isRightOnly`, with `is_atom` as the repository‚Äôs atomicity predicate, in the standard left/right conjunctive theorem shape.

op_000053.proof_sketch: Use `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` to turn `őď ‚áí A` into `HandedSupportClosure őď A`, then apply `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050` to obtain `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` together with `AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s`. If `A` is right-only and non-atomic, `residualAtomicState` only peels outer `rdiv`s, so `őĒ` has the form `őď ++ őĒR` where `őĒR` is a nonempty right-only suffix; also `őď ‚Č† []` by derivability. The smallest extra bridge step is a boundary lemma for `AtomicCandidateTree`: by induction on its constructors, `step_ldiv` can only rewrite the left-only side and `step_rdiv` can only rewrite the right-only side, so a context with a nonempty left-only prefix and a nonempty right-only suffix can never reduce to the base tree `[ # s ]`. Hence the mixed non-atomic case is impossible, so `A` must be atomic. The second conjunct is the exact left/right mirror of the same argument.

op_000061.source_statement: Prove a residual subformula theorem: if `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)`, then every formula in `őĒ` is a subformula of some formula in `őď` or of `A`, and the atom `s` already occurs in `őď` or `A`.
op_000061.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őĒ, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [A] ++ őď, subformula B C) ‚ąß
    ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [A] ++ őď, occurs_atom s C
op_000061.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a minimal local `subformula` predicate because the provided context defines `occurs_atom` but no existing subformula relation.

op_000061.proof_sketch: Prove it by induction on `A`, unfolding `residualAtomicState`; no heavy derived theorem is needed because the procedure just peels outer `‚ßĻ` and `‚ßł` constructors deterministically. If `A = # t`, then the equation forces `őĒ = őď` and `s = t`, so every `B ‚ąą őĒ` already lies in `[A] ++ őď`, and `occurs_atom s A` gives the atom witness. If `A = B ‚ßĻ C`, the `őď = []` branch is impossible since `residualAtomicState [] (B ‚ßĻ C) = none`; otherwise the equation reduces to `residualAtomicState ([B] ++ őď) C = some (őĒ, s)`, and the induction hypothesis gives witnesses in `[C] ++ [B] ++ őď`. Transport those witnesses to `[B ‚ßĻ C] ++ őď`: a witness formula coming from `őď` is kept as is, while one coming from `C` or `B` becomes a subformula of `B ‚ßĻ C`, and similarly any `occurs_atom s` witness in `C` or `B` lifts to `occurs_atom s (B ‚ßĻ C)` by unfolding `occurs_atom`. The `A = C ‚ßł B` case is identical, using the recursive call on `(őď ++ [B], C)` and the corresponding one-step embeddings into `C ‚ßł B`.

‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String),

op_000061.proof_sketch: Unfold `residualAtomicState` and prove the statement by induction on `A`, with a small auxiliary lemma that `subformula` is transitive. If `A = # t`, then the hypothesis forces `őĒ = őď` and `s = t`; for each `B ‚ąą őĒ`, choose `C := B` using reflexivity of `subformula`, and witness the atom clause by `C := A` since `occurs_atom t (# t)` holds. If `A = B ‚ßĻ C`, the `őď = []` branch is impossible because `residualAtomicState [] (B ‚ßĻ C) = none`, and otherwise the equation becomes `residualAtomicState ([B] ++ őď) C = some (őĒ, s)`, so the induction hypothesis gives witnesses in `[C] ++ [B] ++ őď`. A witness already lying in `őď` is kept, while a witness `C` or `B` is lifted to the single formula `B ‚ßĻ C` because both are immediate subformulas of it and `occurs_atom s` also lifts along that constructor. The `A = C ‚ßł B` case is identical, using the recursive call on `residualAtomicState (őď ++ [B]) C` and the corresponding one-step subformula and `occurs_atom` embeddings into `C ‚ßł B`.

‚ąÄ {őď őĒ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {s : String},
  residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őĒ, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], occurs_atom s C)

op_000061.proof_sketch: Induct on `A`, unfold `residualAtomicState`, and in the division cases pass the induction-hypothesis witnesses from the residualized context to `őď ++ [A]` by splitting the membership witness and lifting through the outer `‚ßĻ` or `‚ßł` constructor.
op_000061.prelude_code:
inductive Subformula : Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Prop where
  | refl (A : Tp) : Subformula A A
  | ldiv_left {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A B ‚Üí Subformula A (B ‚ßĻ C)
  | ldiv_right {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A C ‚Üí Subformula A (B ‚ßĻ C)
  | rdiv_left {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A B ‚Üí Subformula A (B ‚ßł C)
  | rdiv_right {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A C ‚Üí Subformula A (B ‚ßł C)
op_000061.proof_text:
intro őď őĒ A s hres
induction A generalizing őď őĒ s with
| atom t =>
    simp [residualAtomicState] at hres
    rcases hres with ‚ü®rfl, rfl‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro B hB
      exact ‚ü®B, by simp [hB], Subformula.refl B‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact ‚ü®# t, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom]‚ü©
| ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
    cases őď with
    | nil =>
        simp [residualAtomicState] at hres
    | cons A őď =>
        have hih := ihC (őď := B :: A :: őď) (őĒ := őĒ) (s := s) hres
        rcases hih with ‚ü®hsub, hocc‚ü©
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro X hX
          rcases hsub X hX with ‚ü®Y, hYmem, hXY‚ü©
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYeq | hYmem | hYeq
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®B ‚ßĻ C, by simp, Subformula.ldiv_left hXY‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®A, by simp, hXY‚ü©
          ¬∑ exact ‚ü®Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hXY‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®B ‚ßĻ C, by simp, Subformula.ldiv_right hXY‚ü©
        ¬∑ rcases hocc with ‚ü®Y, hYmem, hYs‚ü©
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYeq | hYmem | hYeq
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®B ‚ßĻ C, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®A, by simp, hYs‚ü©
          ¬∑ exact ‚ü®Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hYs‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®B ‚ßĻ C, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]‚ü©
| rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
    cases őď with
    | nil =>
        simp [residualAtomicState] at hres
    | cons A őď =>
        have hih := ihC (őď := (A :: őď) ++ [B]) (őĒ := őĒ) (s := s) hres
        rcases hih with ‚ü®hsub, hocc‚ü©
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro X hX
          rcases hsub X hX with ‚ü®Y, hYmem, hXY‚ü©
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYmem | hYeq | hYeq
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®A, by simp, hXY‚ü©
          ¬∑ exact ‚ü®Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hXY‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®C ‚ßł B, by simp, Subformula.rdiv_right hXY‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®C ‚ßł B, by simp, Subformula.rdiv_left hXY‚ü©
        ¬∑ rcases hocc with ‚ü®Y, hYmem, hYs‚ü©
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYmem | hYeq | hYeq
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®A, by simp, hYs‚ü©
          ¬∑ exact ‚ü®Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hYs‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®C ‚ßł B, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]‚ü©
          ¬∑ subst Y
            exact ‚ü®C ‚ßł B, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]‚ü©

op_000062.source_statement: Define the atomic residual transition graph on states `(őĒ, s)` generated by one-step decompositions of `AtomicCandidateTree`, and prove finite-state recognition: `őď ‚áí A` holds exactly when the initial state returned by `residualAtomicState őď A` is accepted by that graph.
op_000062.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  (őď ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ őĒ s, residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚ąß
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s)
op_000062.statement_formalization_notes: Conservatively formalized finite-state recognition as recognition by a residual-state graph, without adding a separate finiteness/cardinality clause.

op_000062.proof_sketch: First prove that `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s)` is equivalent to `AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s` by induction, since `AtomicResidualGraphStep.rdiv/ldiv` match `AtomicCandidateTree.step_rdiv/step_ldiv` exactly and the base cases coincide. Then combine this bridge with `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050` and `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` to move between sequents, handed support closure, and residual witnesses.
op_000062.prelude_code:
abbrev AtomicResidualState := List Tp √ó String

inductive AtomicResidualGraphStep : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Prop where
  | rdiv (őď L őĒ őõ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A) :
      AtomicResidualGraphStep (őď, s) (L ++ [B] ++ őõ, s)
  | ldiv (őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A) :
      AtomicResidualGraphStep (őď, s) (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R, s)

inductive AtomicResidualGraphAccepts : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts ([# s], s)
  | step {p q : AtomicResidualState}
      (hstep : AtomicResidualGraphStep p q)
      (hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q) :
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p
op_000062.proof_text:
intro őď A
have hgraph_to_tree :
    ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ‚Üí AtomicCandidateTree p.1 p.2
¬∑ intro p hacc
  induction hacc with
  | base s =>
      exact AtomicCandidateTree.base s
  | step hstep hacc ih =>
      cases hstep with
      | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg =>
          simpa using AtomicCandidateTree.step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg ih
      | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
          simpa using AtomicCandidateTree.step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg ih
have htree_to_graph :
    ‚ąÄ {őĒ : List Tp} {s : String},
      AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s ‚Üí AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s)
¬∑ intro őĒ s htree
  induction htree with
  | base s =>
      simpa using AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base s
  | step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      exact AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step
        (AtomicResidualGraphStep.rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg) ih
  | step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      exact AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step
        (AtomicResidualGraphStep.ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg) ih
constructor
¬∑ intro hseq
  have hhand : HandedSupportClosure őď A :=
    (thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 őď A).mpr hseq
  rcases ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 őď A).1).mp hhand with
    ‚ü®őĒ, s, hres, htree‚ü©
  exact ‚ü®őĒ, s, hres, htree_to_graph htree‚ü©
¬∑ rintro ‚ü®őĒ, s, hres, hacc‚ü©
  have hhand : HandedSupportClosure őď A :=
    ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 őď A).1).mpr
