op_000057.source_statement: Prove a quantitative residual-search bound: if `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` and `AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s`, then every branch of the atomic search has length at most `list_degree őĒ`, so after residualization the nondeterministic part of proof search is depth-bounded by the residual context alone.
op_000057.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
  AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s ‚Üí
  ‚ąÄ n : Nat, AtomicBranchLength őĒ s n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ
op_000057.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized branch length with an indexed proposition `AtomicBranchLength őĒ s n` counting recursive atomic decomposition steps; the explicit `AtomicCandidateTree őĒ s` premise is kept to match the source statement.

op_000057.proof_sketch: Ignore the residual assumptions and prove the stronger fact by induction on `AtomicBranchLength`. In each step, `candidates_list_degree` identifies the ambient context, and `omega` closes the resulting one-step degree-gap inequality.
op_000057.prelude_code:
inductive AtomicBranchLength : List Tp ‚Üí String ‚Üí Nat ‚Üí Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      AtomicBranchLength [# s] s 0
  | step_rdiv (őď L őĒ őõ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String) (n : Nat)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A)
      (hrec : AtomicBranchLength (L ++ [B] ++ őõ) s n) :
      AtomicBranchLength őď s (n + 1)
  | step_ldiv (őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String) (n : Nat)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R ‚ąą candidates őď)
      (harg : őĒ ‚áí A)
      (hrec : AtomicBranchLength (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R) s n) :
      AtomicBranchLength őď s (n + 1)
op_000057.proof_text:
intro őď A őĒ s _ _ n hlen
have hmain :
    ‚ąÄ {őĒ : List Tp} {s : String} {n : Nat},
      AtomicBranchLength őĒ s n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ := by
  intro őĒ s n h
  induction h with
  | base s =>
      simp [list_degree, tp_degree]
  | step_rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s n hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hőď : őď = L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ) hc)
      have hgap : list_degree (L ++ [B] ++ őõ) + 1 ‚Č§ list_degree őď := by
        rw [hőď]
        simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
        omega
      exact le_trans (Nat.succ_le_succ ih) hgap
  | step_ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s n hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hőď : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
      have hgap : list_degree (őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R) + 1 ‚Č§ list_degree őď := by
        rw [hőď]
        simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
        omega
      exact le_trans (Nat.succ_le_succ ih) hgap
exact hmain hlen

op_000064.source_statement: If `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` and `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s)`, then every state visited along any accepting run from `(őĒ, s)` has the form `(őĒ', s)` with every formula in `őĒ'` a subformula of some formula in `őď` or of `A`.
op_000064.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
  AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
  ‚ąÄ (őĒ' : List Tp) (t : String),
    AtomicResidualGraphVisited (őĒ, s) (őĒ', t) ‚Üí
      t = s ‚ąß ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őĒ', ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [A] ++ őď, TpSubformula B C
op_000064.statement_formalization_notes: Used a local `TpSubformula` relation and an `AtomicResidualGraphVisited` predicate to encode states occurring along accepting runs.

op_000064.proof_sketch: Prove first that `TpSubformula` is transitive; this is the smallest missing bridge, because a graph step introduces the new formula `B` only as an immediate subformula of the principal division formula already present in the parent context. Next, from `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)`, do induction on `A` (with the empty-context division cases impossible) to show the root invariant `‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őĒ, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [A] ++ őď, TpSubformula B C`; in the `ldiv` and `rdiv` cases, formulas coming from the recursive residual are lifted back to the original goal by `TpSubformula.ldiv_left/right` or `TpSubformula.rdiv_left/right`. Then show that `AtomicResidualGraphStep` preserves the stronger invariant `t = s ‚ąß ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą context, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [A] ++ őď, TpSubformula B C`: `candidates_list_degree` rewrites the parent context as `L ++ [B ‚ßł A'] ++ őĒ ++ őõ` or `őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A' ‚ßĻ B] ++ R`, so inherited formulas are handled by the parent invariant, while the new `B` is bounded by transitivity through the principal formula. Finally, induct on `AtomicResidualGraphVisited (őĒ, s) (őĒ', t)`; the root case gives `t = s` and the root subformula bound from the residual-state lemma, and the step case uses the one-step preservation lemma. This yields `t = s` and `‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őĒ', ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [A] ++ őď, TpSubformula B C` as required.

op_000064.source_statement: If `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` and `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s)`, then every state visited along any accepting run from `(őĒ, s)` has the form `(őĒ', s)` with every formula in `őĒ'` a subformula of some formula in `őď` or of `A`.
op_000064.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
  AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
  ‚ąÄ (p : AtomicResidualState),
    AtomicResidualVisitedOnAcceptingRun (őĒ, s) p ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ őĒ' : List Tp, p = (őĒ', s) ‚ąß residualStateSubformulaBound őď A (őĒ', s)
op_000064.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a minimal recursive subformula predicate and a visit predicate for states lying on some accepting run.

op_000064.proof_sketch: First prove that `residualSubformulaOf` is transitive; that is the smallest missing bridge needed to lift recursive residual bounds through a peeled `‚ßĻ` or `‚ßł` formula. Then induct on `A` to show that `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` implies `residualStateSubformulaBound őď A (őĒ, s)`: in the atomic case `őĒ = őď`, and in the division cases the recursive bound for `[B] ++ őď` or `őď ++ [B]` is pushed back to the original goal by transitivity through the principal formula `B ‚ßĻ C` or `C ‚ßł B`. Next prove one-step preservation: if `residualStateSubformulaBound őď0 A0 (ctx, s)` and `AtomicResidualGraphStep (ctx, s) (ctx', s)`, then `residualStateSubformulaBound őď0 A0 (ctx', s)`; `candidates_list_degree` rewrites `ctx` as either `L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ` or `őď1 ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R`, so carried formulas stay bounded and the newly exposed `B` is an immediate subformula of the principal division formula. Finally, induct on `AtomicResidualVisitedOnAcceptingRun (őĒ, s) p`, threading the fixed second component and the bound from the initial residual state. This yields `p = (őĒ', s)` for some `őĒ'` together with `residualStateSubformulaBound őď A (őĒ', s)`; the explicit `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s)` assumption is only needed for the `here` base case.

op_000059.source_statement: Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `őď` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ HandedSupportClosure őď A`; dually, if every formula in `őď` and `A` is right-only, then `SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ HandedSupportClosure őď A`.
op_000059.formalized_statement:
(‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isLeftOnly x) ‚Üí
    isLeftOnly A ‚Üí
    (SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ HandedSupportClosure őď A))
  ‚ąß
  (‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őď, isRightOnly x) ‚Üí
    isRightOnly A ‚Üí
    (SupportClosure őď A ‚ÜĒ HandedSupportClosure őď A))
op_000059.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized in the standard left/right conjunctive shape using the existing predicates `isLeftOnly`, `isRightOnly`, `SupportClosure`, and `HandedSupportClosure`.

op_000059.proof_sketch: Refute the conjunction using the left-only side. Take őď = [#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] and A = #"p". Every member of őď is left-only, and A is left-only. `SupportClosure [#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] (#"p")` holds by replacing `#"p"` with `#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"` in the singleton support witness. If the claimed equivalence held, we would get `HandedSupportClosure [#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] (#"p")`, hence `[ #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p" ] ‚áí #"p"` by `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048`. Then `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011` forces `#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p" = #"p"`, contradiction.
op_000059.proof_text:
intro h
let bad : Tp := #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"
have hleft : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą [bad], isLeftOnly x := by
  intro x hx
  rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hx with rfl
  simp [bad, isLeftOnly]
have hA : isLeftOnly (#"p" : Tp) := by
  simp [isLeftOnly]
have hsc : SupportClosure [bad] (#"p") := by
  simpa [bad] using
    (SupportClosure.replace
      (őď := []) (L := []) (R := []) (őõ := [])
      (B := #"p") (B' := bad) (C := #"p")
      (fun s hs => by
        simp [occurs_atom, bad] at hs ‚äĘ
        exact hs)
      (SupportClosure.self (#"p")))
have hhand : HandedSupportClosure [bad] (#"p") :=
  (h.1 [bad] (#"p") hleft hA).mp hsc
have hseq : [bad] ‚áí #"p" :=
  (thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 [bad] (#"p")).mp hhand
have heq : bad = #"p" :=
  (thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011 (A := bad) (s := "p")).mp hseq
simp [bad] at heq
op_000059.counterexample_text: Concrete refutation: set őď = [#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"] and A = #"p". Then the left-only assumptions are satisfied, `SupportClosure őď A` holds, but `HandedSupportClosure őď A` does not.

op_000063.source_statement: Define a self-contained branching residual automaton on atomic states: from `(őď, s)`, each division candidate yields the continuation state together with the residual state obtained by residualizing its argument premise. Prove that `őď ‚áí A` iff the initial state returned by `residualAtomicState őď A` is accepted by this internal branching automaton.
op_000063.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  (őď ‚áí A) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ őĒ s, residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚ąß
      AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts (őĒ, s)
op_000063.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a ternary branching step so each candidate carries both the continuation state and the residualized argument state, then stated the exactness theorem using the same residualAtomicState root interface as the existing graph theorem.

op_000063.proof_sketch: This is the same strong-induction proof pattern as `thm_residual_graph_recognizes_sequent_000062`; the only new ingredient is that a branching step carries a second child witnessing the residualized argument premise. Induct on `list_degree őď + tp_degree A`. For atomic goals `A = # s`, use `thm_atom_goal_candidate_cases_000007`: the base case `őď = [# s]` gives `AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts.base`, and each candidate case produces a continuation sequent plus an argument sequent `őĒ ‚áí A'`; the continuation is smaller by the same degree inequality used in `thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030`, while the argument branch is handled by the induction hypothesis applied to the witness `residualAtomicState őĒ A' = some arg`, yielding `AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts.step`. For the reverse direction, induct on `AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts (őĒ, s)`: `base` is `Sequent.ax`, and in a `step` the continuation child gives the atomic continuation sequent by IH while the argument child, together with its residual witness, gives `őĒ ‚áí A'` by the smaller-instance induction hypothesis; then close with `Sequent.rdiv_l` or `Sequent.ldiv_l`. For `ldiv` and `rdiv` outer goals, `residualAtomicState` just peels the top connective, so the induction hypothesis combines with `ldiv_invertible`/`rdiv_invertible`, `Sequent.ldiv_r`/`Sequent.rdiv_r`, and the empty-context `none` cases match `nonempty_premises`.

Then rewrite `(Gamma => A)` by `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` and the first half of `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050`, and replace `AtomicCandidateTree` with the bridge to get exactly the claimed residual-branching acceptance criterion.

mt_main_theorem.proof_sketch: The statement is false. Use the existing same-handed counterexample `bad := #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"`. Its residual atomic state at goal `#"p"` is `some ([bad], "p")`; the residual context is left-only and contains an occurrence of `"p"`, but graph acceptance would imply `[bad] ‚áí #"p"`, contradicting the verified singleton-atom sequent characterization.
mt_main_theorem.counterexample_text: Take `bad := #"p" ‚ßĻ #"p"`, `őď = [bad]`, `A = #"p"`, `őĒ = [bad]`, and `s = "p"`. Then `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)` by simplification. Also `(‚ąÄ x ‚ąą őĒ, isLeftOnly x)` holds, since `bad` is left-only, so the same-handed premise is satisfied. The right side `‚ąÉ B ‚ąą őĒ, occurs_atom s B` holds with `B = bad`, because `occurs_atom "p" (#"p" ‚ßĻ #"p")` is true. But `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s)` is false: otherwise, using `thm_residual_graph_recognizes_sequent_000062` together with the residual-state equality, we would obtain `[bad] ‚áí #"p"`; then `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011` would force `bad = #"p"`, contradiction. So for this residual state, occurrence holds while acceptance fails.

Conversely, derivability implies support by `thm_derivable_support_ok_000032`, and an explicit residual witness gives `SupportClosure` directly.

    exact (thm_residual_graph_recognizes_sequent_000062 őď A).2 ‚ü®őĒ, s, hres, hacc‚ü©

¬∑ intro h

op_000065.source_statement: For any őď A őĒ s, if residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s), then every state reachable in the residual graph from (őĒ, s) has the form (őĒ', s) with every formula in őĒ' a subformula of some formula in őď ++ [A].
op_000065.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ {őď őĒ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {s : String}, residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) p ‚Üí ‚ąÉ őĒ' : List Tp, p = (őĒ', s) ‚ąß ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őĒ', ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C
op_000065.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized reachability as the reflexive-transitive closure `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep`, so the start state is included and every reachable state is required to preserve the same atom label `s`.

op_000065.proof_sketch: Proceed by induction on the reachability proof `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) p`. In the reflexive base case, apply `thm_residual_state_subformula_occurs_000061` to the hypothesis `residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s)`; this gives exactly that every `B ‚ąą őĒ` is a subformula of some formula in `őď ++ [A]`, and the state is `(őĒ, s)`. For the step case, first use the induction hypothesis on the predecessor state `(őĒ0, s)` to write it as `(őĒ0', s)` with the desired support property, then case on the last `AtomicResidualGraphStep`. In either constructor, `candidates_list_degree` shows the predecessor context decomposes as either `L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ` or `őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R`, so every formula in the successor context is either unchanged from the predecessor or is the exposed formula `B`. Unchanged formulas keep their original witness, and for the new `B` one uses `Subformula.rdiv_left (Subformula.refl B)` or `Subformula.ldiv_right (Subformula.refl B)` together with a small transitivity lemma for `Subformula` to compose with the predecessor witness, so the label `s` is preserved and the support invariant propagates to every reachable state.

op_000065.proof_sketch: Induct on the `Relation.ReflTransGen` reachability derivation. The base case is exactly the initial residual-state subformula theorem. In the step case, case on the final `AtomicResidualGraphStep`; inherited formulas use the induction hypothesis, while the newly exposed formula uses the predecessor witness for the principal division formula together with `subformula_trans` and `Subformula.rdiv_left` or `Subformula.ldiv_right`.
op_000065.prelude_code:
lemma subformula_trans {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A B ‚Üí Subformula B C ‚Üí Subformula A C := by
  intro hAB hBC
  induction hBC with
  | refl => exact hAB
  | ldiv_left h ih => exact Subformula.ldiv_left ih
  | ldiv_right h ih => exact Subformula.ldiv_right ih
  | rdiv_left h ih => exact Subformula.rdiv_left ih
  | rdiv_right h ih => exact Subformula.rdiv_right ih
op_000065.proof_text:
intro őď őĒ A s q hres hreach
have hstart : ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őĒ, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C :=
  (thm_residual_state_subformula_occurs_000061 (őď := őď) (őĒ := őĒ) (A := A) (s := s) hres).1
induction hreach with
| refl =>
    constructor
    ¬∑ rfl
    ¬∑ simpa using hstart
| tail hreach hstep ih =>
    rcases ih with ‚ü®hs, hsub‚ü©
    cases hstep with
    | rdiv őď0 L őĒ0 őõ A0 B0 s0 hc harg =>
        constructor
        ¬∑ exact hs
        ¬∑ intro X hX
          have hctx : őď0 = L ++ [B0 ‚ßł A0] ++ őĒ0 ++ őõ :=
            cand_rdiv_context_eq (őď := őď0) (L := L) (őĒ := őĒ0) (őõ := őõ) (A := A0) (B := B0) hc
          have hcases : X ‚ąą L ‚ą® X = B0 ‚ą® X ‚ąą őõ := by
            simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
          rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
          ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem
          ¬∑ have hmem : Tp.rdiv X A0 ‚ąą őď0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append]
            rcases hsub (Tp.rdiv X A0) hmem with ‚ü®C, hC, hSC‚ü©
            exact ‚ü®C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.rdiv_left (Subformula.refl X)) hSC‚ü©
          ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem
    | ldiv őď0 őď1 őĒ0 R A0 B0 s0 hc harg =>
        constructor
        ¬∑ exact hs
        ¬∑ intro X hX
          have hctx : őď0 = őď1 ++ őĒ0 ++ [A0 ‚ßĻ B0] ++ R := by
            symm
            simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď0) (c := Cand.ldiv őď1 őĒ0 A0 B0 R) hc)
          have hcases : X ‚ąą őď1 ‚ą® X = B0 ‚ą® X ‚ąą R := by
            simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
          rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
          ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem
          ¬∑ have hmem : Tp.ldiv A0 X ‚ąą őď0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append]
            rcases hsub (Tp.ldiv A0 X) hmem with ‚ü®C, hC, hSC‚ü©
            exact ‚ü®C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.ldiv_right (Subformula.refl X)) hSC‚ü©
          ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem

op_000067.source_statement: There is a finite branching automaton built only from subformulas of őď ++ [A] such that, whenever residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s), acceptance from (őĒ, s) in the residual graph is equivalent to acceptance in that finite automaton.

‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  ‚ąÉ M : FiniteAtomicBranchingAutomaton,
    (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą M.states, ResidualStateSubformulaBound őď A p) ‚ąß
    ‚ąÄ (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String),
      residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí
        (őĒ, s) ‚ąą M.states ‚ąß
        (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (őĒ, s) ‚ÜĒ FiniteAtomicBranchingAccepts M (őĒ, s))
op_000067.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a finite binary branching automaton on atomic residual states. ‚ÄúBuilt only from subformulas of őď ++ [A]‚ÄĚ is encoded by requiring every automaton state to satisfy the existing root-relative `Subformula` and atom-occurrence bound.

op_000067.proof_sketch: The proposed route needs two substantial ingredients that are not available in the imported theory. First, one must prove a closure lemma saying every continuation/argument state used by the internal branching presentation still satisfies the root-relative predicate `ResidualStateSubformulaBound őď A`; this is essentially the missing global reachable-state control behind open problem `op_000065`. Second, one must turn that bound into an actual `Finset` of states for `FiniteAtomicBranchingAutomaton`; no finite-enumeration machinery for bounded residual states is present, and building it from scratch requires a sizeable new development. Although `thm_residual_branching_automaton_correct_000063` exists in `Scratch.loop.lean`, it is not part of the imported context, and even after recreating it locally the finite packaging step remains unproved. So there is not a defensible short Lean formalization of the target from the verified environment provided here.

op_000067.proof_sketch: Tried a materially different route from the previous `stuck`: define a finite automaton whose states are root-subformula-bounded residual states with an additional degree cap `list_degree p.1 + 1 ‚Č§ list_degree őď + tp_degree A`, and define branches by filtering the finite state-product with the candidate/residual relation. This would use `thm_residual_state_subformula_occurs_000061`, `thm_residual_graph_recognizes_sequent_000062`, and `thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065` to prove the two acceptance directions. However, in the imported environment this still requires a substantial new prelude: the automaton/acceptance declarations themselves, an exact residual-degree lemma for `residualAtomicState`, finite-enumeration lemmas for bounded residual states, and closure lemmas showing argument residuals produced from a bounded state remain root-bounded and inside the degree cap. Those ingredients are not already available, and the helper checks show they are nontrivial rather than short wrappers around existing theorems, so I cannot provide a defensible compile-ready Lean proof here.

op_000066.proof_sketch: Reuse the derived reachable-subformula invariant, then bound reachable contexts by finite subformula support and by the degree-decreasing residual step relation. This is the same argument already verified in `Scratch.loop.lean`, inlined here using helper lemmas because the proof harness imports `Generated.Manifest` and `Derived`, not `Scratch.loop`.
op_000066.prelude_code:
lemma subformula_set_finite (C : Tp) : Set.Finite {B : Tp | Subformula B C} := by
  induction C with
  | atom s =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton (# s)).subset ?_
      intro B hB
      cases hB
      simp
  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (A ‚ßĻ B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | ldiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | ldiv_right h =>
          simp [h]
  | rdiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (A ‚ßł B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | rdiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | rdiv_right h =>
          simp [h]

lemma subformula_support_finite (src : List Tp) :
    Set.Finite {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C} := by
  induction src with
  | nil =>
      simpa using (Set.finite_empty : Set.Finite (‚ąÖ : Set Tp))
  | cons C src ih =>
      rw [show ({B : Tp | ‚ąÉ D ‚ąą C :: src, Subformula B D} : Set Tp) =
          {B : Tp | Subformula B C} ‚ą™ {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ D ‚ąą src, Subformula B D} by
        ext B
        simp]
      exact (subformula_set_finite C).union ih

lemma finite_lists_bounded_of_finite {őĪ : Type*} {S : Set őĪ} (hS : S.Finite) :
    ‚ąÄ N : Nat,
      Set.Finite {l : List őĪ | l.length ‚Č§ N ‚ąß ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą l, a ‚ąą S}
  | 0 =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton ([] : List őĪ)).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      have hl0 : l.length = 0 := Nat.eq_zero_of_le_zero hl.1
      have hnil : l = [] := by
        rwa [List.length_eq_zero_iff] at hl0
      simpa [hnil]
  | N + 1 =>
      let T : Set (List őĪ) := {l | l.length ‚Č§ N ‚ąß ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą l, a ‚ąą S}
      have hT : T.Finite := finite_lists_bounded_of_finite hS N
      refine ((Set.finite_singleton ([] : List őĪ)).union
        (Set.Finite.image (fun p : őĪ √ó List őĪ => p.1 :: p.2) (hS.prod hT))).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      cases l with
      | nil =>
          simp
      | cons a tl =>
          right
          refine ‚ü®(a, tl), ?_, rfl‚ü©
          constructor
          ¬∑ exact hl.2 a (by simp)
          ¬∑ change tl.length ‚Č§ N ‚ąß ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą tl, b ‚ąą S
            constructor
            ¬∑ simpa using hl.1
            ¬∑ intro b hb
              exact hl.2 b (by simp [hb])

lemma tp_degree_pos (A : Tp) : 0 < tp_degree A := by
  cases A <;> simp [tp_degree]

lemma list_length_le_list_degree : ‚ąÄ őď : List Tp, őď.length ‚Č§ list_degree őď
  | [] => by simp [list_degree]
  | A :: őď => by
      have hA : 1 ‚Č§ tp_degree A := Nat.succ_le_of_lt (tp_degree_pos A)
      simpa [list_degree] using Nat.add_le_add hA (list_length_le_list_degree őď)

lemma atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 := by
  cases h with
  | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg =>
      have hőď : őď = L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ :=
        cand_rdiv_context_eq (őď := őď) (L := L) (őĒ := őĒ) (őõ := őõ) (A := A) (B := B) hc
      rw [hőď]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega
  | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
      have hőď : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
      rw [hőď]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega

lemma residual_reachable_degree_le {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree p.1 := by
  induction h with
  | refl =>
      exact le_rfl
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      exact le_trans (atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep).le ih
op_000066.proof_text:
intro őď őĒ A s hres
have hsubsrc : Set.Finite {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  subformula_support_finite (őď ++ [A])
have hctxfin :
    Set.Finite {ctx : List Tp |
      ctx.length ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą ctx, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  finite_lists_bounded_of_finite
    (S := {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C})
    hsubsrc (list_degree őĒ)
refine (Set.Finite.image (fun ctx : List Tp => (ctx, s)) hctxfin).subset ?_
intro q hq
rcases thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065 őď őĒ A s q hres hq with
  ‚ü®hs, hsub‚ü©
have hdeg : list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ :=
  residual_reachable_degree_le hq
refine ‚ü®q.1, ?_, ?_‚ü©
¬∑ exact ‚ü®le_trans (list_length_le_list_degree q.1) hdeg, hsub‚ü©
¬∑ cases q
  simpa [hs]

op_000068.proof_sketch: Case split on the residual-graph step. In each constructor the string component is definitionally unchanged. For the context component, use the candidate-context equality to rewrite the source list, split membership in the successor context into left/principal/right cases, and in the principal case compose the predecessor subformula witness with `Subformula.rdiv_left` or `Subformula.ldiv_right` via `subformula_trans`.
op_000068.proof_text:
intro src p q hsrc hstep
cases hstep with
| rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg =>
    constructor
    ¬∑ rfl
    ¬∑ intro X hX
      have hctx : őď = L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ :=
        cand_rdiv_context_eq (őď := őď) (L := L) (őĒ := őĒ) (őõ := őõ) (A := A) (B := B) hc
      have hcases : X ‚ąą L ‚ą® X = B ‚ą® X ‚ąą őõ := by
        simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
      rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ßł A ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append]
        rcases hsrc (X ‚ßł A) hmem with ‚ü®C, hC, hSC‚ü©
        exact ‚ü®C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.rdiv_left (Subformula.refl X)) hSC‚ü©
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem
| ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
    constructor
    ¬∑ rfl
    ¬∑ intro X hX
      have hctx : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
      have hcases : X ‚ąą őď‚āĀ ‚ą® X = B ‚ą® X ‚ąą R := by
        simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
      rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem
      ¬∑ have hmem : A ‚ßĻ X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append]
        rcases hsrc (A ‚ßĻ X) hmem with ‚ü®C, hC, hSC‚ü©
        exact ‚ü®C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.ldiv_right (Subformula.refl X)) hSC‚ü©
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem

    constructor
    ¬∑ rfl
    ¬∑ intro X hX
      have hctx : őď = L ++ [B ‚ßł A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ :=
        cand_rdiv_context_eq (őď := őď) (L := L) (őĒ := őĒ) (őõ := őõ) (A := A) (B := B) hc
      have hcases : X ‚ąą L ‚ą® X = B ‚ą® X ‚ąą őõ := by
        simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
      rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem
      ¬∑ have hmem : Tp.rdiv X A ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append]
        rcases hsrc (Tp.rdiv X A) hmem with ‚ü®C, hC, hSC‚ü©
        exact ‚ü®C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.rdiv_left (Subformula.refl X)) hSC‚ü©
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem
| ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
    constructor
    ¬∑ rfl
    ¬∑ intro X hX
      have hctx : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [A ‚ßĻ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
      have hcases : X ‚ąą őď‚āĀ ‚ą® X = B ‚ą® X ‚ąą R := by
        simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
      rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem
      ¬∑ have hmem : Tp.ldiv A X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append]
        rcases hsrc (Tp.ldiv A X) hmem with ‚ü®C, hC, hSC‚ü©
        exact ‚ü®C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.ldiv_right (Subformula.refl X)) hSC‚ü©
      ¬∑ have hmem : X ‚ąą őď := by
          rw [hctx]
          simp [List.mem_append, hX]
        exact hsrc X hmem

op_000071.source_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď őĒ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (s : String), residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí Set.Finite { q : AtomicResidualState | Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) q }
op_000071.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď őĒ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (s : String), residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚Üí Set.Finite { q : AtomicResidualState | Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) q }

op_000071.proof_sketch: Use the reachable-state invariant from `thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065`, add a local lemma that each residual step strictly decreases `list_degree`, deduce every reachable context has degree and hence length bounded by `list_degree őĒ`, and combine this with finiteness of the source subformula set and finiteness of bounded-length lists over a finite alphabet.
op_000071.prelude_code:
lemma subformula_set_finite (C : Tp) : Set.Finite {B : Tp | Subformula B C} := by
  induction C with
  | atom s =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton (Tp.atom s)).subset ?_
      intro B hB
      cases hB
      simp
  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (Tp.ldiv A B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | ldiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | ldiv_right h =>
          simp [h]
  | rdiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (Tp.rdiv A B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | rdiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | rdiv_right h =>
          simp [h]

lemma subformula_support_finite (src : List Tp) :
    Set.Finite {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C} := by
  induction src with
  | nil =>
      simpa using (Set.finite_empty : Set.Finite (‚ąÖ : Set Tp))
  | cons C src ih =>
      rw [show ({B : Tp | ‚ąÉ D ‚ąą C :: src, Subformula B D} : Set Tp) =
          {B : Tp | Subformula B C} ‚ą™ {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ D ‚ąą src, Subformula B D} by
        ext B
        simp]
      exact (subformula_set_finite C).union ih

lemma finite_lists_bounded_of_finite {őĪ : Type*} {S : Set őĪ} (hS : S.Finite) :
    ‚ąÄ N : Nat,
      Set.Finite {l : List őĪ | l.length ‚Č§ N ‚ąß ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą l, a ‚ąą S} := by
  intro N
  induction N with
  | zero =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton ([] : List őĪ)).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      cases l with
      | nil =>
          simp
      | cons a tl =>
          simp at hl
  | succ N ih =>
      let T : Set (List őĪ) := {l | l.length ‚Č§ N ‚ąß ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą l, a ‚ąą S}
      have hT : T.Finite := ih
      refine ((Set.finite_singleton ([] : List őĪ)).union
        (Set.Finite.image (fun p : őĪ √ó List őĪ => p.1 :: p.2) (hS.prod hT))).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      cases l with
      | nil =>
          simp
      | cons a tl =>
          right
          refine ‚ü®(a, tl), ?_, rfl‚ü©
          constructor
          ¬∑ exact hl.2 a (by simp)
          ¬∑ change tl.length ‚Č§ N ‚ąß ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą tl, b ‚ąą S
            constructor
            ¬∑ simpa using hl.1
            ¬∑ intro b hb
              exact hl.2 b (by simp [hb])

lemma list_length_le_list_degree : ‚ąÄ őď : List Tp, őď.length ‚Č§ list_degree őď
  | [] => by
      simp [list_degree]
  | A :: őď => by
      have hA : 1 ‚Č§ tp_degree A := by
        cases A <;> simp [tp_degree]
      have hőď : őď.length ‚Č§ list_degree őď := list_length_le_list_degree őď
      calc
        (A :: őď).length = őď.length + 1 := by simp
        _ = 1 + őď.length := by omega
        _ ‚Č§ tp_degree A + list_degree őď := Nat.add_le_add hA hőď
        _ = list_degree (A :: őď) := by simp [list_degree]

lemma atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 := by
  cases h with
  | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg =>
      have hőď : őď = L ++ [Tp.rdiv B A] ++ őĒ ++ őõ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ) hc)
      rw [hőď]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega
  | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
      have hőď : őď = őď‚āĀ ++ őĒ ++ [Tp.ldiv A B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (őď := őď) (c := Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R) hc)
      rw [hőď]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega

lemma residual_reachable_degree_le {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree p.1 := by
  induction h with
  | refl =>
      exact le_rfl
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      exact le_trans (atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep).le ih
op_000071.proof_text:
intro őď őĒ A s hres
have hsubsrc : Set.Finite {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  subformula_support_finite (őď ++ [A])
have hctxfin :
    Set.Finite {ctx : List Tp |
      ctx.length ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą ctx, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  finite_lists_bounded_of_finite
    (S := {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C})
    hsubsrc (list_degree őĒ)
refine (Set.Finite.image (fun ctx : List Tp => (ctx, s)) hctxfin).subset ?_
intro q hq
rcases thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065 őď őĒ A s q hres hq with
  ‚ü®hs, hsub‚ü©
have hdeg : list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ :=
  residual_reachable_degree_le hq
refine ‚ü®q.1, ?_, ?_‚ü©
¬∑ exact ‚ü®le_trans (list_length_le_list_degree q.1) hdeg, hsub‚ü©
¬∑ cases q
  simpa using hs.symm

op_000075.source_statement: For every finite list src and atom s, the class of states p such that p.2 = s and every formula B in p.1 is a subformula of some formula C in src is finite.
op_000075.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (src : List Tp) (s : String), Set.Finite { p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ‚ąß ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C }
op_000075.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the class as a set of `AtomicResidualState` with fixed second component `s` and with every formula in the first component a subformula of some formula in `src`; the phrase 'finite list' is represented by Lean's `List`.

op_000075.proof_sketch: The statement is false. Specialize the claimed finiteness to `src = [# "a"]` and `s = "a"`. Because `Subformula` is reflexive, every state `(List.replicate n (# "a"), "a")` lies in the resulting set: every formula occurring in the first component is exactly `# "a"`, hence a subformula of the unique source formula. This yields an injective map `Nat ‚Üí S`. If `S` were finite, then the subtype `S` would be a finite type, and `Finite.of_injective` would imply that `Nat` is finite, contradiction.
op_000075.proof_text:
intro h
let S : Set AtomicResidualState :=
  { p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = "a" ‚ąß ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [# "a"], Subformula B C }
have hSfin : S.Finite := by
  simpa [S] using h [# "a"] "a"
let f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí S := fun n =>
  ‚ü®(List.replicate n (# "a"), "a"), by
    constructor
    ¬∑ rfl
    ¬∑ intro B hB
      refine ‚ü®# "a", by simp, ?_‚ü©
      have hEq : B = # "a" := eq_of_mem_replicate hB
      simpa [hEq] using (Subformula.refl (# "a"))‚ü©
letI : Fintype S := hSfin.fintype
have hf : Function.Injective f := by
  intro m n hmn
  simpa [f] using congr_arg (fun x : S => x.1.1.length) hmn
haveI : Finite ‚Ąē := Finite.of_injective f hf
exact not_finite ‚Ąē
op_000075.counterexample_text: Take `src = [# "a"]` and `s = "a"`. For each `n : Nat`, the state `p_n = (List.replicate n (# "a"), "a")` satisfies the defining property, since every `B ‚ąą p_n.1` is equal to `# "a"`, and `Subformula.refl` gives `Subformula (# "a") (# "a")`. The states `p_n` are pairwise distinct because their first components have different lengths, so this same-atom subformula-bounded class is infinite.

op_000075.proof_sketch: The statement is false. Specialize to `src = [# "a"]` and `s = "a"`. Then every state `(List.replicate n (# "a"), "a")` belongs to the set, because each formula appearing in the first component is equal to `# "a"`, hence is a subformula of the unique source formula by `Subformula.refl`. This gives an injective map `Nat ‚Üí S`. If `S` were finite, then `S` would be a fintype, so `Finite.of_injective` would make `Nat` finite, contradicting `not_finite ‚Ąē`.
op_000075.proof_text:
intro h
let S : Set AtomicResidualState :=
  { p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = "a" ‚ąß ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą [# "a"], Subformula B C }
have hSfin : S.Finite := by
  simpa [S] using h [# "a"] "a"
let f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí S := fun n =>
  ‚ü®(List.replicate n (# "a"), "a"), by
    constructor
    ¬∑ rfl
    ¬∑ intro B hB
      refine ‚ü®# "a", by simp, ?_‚ü©
      have hEq : B = # "a" := List.eq_of_mem_replicate hB
      simpa [hEq] using (Subformula.refl (# "a"))‚ü©
letI : Fintype S := hSfin.fintype
have hf : Function.Injective f := by
  intro m n hmn
  simpa [f] using congr_arg (fun x : S => x.1.1.length) hmn
haveI : Finite ‚Ąē := Finite.of_injective f hf
exact not_finite ‚Ąē
op_000075.counterexample_text: Take `src = [# "a"]` and `s = "a"`. For each `n : Nat`, define `p_n = (List.replicate n (# "a"), "a")`. Every `B ‚ąą p_n.1` is equal to `# "a"` by `List.eq_of_mem_replicate`, so `Subformula.refl (# "a")` shows that `B` is a subformula of the unique element of `src`. Thus each `p_n` lies in the target set. These states are pairwise distinct because their first components have lengths `n`, so the set is infinite. Therefore the claimed finiteness statement is false.

op_000070.source_statement: ‚ąÄ (src : List Tp) (p q : AtomicResidualState), (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) ‚Üí AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí q.2 = p.2 ‚ąß list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 ‚ąß (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą q.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C)
op_000070.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (src : List Tp) (p q : AtomicResidualState), (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) ‚Üí AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí q.2 = p.2 ‚ąß list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 ‚ąß (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą q.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C)
op_000070.statement_formalization_notes: This matches the natural-language target directly and aligns with the existing one-step subformula-closure and degree-descent lemmas in Derived.

op_000070.proof_sketch: Combine the existing one-step subformula-closure theorem with the one-step strict degree-drop lemma, then package the conclusions in the required conjunction order.
op_000070.proof_text:
intro src p q hsrc hstep
rcases thm_residual_step_subformula_closed_000068 src p q hsrc hstep with ‚ü®hs, hsub‚ü©
exact ‚ü®hs, atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep, hsub‚ü©

op_000080.source_statement: There exist őď, A, őĒ, s, and a state p such that residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s), p.2 = s, list_degree p.1 ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ, every formula in p.1 is a subformula of some formula in őď ++ [A], but p is not reachable from (őĒ, s) by residual-graph steps.
op_000080.formalized_statement:
‚ąÉ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (őĒ : List Tp) (s : String) (p : AtomicResidualState),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some (őĒ, s) ‚ąß
  p.2 = s ‚ąß
  list_degree p.1 ‚Č§ list_degree őĒ ‚ąß
  (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą őď ++ [A], Subformula B C) ‚ąß
  ¬¨ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) p
op_000080.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the witness state as `p : AtomicResidualState` and interpreted ‚Äúreachable by residual-graph steps‚ÄĚ as `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) p`, matching the repository‚Äôs existing reachability statements.

op_000080.proof_sketch: Use witnesses őď = [# "a"], A = (# "a" ‚ßĻ # "a"), őĒ = [# "a", # "a"], s = "a", and p = ([# "a"], "a"). The residual-state equation is by unfolding `residualAtomicState`; the degree and subformula bounds are immediate. For non-reachability, show that every state reachable from `([# "a", # "a"], "a")` equals the start state, because `candidates [# "a", # "a"]` is empty, so no residual step can occur from that all-atom context.
op_000080.proof_text:
refine ‚ü®[# "a"], # "a" ‚ßĻ # "a", [# "a", # "a"], "a", ([# "a"], "a"), ?_‚ü©
constructor
¬∑ simp [residualAtomicState]
constructor
¬∑ rfl
constructor
¬∑ simp [list_degree, tp_degree]
constructor
¬∑ intro B hB
  simp at hB
  subst hB
  refine ‚ü®# "a", ?_, Subformula.refl (# "a")‚ü©
  simp
¬∑ intro hreach
  suffices hstay : ‚ąÄ q, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep ([# "a", # "a"], "a") q ‚Üí q = ([# "a", # "a"], "a")
  ¬∑ have hp : ([# "a"], "a") = ([# "a", # "a"], "a") := hstay ([# "a"], "a") hreach
    simp at hp
  ¬∑ intro q h
    induction h with
    | refl => rfl
    | tail hreach hstep ih =>
        subst ih
        exfalso
        cases hstep with
        | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg =>
            simp [candidates, picks] at hc
        | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
            simp [candidates, picks] at hc

op_000072.source_statement: ‚ąÄ (src : List Tp) (s : String) (N : Nat), Set.Finite { q : AtomicResidualState | q.2 = s ‚ąß list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ N ‚ąß (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą q.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) }
op_000072.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (src : List Tp) (s : String) (N : Nat),
  Set.Finite { q : AtomicResidualState |
    q.2 = s ‚ąß
    list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ N ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą q.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) }
op_000072.statement_formalization_notes: Kept the statement in the ambient `AtomicResidualState` set-comprehension form used by nearby residual-state theorems; no extra prelude declarations are needed.

op_000072.proof_sketch: Use `subformula_support_finite src` to get a finite support set of formulas, then `finite_lists_bounded_of_finite` to show there are only finitely many lists of length at most `N` whose entries lie in that support. Every state in the target has second component exactly `s`, so it lies in the image of that finite list set under `ctx ‚Ü¶ (ctx, s)`. The degree bound implies the needed length bound via `list_length_le_list_degree`.
op_000072.proof_text:
intro src s N
have hsubsrc : Set.Finite {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C} :=
  subformula_support_finite src
have hctxfin :
    Set.Finite {ctx : List Tp |
      ctx.length ‚Č§ N ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą ctx, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C} :=
  finite_lists_bounded_of_finite
    (S := {B : Tp | ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C})
    hsubsrc N
refine (Set.Finite.image (fun ctx : List Tp => (ctx, s)) hctxfin).subset ?_
intro q hq
refine ‚ü®q.1, ?_, ?_‚ü©
¬∑ exact ‚ü®le_trans (list_length_le_list_degree q.1) hq.2.1, hq.2.2‚ü©
¬∑ cases q
  simpa using hq.1.symm

op_000085.source_statement: For every `src`, `s`, and `N`, residual acceptance on the bounded slice `{ p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ‚ąß list_degree p.1 ‚Č§ N ‚ąß (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) }` admits an internal finite characterization by backward induction on `list_degree`.
op_000085.statement_formalization_notes: The phrase ‚Äúadmits an internal finite characterization by backward induction on list_degree‚ÄĚ is not precise enough to formalize reliably from the current context alone. It could mean a finite-set exactness theorem, existence of a recursive boolean recognizer on the bounded slice, or a fixed-point/inductive characterization of `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts`, and the repository does not provide a canonical predicate for that notion.

op_000085.source_statement: For every `src`, `s`, and `N`, residual acceptance on the bounded slice `{ p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ‚ąß list_degree p.1 ‚Č§ N ‚ąß (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) }` admits an internal finite characterization by backward induction on `list_degree`.
op_000085.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (src : List Tp) (s : String) (N : Nat),
  ‚ąÉ S : Finset AtomicResidualState,
    (‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
      p ‚ąą S ‚ÜĒ
        p.2 = s ‚ąß
        list_degree p.1 ‚Č§ N ‚ąß
        (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C)) ‚ąß
    ‚ąÉ good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Prop,
      (‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, p ‚ąą S ‚Üí
        (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ‚ÜĒ good p)) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, p ‚ąą S ‚Üí
        (good p ‚ÜĒ
          p = ([# s], s) ‚ą®
            ‚ąÉ q : AtomicResidualState,
              q ‚ąą S ‚ąß
              list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 ‚ąß
              AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚ąß
              good q)
op_000085.statement_formalization_notes: Interprets 'internal finite characterization' as a finite set exactly equal to the bounded slice together with a degree-decreasing recursive predicate equivalent to `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on that slice.

op_000085.proof_sketch: Take the bounded slice as a finite set using `thm_degree_bounded_subformula_slice_finite_000072`, convert it to a `Finset`, and set `good := AtomicResidualGraphAccepts`. The recursive characterization then follows by cases on acceptance: `base` gives the singleton state, while `step` stays inside the slice and strictly decreases `list_degree` by `thm_residual_step_degree_subformula_000070`.
op_000085.proof_text:
intro src s N
classical
let T : Set AtomicResidualState :=
  { p : AtomicResidualState |
      p.2 = s ‚ąß
      list_degree p.1 ‚Č§ N ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) }
have hfin : T.Finite := by

op_000084.source_statement: For every finite `src` and atom `s`, a subset of `{ p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ‚ąß (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) }` is finite if and only if the values `list_degree p.1` are uniformly bounded on that subset.
op_000084.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (src : List Tp) (s : String) (S : Set AtomicResidualState), S ‚äÜ { p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ‚ąß (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą src, Subformula B C) } ‚Üí (S.Finite ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ N : Nat, ‚ąÄ p ‚ąą S, list_degree p.1 ‚Č§ N)
op_000084.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the unspecified subset as a set `S` contained in the ambient same-atom subformula state set; `src` is taken finite via the repository‚Äôs `List Tp` representation.

op_000084.proof_sketch: Forward: the image of a finite set `S` under `p ‚Ü¶ list_degree p.1` is a finite set of naturals, hence bounded above by `Set.finite_iff_bddAbove`. Reverse: if all `list_degree p.1` are bounded by `N`, then `S` is contained in the finite degree-bounded same-atom subformula slice from `thm_degree_bounded_subformula_slice_finite_000072`.

intro src s S hSub
constructor
¬∑ intro hfin
  have hfinImg :
      Set.Finite ((fun p : AtomicResidualState => list_degree p.1) '' S) :=
    Set.Finite.image (fun p : AtomicResidualState => list_degree p.1) hfin
  rcases (Set.finite_iff_bddAbove.mp hfinImg) with ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®N, ?_‚ü©
  intro p hp
  exact hN ‚ü®p, hp, rfl‚ü©
¬∑ rintro ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
  refine (thm_degree_bounded_subformula_slice_finite_000072 src s N).subset ?_
  intro p hp
  have hp' := hSub hp
  exact ‚ü®hp'.1, hN p hp, hp'.2‚ü©

op_000078.proof_sketch: Use `list_degree` on the residual context as a measure. The lemma `atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt` shows that any step `AtomicResidualGraphStep p q` gives `list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1`, so the reversed step relation is a subrelation of `measure (fun p : AtomicResidualState => list_degree p.1)`, hence well-founded.

intro q p hstep

op_000103.source_statement: For every root state p, the set of states reachable from p is finite and AtomicResidualGraphAccepts restricted to that reachable set is the least subset that contains ([# (p.2)], p.2) and is closed under taking predecessors along AtomicResidualGraphStep.
op_000103.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
  Set.Finite { q : AtomicResidualState | Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q } ‚ąß
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí
        (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q ‚ÜĒ
          ‚ąÄ S : Set AtomicResidualState,
            ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą S ‚Üí
            (‚ąÄ a b : AtomicResidualState,
              Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p a ‚Üí
              Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p b ‚Üí
              AtomicResidualGraphStep a b ‚Üí
              b ‚ąą S ‚Üí
              a ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
            q ‚ąą S)
op_000103.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized leastness by a universal property over sets containing the base state, with predecessor closure restricted to states reachable from the root p.

op_000103.proof_sketch: Prove a reachable-state invariant by induction on `ReflTransGen`: the atom label is preserved, degree never increases, and every reachable formula stays a subformula of some formula in the root context. This puts the reachable set inside the finite bounded subformula slice. For leastness, first prove `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r` iff `r` reaches its distinguished base state. The forward direction then inducts backward along a path to the base inside any predecessor-closed reachable set, and the reverse direction uses the set consisting of the base state together with reachable accepting states.
op_000103.proof_text:
intro p
have hp_sub : ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą p.1, Subformula B C := by
  intro B hB
  exact ‚ü®B, hB, Subformula.refl B‚ü©
have hreach_inv :
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí
        q.2 = p.2 ‚ąß
          list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree p.1 ‚ąß
          (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą q.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą p.1, Subformula B C) := by
  intro q hq
  induction hq with
  | refl =>
      exact ‚ü®rfl, le_rfl, hp_sub‚ü©
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      rcases thm_residual_step_degree_subformula_000070 p.1 _ _ ih.2.2 hstep with
        ‚ü®hs, hlt, hsub‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®hs.trans ih.1, le_trans (Nat.le_of_lt hlt) ih.2.1, hsub‚ü©
have hacc_to_reaches_base :
    ‚ąÄ r : AtomicResidualState,
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r ‚Üí
        Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep r ([# (r.2)], r.2) := by
  intro r hacc
  induction hacc with
  | base s =>
      exact Relation.ReflTransGen.refl
  | step hstep hacc ih =>
      cases hstep with
      | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg =>
          simpa using
            Relation.ReflTransGen.head
              (AtomicResidualGraphStep.rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg) ih
      | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
          simpa using
            Relation.ReflTransGen.head
              (AtomicResidualGraphStep.ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg) ih
have hreaches_base_to_acc :
    ‚ąÄ r : AtomicResidualState,
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep r ([# (r.2)], r.2) ‚Üí
        AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r := by
  intro r
  rcases r with ‚ü®őď, s‚ü©
  intro hreach
  have hback :
      ‚ąÄ {a : AtomicResidualState},
        Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep a ([# s], s) ‚Üí
          AtomicResidualGraphAccepts a := by
    intro a h
    exact Relation.ReflTransGen.head_induction_on h
      (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base s)
      (fun hstep _ ih => AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step hstep ih)
  exact hback hreach
constructor
¬∑ refine (thm_degree_bounded_subformula_slice_finite_000072 p.1 p.2 (list_degree p.1)).subset ?_
  intro q hq
  exact hreach_inv q hq
¬∑ intro q hq
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro hacc
    intro S hbase hpred
    have hs : q.2 = p.2 := (hreach_inv q hq).1
    have hqbase : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q ([# (p.2)], p.2) := by
      simpa [hs] using hacc_to_reaches_base q hacc
    have hleast_of_path :
        ‚ąÄ {a : AtomicResidualState},
          Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep a ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚Üí
          Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p a ‚Üí
          a ‚ąą S := by
      intro a habase
      induction habase using Relation.ReflTransGen.head_induction_on with
      | refl =>
          intro _
          exact hbase
      | head hstep hrest ih =>
          intro hpa
          exact hpred _ _ hpa (Relation.ReflTransGen.tail hpa hstep) hstep
            (ih (Relation.ReflTransGen.tail hpa hstep))
    exact hleast_of_path hqbase hq
  ¬∑ intro hleast
    let S : Set AtomicResidualState :=
      {r : AtomicResidualState |
        r = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
          (Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p r ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r)}
    have hbase : ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą S := by
      left
      rfl
    have hclosed :
        ‚ąÄ a b : AtomicResidualState,
          Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p a ‚Üí
          Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p b ‚Üí
          AtomicResidualGraphStep a b ‚Üí
          b ‚ąą S ‚Üí
          a ‚ąą S := by
      intro a b ha hb hstep hbS
      rcases hbS with rfl | ‚ü®_, hbacc‚ü©
      ¬∑ right

    have hqS : q ‚ąą S := hleast S hbase hclosed
    rcases hqS with rfl | ‚ü®_, hacc‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base (p.2)
    ¬∑ exact hacc

op_000101.source_statement: For every `őď`, `A`, and `p`, if `residualAtomicState őď A = some p`, then there exists a finite set `R` exactly equal to the states reachable from `p` by residual steps together with a Boolean labeling on `R`, computed by reverse recursion on `list_degree`, whose value at each state is equivalent to `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on that state.
op_000101.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (p : AtomicResidualState),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some p ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÉ good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool,
        (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)) ‚ąß
        ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
          (good q = true ‚ÜĒ
            q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
              ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
                r ‚ąą R ‚ąß
                list_degree r.1 < list_degree q.1 ‚ąß
                AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß
                good r = true)
op_000101.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the finite reachable region as a `Finset` and the labeling as `AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool`; the reverse-recursion clause is encoded by recursive dependence only on strictly smaller `list_degree` states.

op_000101.proof_sketch: Take `R` to be the `toFinset` of the reachable set from `p`, using `thm_residual_reachable_finite_000071`. Define `good q := decide (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)` classically. On reachable states, `good q = true` is equivalent to acceptance by simplification. For the recursive clause, use `thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097`; the base case is rewritten to `([# (p.2)], p.2)` via `thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065`, and the step case stays inside `R` by `Relation.ReflTransGen.tail` with degree decrease from `atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt`.
op_000101.proof_text:
intro őď A p hres
classical
rcases p with ‚ü®őĒ, s‚ü©
have hfin :
    Set.Finite {q : AtomicResidualState |
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) q} :=
  thm_residual_reachable_finite_000071 őď őĒ A s hres
refine ‚ü®hfin.toFinset, ?_, ?_‚ü©
¬∑ intro q
  simpa using (Set.Finite.mem_toFinset hfin (a := q))
¬∑ let good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool := fun q => decide (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
  refine ‚ü®good, ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ intro q hq
    simp [good]
  ¬∑ intro q hq
    have hreach : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (őĒ, s) q := by

op_000106.source_statement: For every root state p, there exists a unique Boolean labeling of the finite set `{ q : AtomicResidualState | Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q }` such that `([# (p.2)], p.2)` is labeled `true` and any other reachable state q is labeled `true` exactly when some one-step successor of q is labeled `true`; moreover this labeling agrees with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on the reachable set.
op_000106.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
  ‚ąÉ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
    (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚ąß
    ‚ąÉ! good : {q // q ‚ąą R} ‚Üí Bool,
      (‚ąÄ q : {q // q ‚ąą R},
        good q = true ‚ÜĒ
          q.1 = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
            ‚ąÉ r : {r // r ‚ąą R},
              AtomicResidualGraphStep q.1 r.1 ‚ąß
              good r = true) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ q : {q // q ‚ąą R},
        (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1 ‚ÜĒ good q = true)
op_000106.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the reachable set as a `Finset` and the labeling as a Boolean function on the reachable-state subtype; the base case is folded into the recursion clause via the disjunct `q.1 = ([# (p.2)], p.2)`.

op_000106.proof_sketch: Take `R` to be the reachable finset from `thm_reachable_accepts_least_set_000103`. Define `good` on the reachable subtype by `decide (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1)`. The acceptance clause is immediate. For the recursive clause, use `thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097`; any accepting successor of a reachable state is reachable by transitivity, and reachability preserves the second component so the base state matches `([# (p.2)], p.2)`. Uniqueness is pointwise from the acceptance-equivalence clause.
op_000106.proof_text:
intro p
classical
rcases thm_reachable_accepts_least_set_000103 p with ‚ü®hfin, _‚ü©
let R : Finset AtomicResidualState := hfin.toFinset
have hR : ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q := by
  intro q
  simpa [R] using (Set.Finite.mem_toFinset hfin (a := q))
have hp_sub : ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą p.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą p.1, Subformula B C := by
  intro B hB
  exact ‚ü®B, hB, Subformula.refl B‚ü©
have hreach_inv :
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí
        q.2 = p.2 ‚ąß
          (‚ąÄ B ‚ąą q.1, ‚ąÉ C ‚ąą p.1, Subformula B C) := by
  intro q hq
  induction hq with
  | refl =>
      exact ‚ü®rfl, hp_sub‚ü©
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      rcases thm_residual_step_subformula_closed_000068 p.1 _ _ ih.2 hstep with
        ‚ü®hs, hsub‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®hs.trans ih.1, hsub‚ü©
refine ‚ü®R, hR, ?_‚ü©
let good : {q // q ‚ąą R} ‚Üí Bool := fun q => decide (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1)
have hgood_acc : ‚ąÄ q : {q // q ‚ąą R}, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1 ‚ÜĒ good q = true := by
  intro q
  simp [good]
refine ‚ü®good, ?_, ?_‚ü©
¬∑ constructor
  ¬∑ intro q
    have hreach : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q.1 := (hR q.1).mp q.2
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hgood
      have hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1 := (hgood_acc q).mpr hgood
      rcases (thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097 q.1).mp hacc with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, hracc‚ü©
      ¬∑ left
        have hs : q.1.2 = p.2 := (hreach_inv q.1 hreach).1
        simpa [hs] using hbase
      ¬∑ right
        let r' : {r // r ‚ąą R} := ‚ü®r, (hR r).2 (Relation.ReflTransGen.tail hreach hstep)‚ü©
        refine ‚ü®r', ?_, ?_‚ü©
        ¬∑ simpa [r'] using hstep
        ¬∑ exact (hgood_acc r').mp hracc
    ¬∑ intro h
      rcases h with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, hgood‚ü©
      ¬∑ have hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1 := by
          simpa [hbase] using (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base (p.2))
        exact (hgood_acc q).mp hacc
      ¬∑ have hracc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r.1 := (hgood_acc r).mpr hgood
        have hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1 := AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step hstep hracc
        exact (hgood_acc q).mp hacc
  ¬∑ exact hgood_acc
¬∑ intro g hg
  rcases hg with ‚ü®_, hg_acc‚ü©
  funext q
  have hiff : g q = true ‚ÜĒ good q = true := by
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hgtrue
      exact (hgood_acc q).mp ((hg_acc q).mpr hgtrue)
    ¬∑ intro hgoodtrue
      exact (hg_acc q).mp ((hgood_acc q).mpr hgoodtrue)
  cases hgq : g q <;> cases hgoodq : good q <;> simp [hgq, hgoodq] at hiff ‚äĘ

op_000110.source_statement: For every `őď`, `A`, and `p` with `residualAtomicState őď A = some p`, any reverse topological sweep of the finite reachable region from `p` that marks `([# (p.2)], p.2)` as `true` and propagates truth backward along `AtomicResidualGraphStep` computes the same Boolean labeling, and that labeling agrees exactly with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on the reachable states.
op_000110.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (A : Tp) (p : AtomicResidualState),
  residualAtomicState őď A = some p ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÉ ord‚āÄ : List AtomicResidualState,
        ‚ąÉ good‚āÄ : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool,
          ReverseTopologicalSweep p R ord‚āÄ good‚āÄ ‚ąß
          ‚ąÄ ord : List AtomicResidualState,
            ‚ąÄ good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool,
              ReverseTopologicalSweep p R ord good ‚Üí
              (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí good q = good‚āÄ q) ‚ąß
              (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q))
op_000110.statement_formalization_notes: Encoded a reverse topological sweep by a list order on the reachable `Finset` with every residual step pointing to an earlier `idxOf`, and encoded the sweep result as the Boolean labeling satisfying the backward base-or-successor recursion on that reachable region.

op_000110.proof_sketch: The nearest reusable theorem is `thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106`, which already gives for any residual state `p` a reachable finset `R`, exact characterization of reachability, and a unique Boolean labeling on `{q // q ‚ąą R}` satisfying the base/step recursion and agreeing with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts`. From that theorem, extend the unique subtype-labeling to a total function `good‚āÄ : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool`; on `R` this is the canonical sweep result, and the hypothesis `residualAtomicState őď A = some p` is not essential because the theorem is stronger and works for arbitrary `p`. The only bridge missing from the current derived results is existence of `ord‚āÄ`: enumerate `R` in nondecreasing `list_degree q.1` (or any linear extension of that measure). This is a reverse topological order because `atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt` shows every edge `q ‚ü∂ r` strictly lowers `list_degree r.1`, so `r` must occur earlier than `q`; equivalently `thm_atomic_residual_step_wellfounded_000078` gives the needed acyclicity/well-foundedness. For any other `ReverseTopologicalSweep p R ord good`, its recursion clause is exactly the one used in `thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106`, so uniqueness forces `good q = good‚āÄ q` on `R`. The acceptance equivalence `(good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)` on `R` then follows from the same theorem.

op_000105.source_statement: For every root state p and every state q reachable from p, `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q` iff every terminal state r reachable from q is equal to `([# (p.2)], p.2)`.
op_000105.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ p q : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ r : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚Üí r = ([# (p.2)], p.2))
op_000105.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized ‚Äúreachable‚ÄĚ with `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep` and ‚Äúterminal‚ÄĚ as having no outgoing `AtomicResidualGraphStep` successor.

op_000112.source_statement: For every p : AtomicResidualState, any reverse dynamic-programming evaluation on a topological ordering of the states reachable from p, using the local rule 'base state or some one-step successor is labeled true', produces the same Boolean labeling on the reachable region, and that labeling agrees with AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.
op_000112.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
  ‚ąÉ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
    (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚ąß
    ‚ąÉ ord‚āÄ : List AtomicResidualState,
      ‚ąÉ good‚āÄ : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool,
        ReverseTopologicalSweep p R ord‚āÄ good‚āÄ ‚ąß
        ‚ąÄ ord : List AtomicResidualState,
          ‚ąÄ good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool,
            ReverseTopologicalSweep p R ord good ‚Üí
            (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí good q = good‚āÄ q) ‚ąß
            (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q))
op_000112.statement_formalization_notes: Reused the prior reverse-topological-sweep encoding: the reachable region is a `Finset`, a topological order is a nodup list whose `idxOf` decreases along `AtomicResidualGraphStep`, and a sweep result is a total Boolean labeling satisfying the base-or-successor recursion on that region.

op_000112.proof_sketch: `thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106` does give the canonical reachable-set Boolean labeling and its uniqueness, so the only real missing ingredient is an explicit `ord‚āÄ : List AtomicResidualState` witnessing `ReverseTopologicalSweep`. In this repo there is no existing `ReverseTopologicalSweep`/topological-order infrastructure, and the natural way to build `ord‚āÄ` is to sort the finite reachable set by a lexicographic key refining `list_degree`. I was able to identify a plausible route using `atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt`, but making it Lean-clean requires new order/encoding machinery for `AtomicResidualState` plus nontrivial `Finset.sort`/`idxOf` arguments, and that bridge is not already present among the verified theorems. Given the need for substantial new prelude infrastructure and the lack of a verified end-to-end check here, I cannot defensibly claim a finished proof.

op_000112.source_statement: For every p : AtomicResidualState, any reverse dynamic-programming evaluation on a topological ordering of the states reachable from p, using the local rule 'base state or some one-step successor is labeled true', produces the same Boolean labeling on the reachable region, and that labeling agrees with AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.
op_000112.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
  ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
    (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ L : List AtomicResidualState,
      L.Nodup ‚Üí
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚ÜĒ q ‚ąą R) ‚Üí
      (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
        r ‚ąą L ‚Üí
        AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
        L.idxOf q < L.idxOf r) ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ! good : {q // q ‚ąą R} ‚Üí Bool,
        (‚ąÄ q : {q // q ‚ąą R},
          good q = true ‚ÜĒ
            q.1 = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
              ‚ąÉ r : {r // r ‚ąą R},
                AtomicResidualGraphStep q.1 r.1 ‚ąß
                good r = true) ‚ąß
        ‚ąÄ q : {q // q ‚ąą R},
          AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1 ‚ÜĒ good q = true
op_000112.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized a reverse dynamic-programming evaluation by a Boolean labeling on the reachable finite region satisfying the local base-or-successor rule. A topological ordering is encoded by a nodup list of the reachable states with `L.idxOf q < L.idxOf r` for each edge `q ‚Üí r`, so reversing the list corresponds to the intended sweep.

op_000112.proof_sketch: Use `thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106 p` to obtain the unique Boolean labeling on some reachable finset `R‚āÄ`. The hypotheses `‚ąÄ q, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ ReflTransGen ...` and the corresponding characterization of `R‚āÄ` imply `R = R‚āÄ` by `Finset.ext`. After substituting this equality, the target is exactly the uniqueness statement returned by `thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106`; the list/topological-order hypotheses are unused.
op_000112.proof_text:
intro p R hR L hLnodup hL htop
rcases thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106 p with ‚ü®R‚āÄ, hR‚āÄ, huniq‚ü©
have hEq : R = R‚āÄ := by
  apply Finset.ext
  intro q
  rw [hR q, hR‚āÄ q]
subst R
exact huniq

op_000115.source_statement: For every p : AtomicResidualState, if R is the finite set of states reachable from p, then listing the elements of R in nondecreasing order of list_degree q.1 yields a reverse topological ordering: whenever q,r ‚ąą R and AtomicResidualGraphStep q r, the position of r is strictly earlier than the position of q.
op_000115.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState, (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí r ‚ąą R ‚Üí AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí (R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)).idxOf r < (R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)).idxOf q
op_000115.statement_formalization_notes: Encodes the reachable region as a `Finset` and the sorted listing as `R.toList.mergeSort`; positions are compared via `List.idxOf`.

op_000115.proof_sketch: Let L be the degree-sorted list obtained by merge-sorting R.toList. A one-step residual transition strictly decreases list_degree by atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt, so q and r cannot have the same degree and hence q ‚Č† r. The merge-sorted list is pairwise nondecreasing in list_degree, and q,r both occur in L because mergeSort permutes R.toList. If r were not strictly earlier than q, then q would be at or before r; since q ‚Č† r, it would actually be strictly before r, and pairwise sortedness would force list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree r.1, contradicting the strict decrease along q ‚Üí r.
op_000115.proof_text:
intro p R hR q r hqR hrR hstep
classical
let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)
have hqL : q ‚ąą L := by
  simpa [L] using
    ((List.Perm.mem_iff (List.mergeSort_perm _ _)).2 (by simpa using (Finset.mem_toList.2 hqR)))
have hrL : r ‚ąą L := by
  simpa [L] using
    ((List.Perm.mem_iff (List.mergeSort_perm _ _)).2 (by simpa using (Finset.mem_toList.2 hrR)))
have hpair : L.Pairwise (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1) := by
  simpa [L] using
    (R.toList).pairwise_mergeSort
      (le := fun a b => decide (list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1))
      (fun a b c hab hbc => le_trans hab hbc)
      (by intro a b; exact le_total _ _)
have hdeg : list_degree r.1 < list_degree q.1 :=
  atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep
have hqr_ne : q ‚Č† r := by
  intro hEq
  simpa [hEq] using hdeg
have hiq : L.idxOf q < L.length := List.idxOf_lt_length_of_mem hqL
have hir : L.idxOf r < L.length := List.idxOf_lt_length_of_mem hrL
by_contra hnot
have hidx_ne : L.idxOf q ‚Č† L.idxOf r := by
  intro hidx
  exact hqr_ne ((List.idxOf_inj (l := L) (x := q) (y := r) hqL).mp hidx)
have hlt_qr : L.idxOf q < L.idxOf r :=
  lt_of_le_of_ne (Nat.le_of_not_gt hnot) hidx_ne
rw [List.pairwise_iff_get] at hpair
have hle_deg : list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree r.1 := by
  have h := hpair ‚ü®L.idxOf q, hiq‚ü© ‚ü®L.idxOf r, hir‚ü© hlt_qr
  simpa [List.idxOf_get (l := L) (a := q) hiq, List.idxOf_get (l := L) (a := r) hir] using h
exact (not_le_of_gt hdeg) hle_deg
