op_000115.proof_sketch: Let L be `R.toList` sorted by nondecreasing `list_degree` using `mergeSort`. First show `q,r ‚ąą L` by rewriting membership through `List.mergeSort_perm` and `Finset.mem_toList`. Next obtain that `L` is pairwise nondecreasing in `list_degree` from `pairwise_mergeSort`. A residual step `q ‚Üí r` gives `list_degree r.1 < list_degree q.1` by `atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt`, so `q ‚Č† r`. If `r` were not strictly earlier than `q`, then `idxOf q < idxOf r`; applying pairwise sortedness to those two positions yields `list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree r.1`, contradicting the strict degree drop.
op_000115.proof_text:
intro p R hR q r hqR hrR hstep
classical
let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)
have hqL : q ‚ąą L := by
  dsimp [L]
  rw [List.Perm.mem_iff (List.mergeSort_perm _ _)]
  exact Finset.mem_toList.mpr hqR
have hrL : r ‚ąą L := by
  dsimp [L]
  rw [List.Perm.mem_iff (List.mergeSort_perm _ _)]
  exact Finset.mem_toList.mpr hrR
have hpair : L.Pairwise (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1) := by
  simpa [L] using
    (R.toList).pairwise_mergeSort
      (le := fun a b => decide (list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1))
      (fun a b c => by
        simpa using
          (Nat.le_trans :
            list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1 ‚Üí
            list_degree b.1 ‚Č§ list_degree c.1 ‚Üí
            list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree c.1))
      (by
        intro a b
        simpa using (Nat.le_total (list_degree a.1) (list_degree b.1)))
have hdeg : list_degree r.1 < list_degree q.1 :=
  atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep
have hqr_ne : q ‚Č† r := by
  intro hEq
  simp [hEq] at hdeg
have hiq : L.idxOf q < L.length := List.idxOf_lt_length_of_mem hqL
have hir : L.idxOf r < L.length := List.idxOf_lt_length_of_mem hrL
by_contra hnot
have hidx_ne : L.idxOf q ‚Č† L.idxOf r := by
  intro hidx
  exact hqr_ne ((List.idxOf_inj (l := L) (x := q) (y := r) hqL).mp hidx)
have hlt_qr : L.idxOf q < L.idxOf r :=
  lt_of_le_of_ne (Nat.le_of_not_gt hnot) hidx_ne
have hle_deg : list_degree q.1 ‚Č§ list_degree r.1 := by
  have h := List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_lt hpair
    (a := ‚ü®L.idxOf q, hiq‚ü©) (b := ‚ü®L.idxOf r, hir‚ü©) hlt_qr
  simpa using h
exact (not_le_of_gt hdeg) hle_deg

op_000096.source_statement: For every őď : List Tp and s : String, (¬¨ ‚ąÉ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep (őď, s) q) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őď, is_atom B.
op_000096.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ (őď : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp) (s : String), (¬¨ ‚ąÉ q : AutomatedTheoryConstruction.AtomicResidualState, AutomatedTheoryConstruction.AtomicResidualGraphStep (őď, s) q) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ B ‚ąą őď, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.is_atom B
op_000096.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized q explicitly as an AtomicResidualState and kept the state‚Äôs string component fixed at s, matching the graph-step constructors.

op_000096.proof_sketch: Take őď = [#"a" ‚ßĻ #"b"] and s = "a". Any residual step from (őď, s) would have to be an `ldiv` step coming from the unique candidate in this singleton context, which requires a derivation `[] ‚áí #"a"`; this contradicts `nonempty_premises`. Hence the left side holds. But the right side would force `is_atom (#"a" ‚ßĻ #"b")`, which simplifies to `False`.
op_000096.proof_text:
intro h
let X : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp :=
  Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv
    (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a")
    (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
have hiff := h [X] "a"
have hnostep : ¬¨ ‚ąÉ q : AutomatedTheoryConstruction.AtomicResidualState,
    AutomatedTheoryConstruction.AtomicResidualGraphStep ([X], "a") q := by
  intro hs
  rcases hs with ‚ü®q, hq‚ü©
  cases hq with
  | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg =>
      simp [X, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.candidates,
        Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.picks, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.splits] at hc
  | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg =>
      simp [X, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.candidates,
        Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.picks, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.splits] at hc
      rcases hc with ‚ü®rfl, rfl, rfl, rfl, rfl‚ü©
      exact (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.nonempty_premises harg) rfl
have hatom : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.is_atom X := (hiff.mp hnostep) _ (by simp [X])
simp [X, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.is_atom] at hatom
op_000096.counterexample_text: A counterexample is őď = [# "a" ‚ßĻ # "b"] with s = "a". There is no outgoing `AtomicResidualGraphStep` from this state, but the unique element of őď is not atomic.

op_000113.source_statement: For every p q : AtomicResidualState, if Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q and ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q, then there exists r : AtomicResidualState such that Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r, ¬¨ ‚ąÉ s, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s, and ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r.
op_000113.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p q : AtomicResidualState,
  Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí
  ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q ‚Üí
  ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
    Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß
    (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚ąß
    ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r
op_000113.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized terminality as `¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s`, matching the statement‚Äôs ‚Äėno one-step successor‚Äô clause.

op_000113.proof_sketch: Let S be the rejecting states reachable from q. Since q is rejecting, S is nonempty. Apply thm_atomic_residual_step_wellfounded_000078 to get a minimal r in S for the reverse-step relation. Any successor s of r is still reachable from q and, by thm_residual_rejects_local_iff_000094, is also rejecting, so s ‚ąą S. This contradicts minimality, hence r has no successors.
op_000113.proof_text:
intro p q hpq hqrej
let S : Set AtomicResidualState :=
  {r : AtomicResidualState |
    Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß
      ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r}
have hqS : q ‚ąą S := by
  exact ‚ü®Relation.ReflTransGen.refl, hqrej‚ü©
obtain ‚ü®r, hrS, hmin‚ü© :=
  thm_atomic_residual_step_wellfounded_000078.has_min S ‚ü®q, hqS‚ü©
rcases hrS with ‚ü®hqr, hrrej‚ü©
refine ‚ü®r, hqr, ?_, hrrej‚ü©
intro hsucc
rcases hsucc with ‚ü®s, hrs‚ü©
have hlocal :
    r ‚Č† ([# (r.2)], r.2) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ t : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r t ‚Üí ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts t :=
  (thm_residual_rejects_local_iff_000094 r).mp hrrej
have hsS : s ‚ąą S := by
  exact ‚ü®Relation.ReflTransGen.tail hqr hrs, hlocal.2 s hrs‚ü©
exact hmin s hsS hrs

op_000118.source_statement: For every `őď : List Tp` and `s : String`, `¬¨ ‚ąÉ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep (őď, s) q` iff every right-division candidate `Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ ‚ąą candidates őď` has underivable argument context `¬¨ (őĒ ‚áí A)` and every left-division candidate `Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R ‚ąą candidates őď` has underivable argument context `¬¨ (őĒ ‚áí A)`.
op_000118.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ (őď : List Tp) (s : String),
  (¬¨ ‚ąÉ q : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep (őď, s) q) ‚ÜĒ
    ((‚ąÄ (L őĒ őõ : List Tp) (A B : Tp),
        Cand.rdiv L B A őĒ őõ ‚ąą candidates őď ‚Üí ¬¨ (őĒ ‚áí A)) ‚ąß
     ‚ąÄ (őď‚āĀ őĒ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp),
        Cand.ldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ A B R ‚ąą candidates őď ‚Üí ¬¨ (őĒ ‚áí A))
op_000118.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the two candidate clauses as universal implications over constructor-shaped members of `candidates őď`; `s` is quantified as in the statement even though only the left side depends on it.

op_000118.proof_sketch: Unfold the two constructors of `AtomicResidualGraphStep`. A supposed outgoing step is exactly produced by a candidate together with a derivation of its argument context, so `no step` implies both universal negation clauses. Conversely, any step must be either `rdiv` or `ldiv`, and each case is ruled out by the corresponding hypothesis.
op_000118.proof_text:
intro őď s
constructor
¬∑ intro hnostep
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro L őĒ őõ A B hc harg
    exact hnostep ‚ü®(L ++ [B] ++ őõ, s), AtomicResidualGraphStep.rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B s hc harg‚ü©
  ¬∑ intro őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B hc harg
    exact hnostep ‚ü®(őď‚āĀ ++ [B] ++ R, s), AtomicResidualGraphStep.ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B s hc harg‚ü©
¬∑ rintro ‚ü®hrdiv, hldiv‚ü© ‚ü®q, hstep‚ü©
  cases hstep with
  | rdiv _ L őĒ őõ A B _ hc harg =>
      exact hrdiv L őĒ őõ A B hc harg
  | ldiv _ őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B _ hc harg =>
      exact hldiv őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B hc harg

op_000120.source_statement: For every `p : AtomicResidualState`, if `L` is the degree-sorted topological list of the states reachable from `p`, then the concrete one-pass reverse scan along `L` that writes `true` at `q` exactly when `q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)` or some already-scanned successor of `q` is `true` produces a Boolean table agreeing pointwise with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on the reachable region.
op_000120.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
  ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
    (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
        (AtomicResidualReverseScan p (AtomicResidualDegreeSortedTopologicalList R) q = true ‚ÜĒ
          AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
op_000120.statement_formalization_notes: Minimal repair of the previous formalization: the degree-sorted reachable-list helper is marked `noncomputable`, and the statement still packages the exact reachable region via a finset and compares the concrete recursive scan pointwise with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on that region.

op_000120.proof_sketch: Use `thm_degree_sorted_reverse_topology_000115` on the underlying degree-sorted `mergeSort`; after reversing that list, every edge `AtomicResidualGraphStep q r` with `q,r ‚ąą R` becomes `idxOf q < idxOf r`, and the reversed list is still nodup with exactly the elements of `R`. The only missing bridge is a concrete scan lemma: for any nodup list with this edge direction, `AtomicResidualReverseScan` satisfies on each listed state `q` the recursion `scan q = true ‚ÜĒ q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą® ‚ąÉ r, r` is later in the list, `AtomicResidualGraphStep q r`, and `scan r = true`. That lemma is proved by induction on the list: for the head it is exactly the defining equation, and for a tail element the topological-order hypothesis rules out any successor returning to the head, so the induction hypothesis applies unchanged. Now define `good ‚ü®q,hq‚ü© := AtomicResidualReverseScan p (AtomicResidualDegreeSortedTopologicalList R) q`; by the scan lemma this `good` satisfies the recursive clause required by `thm_topological_reverse_sweep_canonical_000112`. Applying `thm_topological_reverse_sweep_canonical_000112` then gives `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q ‚ÜĒ good ‚ü®q,hq‚ü© = true`, which is exactly the target equivalence.

op_000120.source_statement: For every `p : AtomicResidualState`, if `L` is the degree-sorted topological list of the states reachable from `p`, then the concrete one-pass reverse scan along `L` that writes `true` at `q` exactly when `q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)` or some already-scanned successor of `q` is `true` produces a Boolean table agreeing pointwise with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on the reachable region.
op_000120.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState,
  ‚ąÄ L : List AtomicResidualState,
    L.Nodup ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    List.Pairwise (fun q r => list_degree r.1 ‚Č§ list_degree q.1) L ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
      r ‚ąą L ‚Üí
      AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
      List.idxOf q L < List.idxOf r L) ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ q ‚ąą L, atomicResidualReverseScan p L q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q
op_000120.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced an explicit reverse-scan table in the prelude because the repo has no named executable sweep. Interpreted the natural-language list condition as: nodup, exactly the reachable states from p, descending by list_degree, and every graph step goes to a later list position.

op_000120.proof_sketch: Prove a stronger auxiliary lemma by induction on the list: on any nodup successor-closed topological list, the executable reverse scan agrees with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` at every listed state. Successor-closure comes from reachable-state closure under one more `ReflTransGen.tail` step, and the tail case inherits topological order because any edge from the tail back to the head would force an impossible `idxOf` inequality. The head case unfolds the scan definition and uses `thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097`; the tail case is just `Function.update` away from the induction hypothesis.
op_000120.prelude_code:
noncomputable def atomicResidualReverseScan (p : AtomicResidualState) (L : List AtomicResidualState) :
    AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool := by
  classical
  exact
    L.foldr
      (fun q good =>
        Function.update good q
          (decide (q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
            ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß good r = true)))
      (fun _ => false)
op_000120.proof_text:
intro p L hLnodup hReach _ hTop q hq
classical
have hreach_mem :
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą L ‚Üí Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q := by
  intro q hqL
  exact (hReach q).mp hqL
have hclosed :
    ‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
      AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
      r ‚ąą L := by
  intro q r hqL hstep
  exact (hReach r).mpr (Relation.ReflTransGen.tail ((hReach q).mp hqL) hstep)
have hscan :
    ‚ąÄ L : List AtomicResidualState,
      L.Nodup ‚Üí
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
        Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
      (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
        AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
        r ‚ąą L) ‚Üí
      (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
        r ‚ąą L ‚Üí
        AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
        List.idxOf q L < List.idxOf r L) ‚Üí
      ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
        atomicResidualReverseScan p L q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q := by
  intro L
  induction L with
  | nil =>
      intro hnodup hmem hclosed htop q hq
      cases hq
  | cons a t ih =>
      intro hnodup hmem hclosed htop q hq
      have hcons : (a ‚ąČ t) ‚ąß t.Nodup := List.nodup_cons.mp hnodup
      have hnotin : a ‚ąČ t := hcons.1
      have hnodup_t : t.Nodup := hcons.2
      have hmem_t :
          ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
            q ‚ąą t ‚Üí Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q := by
        intro q hq
        exact hmem q (by simp [hq])
      have hclosed_t :
          ‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
            q ‚ąą t ‚Üí
            AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
            r ‚ąą t := by
        intro q r hq hr
        have hr_mem : r ‚ąą a :: t := hclosed q r (by simp [hq]) hr
        rcases hr_mem with rfl | hr_mem
        ¬∑ have hqa : q ‚Č† a := by
            intro hqa
            exact hnotin (hqa ‚Ėł hq)
          have hlt : List.idxOf q (a :: t) < List.idxOf a (a :: t) :=
            htop q a (by simp [hq]) (by simp) hr
          have : False := by
            simpa [List.idxOf_cons_ne _ hqa.symm] using hlt
          exact False.elim this
        ¬∑ exact hr_mem
      have htop_t :
          ‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
            q ‚ąą t ‚Üí
            r ‚ąą t ‚Üí
            AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
            List.idxOf q t < List.idxOf r t := by
        intro q r hq hr hstep
        have hqa : q ‚Č† a := by
          intro hqa
          exact hnotin (hqa ‚Ėł hq)
        have hra : r ‚Č† a := by
          intro hra
          exact hnotin (hra ‚Ėł hr)
        have hlt : List.idxOf q (a :: t) < List.idxOf r (a :: t) :=
          htop q r (by simp [hq]) (by simp [hr]) hstep
        simpa [List.idxOf_cons_ne _ hqa.symm, List.idxOf_cons_ne _ hra.symm] using hlt
      rcases List.mem_cons.mp hq with rfl | hq
      ¬∑ rw [(thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097 a).symm]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro hscan_a
          have hscan_a' :
              a = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
                ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
                  AtomicResidualGraphStep a r ‚ąß
                  atomicResidualReverseScan p t r = true := by
            simpa [atomicResidualReverseScan, Function.update] using hscan_a
          rcases hscan_a' with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, hrscan‚ü©
          ¬∑ exact Or.inl hbase
          ¬∑ have hr_mem : r ‚ąą a :: t := hclosed a r (by simp) hstep
            rcases hr_mem with rfl | hr_mem
            ¬∑ have hlt : List.idxOf a (a :: t) < List.idxOf a (a :: t) :=
                htop a a (by simp) (by simp) hstep
              have : False := by
                simpa using hlt
              exact False.elim this
            ¬∑ exact Or.inr ‚ü®r, hstep, (ih hnodup_t hmem_t hclosed_t htop_t r hr_mem).mp hrscan‚ü©
        ¬∑ intro hacc
          rcases hacc with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, hracc‚ü©
          ¬∑ have :
              a = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
                ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
                  AtomicResidualGraphStep a r ‚ąß
                  atomicResidualReverseScan p t r = true :=
              Or.inl hbase
            simpa [atomicResidualReverseScan, Function.update] using this
          ¬∑ have hr_mem : r ‚ąą a :: t := hclosed a r (by simp) hstep
            rcases hr_mem with rfl | hr_mem
            ¬∑ have hlt : List.idxOf a (a :: t) < List.idxOf a (a :: t) :=
                htop a a (by simp) (by simp) hstep
              have : False := by
                simpa using hlt
              exact False.elim this
            ¬∑ have :
                a = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
                  ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
                    AtomicResidualGraphStep a r ‚ąß
                    atomicResidualReverseScan p t r = true := by
                right
                exact ‚ü®r, hstep, (ih hnodup_t hmem_t hclosed_t htop_t r hr_mem).mpr hracc‚ü©
              simpa [atomicResidualReverseScan, Function.update] using this
      ¬∑ have hqa : q ‚Č† a := by
          intro hqa
          exact hnotin (hqa ‚Ėł hq)
        have htail_eq :
            atomicResidualReverseScan p (a :: t) q = atomicResidualReverseScan p t q := by
          simp [atomicResidualReverseScan, Function.update, hqa]
        rw [htail_eq]
        exact ih hnodup_t hmem_t hclosed_t htop_t q hq
exact hscan L hLnodup hreach_mem hclosed hTop q hq

op_000122.source_statement: For every `q : AtomicResidualState`, `¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q` iff every terminal state `r` reachable from `q` satisfies `¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r`, where terminal means `¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s`.
op_000122.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ r : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚Üí ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r
op_000122.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized ‚Äúreachable from q‚ÄĚ as `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r` and inlined the terminal condition as `¬¨ ‚ąÉ s, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s`.

op_000122.proof_sketch: Forward: acceptance propagates backward along any reachable path by `Relation.ReflTransGen.head_induction_on` and `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step`, so a rejecting start state forces every reachable terminal state to reject. Backward: if `q` accepted, then by `thm_accepts_iff_reaches_base_000099` it reaches `([# (q.2)], q.2)`; that base state has no outgoing step because `candidates [# (q.2)]` is empty, so the hypothesis applied there contradicts `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base`.
op_000122.proof_text:
intro q
constructor
¬∑ intro hq r hqr _ hracc
  apply hq
  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.head_induction_on hqr hracc
    (fun hstep _ hacc => AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step hstep hacc)
¬∑ intro hterm hqacc
  have hqbase : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q ([# (q.2)], q.2) :=
    (thm_accepts_iff_reaches_base_000099 q).1 hqacc
  have hbaseTerminal : ¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep ([# (q.2)], q.2) s := by
    rintro ‚ü®s, hstep‚ü©
    cases hstep with
    | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B t hc harg =>
        simp [candidates, picks] at hc
    | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B t hc harg =>
        simp [candidates, picks] at hc
  have hbaseReject : ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts ([# (q.2)], q.2) :=
    hterm ([# (q.2)], q.2) hqbase hbaseTerminal
  exact hbaseReject (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base (q.2))

op_000124.source_statement: There exist `q r : AtomicResidualState` such that `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q`, `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r`, `¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s`, and `¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r`.
op_000124.formalized_statement: ‚ąÉ q : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q ‚ąß Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚ąß ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r
op_000124.statement_formalization_notes: Direct existential formalization of the statement using the repository's atomic residual graph predicates and explicit nested existentials.

op_000124.proof_sketch: Witness with A := (# "a" ‚ßĻ # "a"), q := ([A, A, A ‚ßĻ # "a"], "a"), and r := ([A, # "a"], "a"). There are explicit `ldiv` steps from q to r and from q to the base state `([# "a"], "a")`, so q accepts. The state r has no successors because its only possible candidate requires an impossible empty-premise derivation, and then `thm_residual_rejects_local_iff_000094` gives `¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r`.
op_000124.proof_text:
let A : Tp := # "a" ‚ßĻ # "a"
let q : AtomicResidualState := ([A, A, A ‚ßĻ # "a"], "a")
let r : AtomicResidualState := ([A, # "a"], "a")
have hA : [A] ‚áí A := by
  exact Sequent.ax
have hAA : [A, A] ‚áí A := by
  have h1 : [# "a", A] ‚áí # "a" := by
    exact Sequent.ldiv_l
      (őĒ := [# "a"]) (A := # "a") (őď := []) (B := # "a") (őõ := [])
      Sequent.ax Sequent.ax
  have h3 : [# "a", A, A] ‚áí # "a" := by
    simpa [List.append_assoc] using
      (Sequent.ldiv_l
        (őĒ := [# "a"]) (A := # "a") (őď := []) (B := # "a") (őõ := [A])
        Sequent.ax h1)
  exact Sequent.ldiv_r (őď := [A, A]) (A := # "a") (B := # "a") (by simp) h3
have hstep_qr : AtomicResidualGraphStep q r := by
  dsimp [q, r]
  refine AtomicResidualGraphStep.ldiv [A, A, A ‚ßĻ # "a"] [A] [A] [] A (# "a") "a" ?_ ?_
  ¬∑ simpa [A] using candidates_ldiv_mem [A] [A] ([] : List Tp) A (# "a")
  ¬∑ simpa using hA
have hstep_qbase : AtomicResidualGraphStep q ([# "a"], "a") := by
  dsimp [q]
  refine AtomicResidualGraphStep.ldiv [A, A, A ‚ßĻ # "a"] [] [A, A] [] A (# "a") "a" ?_ ?_
  ¬∑ simpa [A] using candidates_ldiv_mem ([] : List Tp) [A, A] ([] : List Tp) A (# "a")
  ¬∑ simpa using hAA
have hqacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q := by
  exact AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step hstep_qbase (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base "a")
have hnostep_r : ¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s := by
  intro hs
  rcases hs with ‚ü®s, hstep‚ü©
  dsimp [r, A] at hstep
  cases hstep with
  | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A' B' t hc harg =>
      simp [candidates, picks, splits] at hc
  | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A' B' t hc harg =>
      simp [candidates, picks, splits] at hc
      rcases hc with ‚ü®rfl, rfl, rfl, rfl, rfl‚ü©
      exact (nonempty_premises harg) rfl
have hrrej : ¬¨ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r := by
  refine (thm_residual_rejects_local_iff_000094 r).2 ?_
  constructor
  ¬∑ dsimp [r, A]
    intro h
    cases h
  ¬∑ intro s hstep
    exact False.elim (hnostep_r ‚ü®s, hstep‚ü©)
refine ‚ü®q, r, hqacc, ?_, hnostep_r, hrrej‚ü©
exact Relation.ReflTransGen.head hstep_qr Relation.ReflTransGen.refl

op_000130.source_statement: ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState, (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1); ‚ąÉ! good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool, (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąČ R ‚Üí good q = false) ‚ąß (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą® ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, r ‚ąą R ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß List.idxOf r L < List.idxOf q L ‚ąß good r = true)) ‚ąß ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
op_000130.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)
    ‚ąÉ! good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool,
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąČ R ‚Üí good q = false) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
          (good q = true ‚ÜĒ
            q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
              ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
                r ‚ąą R ‚ąß
                AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß
                List.idxOf r L < List.idxOf q L ‚ąß
                good r = true)) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q))
op_000130.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the stated total Bool labeling over the degree-sorted reachable finset, with outside-of-R values forced to false.

op_000130.proof_sketch: Define `good q := if q ‚ąą R then decide (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q) else false`. Outside `R` this is forced to `false`; on `R`, acceptance is exactly `good q = true`. The recursive clause comes from `thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097`, reachability-closure of successors, and `thm_degree_sorted_reverse_topology_000115`. Uniqueness is pointwise from the outside-`R` clause and the acceptance equivalence on `R`.
op_000130.proof_text:
intro p R hR
classical
dsimp
have hsame :
    ‚ąÄ {q : AtomicResidualState},
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí q.2 = p.2
¬∑ intro q hq
  induction hq with
  | refl =>
      rfl
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      cases hstep <;> simpa using ih
let good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool := fun q =>
  if q ‚ąą R then decide (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q) else false
have hgood_out : ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąČ R ‚Üí good q = false
¬∑ intro q hq
  simp [good, hq]
have hgood_acc :
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
¬∑ intro q hq
  simp [good, hq]
have hgood_rec :
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
        (good q = true ‚ÜĒ
          q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
            ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
              r ‚ąą R ‚ąß
              AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß
              List.idxOf r (R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)) <
                List.idxOf q (R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)) ‚ąß
              good r = true)
¬∑ intro q hqR
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro hgoodq
    have hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q := (hgood_acc q hqR).mp hgoodq
    rcases (thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097 q).mp hacc with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, hracc‚ü©
    ¬∑ left
      have hs : q.2 = p.2 := hsame ((hR q).mp hqR)
      simpa [hs] using hbase
    ¬∑ right
      have hreachq : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q := (hR q).mp hqR
      have hrR : r ‚ąą R := (hR r).mpr (Relation.ReflTransGen.tail hreachq hstep)
      refine ‚ü®r, hrR, hstep, ?_, (hgood_acc r hrR).mpr hracc‚ü©
      simpa using (thm_degree_sorted_reverse_topology_000115 p R hR q r hqR hrR hstep)
  ¬∑ intro h
    rcases h with hbase | ‚ü®r, hrR, hstep, _, hgoodr‚ü©
    ¬∑ have hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q
      ¬∑ simpa [hbase] using (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base (p.2))
      exact (hgood_acc q hqR).mpr hacc
    ¬∑ have hracc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r := (hgood_acc r hrR).mp hgoodr
      have hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q := AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step hstep hracc
      exact (hgood_acc q hqR).mpr hacc
refine ‚ü®good, ‚ü®hgood_out, hgood_rec, hgood_acc‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
intro g hg
rcases hg with ‚ü®hg_out, hg_rec, hg_acc‚ü©
funext q
by_cases hqR : q ‚ąą R
¬∑ have hiff : g q = true ‚ÜĒ good q = true
  ¬∑ constructor
    ¬∑ intro hgtrue
      exact (hgood_acc q hqR).mpr ((hg_acc q hqR).mp hgtrue)
    ¬∑ intro hgoodtrue
      exact (hg_acc q hqR).mpr ((hgood_acc q hqR).mp hgoodtrue)
  cases hgq : g q <;> cases hgoodq : good q <;> simp [hgq, hgoodq] at hiff ‚äĘ
¬∑ have hgfalse : g q = false := hg_out q hqR
  have hgoodfalse : good q = false := hgood_out q hqR
  rw [hgfalse, hgoodfalse]

op_000133.source_statement: ‚ąÄ r : AtomicResidualState, (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚Üí (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r ‚ÜĒ r = ([# (r.2)], r.2))
op_000133.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ r : AtomicResidualState, (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚Üí (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r ‚ÜĒ r = ([# (r.2)], r.2))
op_000133.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a precise Lean proposition, so I preserved it verbatim.

op_000133.proof_sketch: Destructure the residual state as `(őď, t)`. For the forward direction, apply `thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097 (őď, t)`; the step case contradicts terminality, so only the base-state equality remains. For the reverse direction, rewrite by the equality and use `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base t`.
op_000133.proof_text:
rintro ‚ü®őď, t‚ü© hterminal
constructor
¬∑ intro hacc
  rcases (thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097 (őď, t)).1 hacc with hbase | ‚ü®q, hstep, _‚ü©
  ¬∑ exact hbase
  ¬∑ exact False.elim (hterminal ‚ü®q, hstep‚ü©)
¬∑ intro hbase
  simpa [hbase] using (AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base t)

op_000131.source_statement: ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p r ‚ąß (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r
op_000131.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p r ‚ąß (¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep r s) ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphAccepts r
op_000131.statement_formalization_notes: The natural-language target is already a precise Lean proposition using existing repository names.

op_000131.proof_sketch: Forward: use `thm_accepts_iff_reaches_base_000099` and witness the atomic base state, which has no outgoing step by case analysis on `AtomicResidualGraphStep`. Reverse: from a reachable terminal accepting `r`, `thm_terminal_accepts_singleton_atom_000133` shows `r` is the singleton base state, reachability preserves the string component, and then `thm_accepts_iff_reaches_base_000099` gives acceptance of `p`.
op_000131.proof_text:
intro p
constructor
¬∑ intro hp
  refine ‚ü®([# (p.2)], p.2), (thm_accepts_iff_reaches_base_000099 p).1 hp, ?_, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base (p.2)‚ü©
  rintro ‚ü®s, hstep‚ü©
  cases hstep with
  | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B t hc harg =>
      simp [candidates, picks] at hc
  | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B t hc harg =>
      simp [candidates, picks] at hc
¬∑ rintro ‚ü®r, hpr, hterminal, hracc‚ü©
  have hsame :
      ‚ąÄ {q : AtomicResidualState},
        Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí q.2 = p.2 := by
    intro q hq
    induction hq with
    | refl =>
        rfl
    | tail hreach hstep ih =>
        cases hstep <;> simpa using ih
  have hs : r.2 = p.2 := hsame hpr
  have hrbase : r = ([# (r.2)], r.2) :=
    (thm_terminal_accepts_singleton_atom_000133 r hterminal).1 hracc
  have hpbase' : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p ([# (r.2)], r.2) := by
    rw [hrbase] at hpr
    exact hpr
  have hpbase : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p ([# (p.2)], p.2) := by
    simpa [hs] using hpbase'
  exact (thm_accepts_iff_reaches_base_000099 p).2 hpbase

op_000135.source_statement: For every `p : AtomicResidualState` and reachable-region finset `R` as above, if `L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)`, then the canonical label on `R` is computed by a single reverse sweep over `L`: start from the all-`false` function and, when processing `q`, set `q` to `true` exactly when `q = ([#(p.2)], p.2)` or some already-processed one-step successor of `q` is `true`; the resulting Boolean function agrees with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on `R`.
op_000135.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)
    ‚ąÉ! good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool,
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąČ R ‚Üí good q = false) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
          (good q = true ‚ÜĒ
            q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
              ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState,
                r ‚ąą R ‚ąß
                AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß
                List.idxOf r L < List.idxOf q L ‚ąß
                good r = true)) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q))
op_000135.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the reverse sweep using the existing degree-sorted `mergeSort` order and `List.idxOf` to encode already-processed successors; the all-false initialization is represented by `good q = false` off `R`.

op_000135.proof_sketch: Reuse the already verified theorem `AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_degree_sorted_unique_labeling_000130`, which matches the target statement exactly.
op_000135.proof_text:
intro p R hR
simpa using AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_degree_sorted_unique_labeling_000130 p R hR

op_000137.source_statement: For every root state p and every reverse topological ordering L of the exact reachable states from p, the reverse sweep that labels a terminal state q as true exactly when q = ([# (q.2)], q.2), and otherwise labels q as true exactly when some AtomicResidualGraphStep-successor of q already has label true, agrees with AtomicResidualGraphAccepts on every state reachable from p.
op_000137.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ L : List AtomicResidualState,
  L.Nodup ‚Üí
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
  (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
    q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
    r ‚ąą L ‚Üí
    AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
    List.idxOf q L < List.idxOf r L) ‚Üí
  ‚ąÉ! good : {q // q ‚ąą L} ‚Üí Bool,
    (‚ąÄ q : {q // q ‚ąą L},
      good q = true ‚ÜĒ
        (((¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q.1 s) ‚ąß
            q.1 = ([# (q.1.2)], q.1.2)) ‚ą®
          ‚ąÉ r : {r // r ‚ąą L},
            AtomicResidualGraphStep q.1 r.1 ‚ąß
            List.idxOf q.1 L < List.idxOf r.1 L ‚ąß
            good r = true)) ‚ąß
    ‚ąÄ q : {q // q ‚ąą L},
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.1 ‚ÜĒ good q = true
op_000137.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the sweep as a unique Boolean labeling on the listed exact reachable states, with terminal states handled by the singleton-atom base test and nonterminal states by a later-successor recursion along the chosen reverse topological order.

op_000137.proof_sketch: Let `R := L.toFinset`; since `L.Nodup`, membership in `L` and membership in `R` are equivalent, so the reachability hypothesis gives the exact reachable-region predicate needed to apply `thm_topological_reverse_sweep_canonical_000112`. That theorem yields a unique `good : {q // q ‚ąą R} ‚Üí Bool` agreeing with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` and satisfying the reverse-sweep rule with base clause `q.1 = ([# (p.2)], p.2)` plus propagation along one-step successors. For reachable states, residual steps preserve the second component, so by induction on `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q` we get `q.1.2 = p.2`; hence `q.1 = ([# (p.2)], p.2)` is equivalent to `q.1 = ([# (q.1.2)], q.1.2)`. Also, any successor of a reachable `q` is again reachable and therefore lies in `L`, so the given topological-order hypothesis makes the explicit `List.idxOf q.1 L < List.idxOf r.1 L` side condition automatic for successor witnesses. Finally, the singleton atom state has no outgoing residual step by inspection of `AtomicResidualGraphStep`, and `thm_terminal_accepts_singleton_atom_000133` identifies terminal accepting states with exactly that singleton form, so the local rule rewrites to the target terminal-or-successor clause without changing the unique labeling.

op_000137.source_statement: For every root state p and every reverse topological ordering L of the exact reachable states from p, the reverse sweep that labels a terminal state q as true exactly when q = ([# (q.2)], q.2), and otherwise labels q as true exactly when some AtomicResidualGraphStep-successor of q already has label true, agrees with AtomicResidualGraphAccepts on every state reachable from p.
op_000137.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ L : List AtomicResidualState, L.Nodup ‚Üí (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚Üí r ‚ąą L ‚Üí AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí List.idxOf q L < List.idxOf r L) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool, (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚Üí ((¬¨ ‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q s) ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ q = ([# (q.2)], q.2))) ‚ąß ((‚ąÉ s : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q s) ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, r ‚ąą L ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß List.idxOf q L < List.idxOf r L ‚ąß good r = true))) ‚ąß (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q))
op_000137.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized exact reachability by list membership, reverse-topological order by `List.idxOf`, and split the update rule into terminal and nonterminal cases.

op_000137.proof_sketch: Take the canonical reachable-region Boolean labeling from `thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106`, transport its reachable finset to the given list via the exact-reachability hypothesis, and extend the subtype label to a total function on states. Then split the canonical base-or-successor recurrence into terminal and nonterminal cases using same-atom reachability invariance and the fact that `([# s], s)` has no outgoing residual step.
op_000137.proof_text:
intro p L hLnodup hmem htop
classical
have hsame :
    ‚ąÄ {q : AtomicResidualState},
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí q.2 = p.2 := by
  intro q hq
  induction hq with
  | refl =>
      rfl
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      cases hstep <;> simpa using ih
have hbase_terminal :
    ‚ąÄ s : String, ¬¨ ‚ąÉ q : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep ([# s], s) q := by
  intro s
  rintro ‚ü®q, hstep‚ü©
  cases hstep with
  | rdiv őď L őĒ őõ A B t hc harg =>
      simp [candidates, picks] at hc
  | ldiv őď őď‚āĀ őĒ R A B t hc harg =>
      simp [candidates, picks] at hc
rcases thm_unique_reachable_bool_labeling_000106 p with ‚ü®R, hR, ‚ü®good0, hgood0, _‚ü©‚ü©
rcases hgood0 with ‚ü®hgood0_rec, hgood0_acc‚ü©
have hLR : ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚ÜĒ q ‚ąą R := by
  intro q
  rw [hmem q, hR q]
let good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool := fun q =>
  if hqL : q ‚ąą L then good0 ‚ü®q, (hLR q).mp hqL‚ü© else false
refine ‚ü®good, ?_, ?_‚ü©
¬∑ intro q hqL
  have hqR : q ‚ąą R := (hLR q).mp hqL
  have hreachq : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q := (hmem q).mp hqL
  let qR : {q // q ‚ąą R} := ‚ü®q, hqR‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ intro hterminal
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hgoodq
      have hgood0q : good0 qR = true := by
        simpa [good, hqL, qR] using hgoodq
      rcases (hgood0_rec qR).mp hgood0q with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, _‚ü©
      ¬∑ simpa [hsame hreachq] using hbase
      ¬∑ exact False.elim (hterminal ‚ü®r.1, hstep‚ü©)
    ¬∑ intro hbase
      have hbase' : q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) := by
        simpa [hsame hreachq] using hbase
      have hgood0q : good0 qR = true := (hgood0_rec qR).mpr (Or.inl hbase')

    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hgoodq
      have hgood0q : good0 qR = true := by

      rcases (hgood0_rec qR).mp hgood0q with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, hgood0r‚ü©
      ¬∑ cases hbase
        exact False.elim (hbase_terminal (p.2) hsucc)

        simpa [good, (hLR r.1).mpr r.2] using hgood0r
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®r, hrL, hstep, _, hgoodr‚ü©
      have hrR : r ‚ąą R := (hLR r).mp hrL
      have hgood0r : good0 ‚ü®r, hrR‚ü© = true := by
        simpa [good, hrL] using hgoodr

        exact (hgood0_rec qR).mpr (Or.inr ‚ü®‚ü®r, hrR‚ü©, hstep, hgood0r‚ü©)
      simpa [good, hqL, qR] using hgood0q
¬∑ intro q hqL
  have hqR : q ‚ąą R := (hLR q).mp hqL
  let qR : {q // q ‚ąą R} := ‚ü®q, hqR‚ü©

op_000138.source_statement: For every `p : AtomicResidualState` and exact reachable finset `R`, if `L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)`, then the label function obtained by an explicit `List.foldr` over `L` that starts from the all-`false` function and updates each `q` to `true` exactly when `q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)` or some `AtomicResidualGraphStep`-successor of `q` is already labeled `true` agrees with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on every `q ‚ąą R`.
op_000138.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)
    let good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool :=
      List.foldr
        (fun q good r =>
          if r = q then
            decide (q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)) ||
              L.any (fun s => @decide (AtomicResidualGraphStep q s) ((Classical.decRel AtomicResidualGraphStep) q s) && good s)
          else
            good r)
        (fun _ => false)
        L
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
op_000138.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized ‚Äúexact reachable finset‚ÄĚ as `‚ąÄ q, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q` and used an explicit `List.foldr` Bool updater over the degree-sorted list.

op_000138.proof_sketch: The statement is false: `thm_degree_sorted_reverse_topology_000115` says every edge `q -> r` in the reachable region satisfies `idxOf r < idxOf q` in the degree-sorted list, but the stated `List.foldr` over that same list evaluates `q` before those earlier successors have been written. So any accepting non-base state with a direct edge to the base state is mislabeled `false`.
op_000138.counterexample_text: Take `p := ([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q")` and let `R := {([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q"), ([# "q"], "q")}`. This is the exact reachable set from `p`: there is an `AtomicResidualGraphStep.ldiv` from `p` to `([# "q"], "q")` using the derivation `[# "p"] ‚áí # "p"`, and `([# "q"], "q")` is terminal. Hence `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p` holds, because `p` steps to the base-accepting state `([# "q"], "q")`. Now `R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)` orders these two states as `L = [([# "q"], "q"), ([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q")]`, since the list degrees are `1` and `4`. The stated `List.foldr` processes the root state `([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q")` first. At that moment the accumulator is still the all-`false` function, so although `L.any` sees the successor `([# "q"], "q")`, the test `good ([# "q"], "q")` is still `false`; therefore the fold sets `good p = false`. Only afterward does the fold process `([# "q"], "q")` and set that base state to `true`. Thus the final function satisfies `good p = false` while `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p` is true, contradicting the target equivalence.

op_000138.source_statement: For every `p : AtomicResidualState` and exact reachable finset `R`, if `L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)`, then the label function obtained by an explicit `List.foldr` over `L` that starts from the all-`false` function and updates each `q` to `true` exactly when `q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)` or some `AtomicResidualGraphStep`-successor of `q` is already labeled `true` agrees with `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on every `q ‚ąą R`.
op_000138.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    let L := R.toList.mergeSort (fun a b => list_degree a.1 ‚Č§ list_degree b.1)
    let good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool :=
      List.foldr
        (fun q good r =>
          if r = q then
            decide (q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)) ||
              L.any (fun s => @decide (AtomicResidualGraphStep q s) ((Classical.decRel AtomicResidualGraphStep) q s) && good s)
          else
            good r)
        (fun _ => false)
        L
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
op_000138.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the statement directly as an executable fold over the degree-sorted reachable list; the successor test is encoded by `L.any` together with the accumulated Boolean label function.

op_000138.proof_sketch: The claim contradicts the ordering supplied by `thm_degree_sorted_reverse_topology_000115`: whenever `AtomicResidualGraphStep q r` holds inside the reachable set, the successor `r` appears earlier than `q` in the degree-sorted list `L`. The proposed definition uses `List.foldr` on `L`, so when it computes the value for `q` it only has access to labels coming from the suffix of `L`, not from earlier elements. Therefore any non-base state whose acceptance depends on an earlier successor is evaluated too early and is labeled `false`. A concrete instance is `p = ([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q")`, where `p` reaches the base state `([# "q"], "q")`; `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p` is true, but the fold still produces `good p = false`.
op_000138.counterexample_text: Let `p := ([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q")` and let `R` be the reachable set `{([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q"), ([# "q"], "q")}`. There is an `AtomicResidualGraphStep` from `p` to `([# "q"], "q")` by the left-division transition, using the axiom derivation `[# "p"] ‚áí # "p"`, and `([# "q"], "q")` is exactly the base accepting state `([# (p.2)], p.2)`. Hence `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p` holds. But `L` sorts these states by list degree as `([# "q"], "q")` first and `p` second, so `List.foldr` processes `p` before the base state has been marked true. At that moment the accumulator is still all-false, so the successor test fails and the fold sets `good p = false`; only afterward does it set `good ([# "q"], "q") = true`.

op_000143.source_statement: For every root state `p` and every finite set `R` with `q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q`, the subset `{q ‚ąą R | AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q}` is the least subset `S ‚äÜ R` such that `([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą S` whenever that state lies in `R`, and whenever `q ‚ąą R` has some `AtomicResidualGraphStep`-successor in `S`, one also has `q ‚ąą S`.
op_000143.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    let A : Set AtomicResidualState := {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą R ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q}
    A ‚äÜ (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState) ‚ąß
      ((([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą R) ‚Üí ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą A) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
          (‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą A) ‚Üí
            q ‚ąą A) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ S : Set AtomicResidualState,
        S ‚äÜ (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState) ‚Üí
        ((([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą R) ‚Üí ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
        (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
          q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
            (‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
              q ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
        A ‚äÜ S
op_000143.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the finite set as a `Finset` and encoded leastness by subset-minimality among `Set`-subsets of `R`.

op_000143.proof_sketch: Unfold the target set `A`. The first three clauses are immediate from the definition of `A` and the constructors `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base` and `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step`. For minimality, take `q ‚ąą A` and use `thm_reachable_accepts_least_set_000103` on the reachable proof coming from `q ‚ąą R`. Instantiate its least-set characterization with `T := S ‚ą™ (‚ÜĎR)^c`; this makes the theorem‚Äôs unconditional base hypothesis available from the target‚Äôs conditional one, and closure follows from the assumed predecessor-closure of `S` on `R`. Since `q ‚ąą R`, membership `q ‚ąą T` forces `q ‚ąą S`.
op_000143.proof_text:
intro p R hR
dsimp
constructor
¬∑ intro q hqA
  exact hqA.1
constructor
¬∑ intro hpR
  exact ‚ü®hpR, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base (p.2)‚ü©
constructor
¬∑ intro q hqR hsucc
  rcases hsucc with ‚ü®r, hstep, hrA‚ü©
  exact ‚ü®hqR, AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step hstep hrA.2‚ü©
¬∑ intro S _ hSbase hSstep q hqA
  rcases hqA with ‚ü®hqR, hqacc‚ü©
  have hreachq : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q := (hR q).mp hqR
  rcases thm_reachable_accepts_least_set_000103 p with ‚ü®_, hleast‚ü©
  let T : Set AtomicResidualState := {x : AtomicResidualState | x ‚ąą S ‚ą® x ‚ąČ (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState)}
  have hTbase : ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą T := by
    by_cases hpR : ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą R
    ¬∑ left
      exact hSbase hpR
    ¬∑ right
      simpa [T] using hpR
  have hTstep :
      ‚ąÄ a b : AtomicResidualState,
        Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p a ‚Üí
        Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p b ‚Üí
        AtomicResidualGraphStep a b ‚Üí
        b ‚ąą T ‚Üí
        a ‚ąą T := by
    intro a b ha hb hab hbT
    have haR : a ‚ąą R := (hR a).2 ha
    have hbR : b ‚ąą R := (hR b).2 hb
    rcases hbT with hbS | hbnotR
    ¬∑ left
      exact hSstep a haR ‚ü®b, hab, hbS‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact False.elim (hbnotR (by simpa using hbR))
  have hqT : q ‚ąą T := (hleast q hreachq).1 hqacc T hTbase hTstep
  rcases hqT with hqS | hqnotR
  ¬∑ exact hqS
  ¬∑ exact False.elim (hqnotR (by simpa using hqR))

op_000142.source_statement: For every root state `p`, every finite set `R` with `q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q`, and every reverse-topological list `L` enumerating `R`, the explicit `List.foldr` reverse sweep over `L` starting from the all-`false` table computes a Boolean table `good` such that `good q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q` for every `q ‚ąą R`.
op_000142.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
  ‚ąÄ L : List AtomicResidualState,
    L.Nodup ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚ÜĒ q ‚ąą R) ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
      r ‚ąą L ‚Üí
      AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
      List.idxOf q L < List.idxOf r L) ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
        (atomicResidualReverseSweep p L q = true ‚ÜĒ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q)
op_000142.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired the previous statement by moving the concrete `List.foldr` sweep into a prelude helper under `classical`, so the successor test is expressed as a single decidable disjunction instead of branching on `Decidable (‚ąÉ s, AtomicResidualGraphStep q s)`. The proposition keeps the exact reachable finset `R`, the enumeration conditions for `L`, and the reverse-topological index condition.

op_000142.statement_formalization_notes: Moved the concrete foldr sweep into a prelude helper under classical choice so the proposition stays explicit while avoiding the local Decidable-synthesis failure from the previous attempt.

op_000142.proof_sketch: Define `atomicResidualReverseSweep` as the explicit `List.foldr` sweep, then reuse the existing induction on the reverse-topological list `L`: the head case is converted with `thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097`, and the tail case follows because successors of tail elements stay in the tail by the `idxOf` strictness hypothesis.
op_000142.prelude_code:
noncomputable def atomicResidualReverseSweep (p : AtomicResidualState) (L : List AtomicResidualState) :
    AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool := by
  classical
  exact
    List.foldr
      (fun q good t =>
        if t = q then
          decide (q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
            ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß good r = true)
        else
          good t)
      (fun _ => false)
      L

noncomputable def atomicResidualReverseSweep (p : AtomicResidualState) (L : List AtomicResidualState) :
    AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool := by
  classical

    List.foldr
      (fun q good t =>
        if t = q then
          decide (q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ą®
            ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß good r = true)
        else
          good t)
      (fun _ => false)

op_000145.source_statement: For every root state `p`, every finite set `R` with `q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q`, and every reverse-topological list `L` enumerating `R`, if one runs the usual right-to-left Boolean sweep on `L`, then after processing any suffix `U` of `L` the current true states are exactly the least subset of `U` containing `([# (p.2)], p.2)` when that state lies in `U` and closed under predecessors inside `U`.
op_000145.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
  ‚ąÄ L : List AtomicResidualState,
    L.Nodup ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą L ‚ÜĒ q ‚ąą R) ‚Üí
    (‚ąÄ q r : AtomicResidualState,
      q ‚ąą L ‚Üí
      r ‚ąą L ‚Üí
      AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí
      List.idxOf q L < List.idxOf r L) ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ U : List AtomicResidualState,
      (‚ąÉ T : List AtomicResidualState, L = T ++ U) ‚Üí
      let good : AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Bool :=
        List.foldr
          (fun q good r =>
            if r = q then
              decide (q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)) ||
                L.any (fun s =>
                  @decide (AtomicResidualGraphStep q s)
                    ((Classical.decRel AtomicResidualGraphStep) q s) && good s)
            else
              good r)
          (fun _ => false)
          U
      let A : Set AtomicResidualState := {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą U ‚ąß good q = true}
      A ‚äÜ {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą U} ‚ąß
        ((([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą U) ‚Üí ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą A) ‚ąß
        (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
          q ‚ąą U ‚Üí
            (‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, r ‚ąą U ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą A) ‚Üí
              q ‚ąą A) ‚ąß
        ‚ąÄ S : Set AtomicResidualState,
          S ‚äÜ {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą U} ‚Üí
          ((([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą U) ‚Üí ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
          (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
            q ‚ąą U ‚Üí
              (‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, r ‚ąą U ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
                q ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
          A ‚äÜ S
op_000145.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized suffixes as append-decompositions `L = T ++ U` and encoded the sweep by the explicit `List.foldr` Boolean update used in the local scratch theorem pattern.

op_000145.proof_sketch: A plausible route is to show the suffix `U` is successor-closed inside `L`, prove the literal `List.foldr` table on `U` matches `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts` on `U`, and then derive leastness by applying `thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143` to the ambient reachable set with the auxiliary set `S ‚ą™ (R \ U)`. I could not turn that into a defensible Lean script here: the needed bridge from the exact foldr term to the recursive predecessor-closure relation on an arbitrary suffix is not already available in `Derived`, and formalizing the necessary `idxOf`/append/suffix lemmas together with the foldr extensionality argument is substantial. The earlier direct foldr route for `op_000142` also failed because its helper was not actually available, so I avoided repeating that failed path.

op_000145.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced explicit definitions for the base state and the right-to-left Boolean sweep, and represented ‚ÄúU is a suffix of L‚ÄĚ conservatively as `‚ąÉ T, L = T ++ U`. The least-subset clause is stated as a standard minimality property over sets supported on `U`.

op_000145.proof_sketch: The target looks true, and the main reusable backbone is `thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143`: once a suffix `U` is shown to be successor-closed, the accepting states restricted to `U` are again the least predecessor-closed subset of `U`. The real missing bridge is executability, namely proving that the literal function `atomicResidualReverseSweep p U` computes exactly that restricted accepting set. `thm_topological_terminal_sweep_accepts_000137` only gives existence of some Boolean labeling on the whole list `L`, not identification of the explicit fold on an arbitrary suffix, and the local bridge theorem `thm_foldr_reverse_sweep_accepts_000142` in `Scratch.loop.lean` does not currently typecheck. Without a verified prefix-insensitivity lemma for the fold together with the suffix-closure transfer, I cannot justify the formal target from the current verified library.
op_000145.counterexample_text: Concrete test candidate: take `p := ([# "p", # "p" ‚ßĻ # "q"], "q")`, which has a one-step `AtomicResidualGraphStep` to the base state `([# "q"], "q")`. Let `L` be a reverse-topological list of those two reachable states, and test the suffixes `U = L` and `U = [([# "q"], "q")]`; this is the smallest nontrivial chain, so any mistake in the suffix-closure argument or in the claim that prefix processing leaves suffix values unchanged should show up here first. I do not claim this refutes the theorem, only that it is the right concrete model to probe the missing bridge.

op_000144.source_statement: For every root state `p` and every finite set `R` with `q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q`, if `S0` is the singleton set `{([# (p.2)], p.2)}` intersected with `R` and `S_{n+1}` is obtained from `S_n` by adding every `q ‚ąą R` having an `AtomicResidualGraphStep`-successor in `S_n`, then `S_{|R|} = {q ‚ąą R | AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q}`.
op_000144.statement_formalization_notes: Encoded the sequence `S_n` by `Nat.iterate` of the predecessor-closure operator on `Set AtomicResidualState`, starting from the singleton base state intersected with `R`.

op_000144.proof_sketch: Let `A := {q | q ‚ąą R ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q}`. By `thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143`, `A` is contained in `R`, contains the base state `([# (p.2)], p.2)` whenever that state lies in `R`, is closed under the predecessor rule used in `step`, and is the least subset of `R` with those two closure properties. Therefore `S0 ‚äÜ A`, and from the closure clause of `thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143` we get `step S ‚äÜ A` whenever `S ‚äÜ A`; by induction, `Nat.iterate step n S0 ‚äÜ A` for every `n`, in particular for `n = R.card`. For the reverse inclusion, every iterate stays inside `R` and the operator `step` is inflationary; if some iterate before stage `R.card` were not fixed, `step` would add at least one new element of `R`, so the finite chain can strictly increase at most `R.card` times. Thus `Nat.iterate step R.card S0` is a fixed point of `step` inside `R`, contains the base state, and is predecessor-closed, so the leastness part of `thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143` yields `A ‚äÜ Nat.iterate step R.card S0`, giving equality.

op_000146.source_statement: For every root state `p` and every finite set `R` with `q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q`, if `S‚āÄ := {([# (p.2)], p.2)} ‚ą© R` and `S_{n+1}` is obtained from `S‚āô` by adding every `q ‚ąą R` having an `AtomicResidualGraphStep`-successor in `S‚āô`, then for every `n` and every `q ‚ąą R`, `q ‚ąą S‚āô` if and only if there is a path of length at most `n` from `q` to `([# (p.2)], p.2)`.
op_000146.formalized_statement: ‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState, (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí let S : Nat ‚Üí Set AtomicResidualState := Nat.rec (({([# (p.2)], p.2)} : Set AtomicResidualState) ‚ą© (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState)) (fun _ Sn => Sn ‚ą™ {a : AtomicResidualState | a ‚ąą R ‚ąß ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep a r ‚ąß r ‚ąą Sn}); ‚ąÄ n : Nat, ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚Üí (q ‚ąą S n ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ k ‚Č§ n, AtomicBranchLength q.1 q.2 k)
op_000146.statement_formalization_notes: I encoded "path of length at most n" via the existing `AtomicBranchLength`; for reachable `q`, its target base state coincides with `([# (p.2)], p.2)`.

op_000146.proof_sketch: Use induction on `n` for the recursively defined set `S`. For `n = 0`, if `q ‚ąą R` then `q ‚ąą S 0` means exactly `q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)`; this is equivalent to `AtomicBranchLength q.1 q.2 0` once we note that every reachable `q` has `q.2 = p.2`, proved by induction on `(hR q).mp hqR` because each constructor of `AtomicResidualGraphStep` preserves the second component. For the successor step, `q ‚ąą S (n+1)` means either `q ‚ąą S n`, which gives some `k ‚Č§ n` by the induction hypothesis and hence `k ‚Č§ n+1`, or `q ‚ąą R` and there is `r` with `AtomicResidualGraphStep q r` and `r ‚ąą S n`; from reachability of `q` and this step, `r` is also in `R`, so the induction hypothesis gives `AtomicBranchLength r.1 r.2 k` for some `k ‚Č§ n`, and the corresponding `AtomicBranchLength.step_rdiv` or `step_ldiv` constructor gives branch length `k+1` for `q`. Conversely, assume `‚ąÉ k ‚Č§ n+1, AtomicBranchLength q.1 q.2 k`; if `k = 0`, then the only possibility is the base constructor, so `q = ([# (q.2)], q.2) = ([# (p.2)], p.2)` and thus `q ‚ąą S 0 ‚äÜ S (n+1)`. If `k = m+1`, inspect the last constructor of `AtomicBranchLength` to obtain some successor `r` with `AtomicResidualGraphStep q r` and `AtomicBranchLength r.1 r.2 m`; again `r ‚ąą R` by `hR`, so the induction hypothesis gives `r ‚ąą S n`, and therefore `q ‚ąą S (n+1)` by the recursive clause defining `S`.

          q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
            (q ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n ‚ÜĒ
              ‚ąÉ m : Nat, m ‚Č§ n ‚ąß atomic_residual_path_to_base p q m)
op_000146.statement_formalization_notes: Used minimal prelude definitions for the S_n recursion and exact path length, since the current library exposes `ReflTransGen` but not a canonical bounded-length state-path predicate.

op_000146.proof_sketch: Induct on n. The base case reduces membership in the initial saturation layer to equality with the distinguished base state. In the successor case, unfold both recursive definitions: old members use the induction hypothesis directly, and new members correspond exactly to adding one graph step in front of a shorter path. The hypothesis characterizing R as the reachable region gives closure of R under one-step successors.
op_000146.prelude_code:
def atomic_residual_saturation (p : AtomicResidualState) (R : Finset AtomicResidualState) : Nat ‚Üí Set AtomicResidualState
  | 0 => {q | q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąß q ‚ąą R}
  | n + 1 =>
      atomic_residual_saturation p R n ‚ą™
        {q | q ‚ąą R ‚ąß ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n}

def atomic_residual_path_to_base (p q : AtomicResidualState) : Nat ‚Üí Prop
  | 0 => q = ([# (p.2)], p.2)
  | n + 1 =>
      ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß atomic_residual_path_to_base p r n
op_000146.proof_text:
intro p R hR
have hstep_mem :
    ‚ąÄ {q r : AtomicResidualState},
      q ‚ąą R ‚Üí AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚Üí r ‚ąą R := by
  intro q r hqR hstep
  exact (hR r).2 (Relation.ReflTransGen.tail ((hR q).1 hqR) hstep)
intro n
induction n with
| zero =>
    intro q hqR
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hqSat
      have hqbase : q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) := by
        simpa [atomic_residual_saturation, hqR] using hqSat
      exact ‚ü®0, le_rfl, by simpa [atomic_residual_path_to_base] using hqbase‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®m, hmle, hmPath‚ü©
      have hm0 : m = 0 := Nat.eq_zero_of_le_zero hmle
      subst hm0
      simpa [atomic_residual_saturation, atomic_residual_path_to_base, hqR] using hmPath
| succ n ih =>
    intro q hqR
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hqSat
      change q ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n ‚ą®
        q ‚ąą R ‚ąß ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n at hqSat
      rcases hqSat with hOld | hNew
      ¬∑ rcases (ih q hqR).1 hOld with ‚ü®m, hmle, hmPath‚ü©
        exact ‚ü®m, Nat.le_succ_of_le hmle, hmPath‚ü©
      ¬∑ rcases hNew with ‚ü®_, ‚ü®r, hstep, hrSat‚ü©‚ü©
        have hrR : r ‚ąą R := hstep_mem hqR hstep
        rcases (ih r hrR).1 hrSat with ‚ü®m, hmle, hmPath‚ü©
        refine ‚ü®m + 1, Nat.succ_le_succ hmle, ?_‚ü©
        exact ‚ü®r, hstep, hmPath‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®m, hmle, hmPath‚ü©
      change q ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n ‚ą®
        q ‚ąą R ‚ąß ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n
      cases m with
      | zero =>
          have hqSatN : q ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n :=
            (ih q hqR).2 ‚ü®0, Nat.zero_le n, hmPath‚ü©
          exact Or.inl hqSatN
      | succ m =>
          rcases hmPath with ‚ü®r, hstep, hrPath‚ü©
          have hrR : r ‚ąą R := hstep_mem hqR hstep
          have hrSat : r ‚ąą atomic_residual_saturation p R n :=
            (ih r hrR).2 ‚ü®m, Nat.le_of_succ_le_succ hmle, hrPath‚ü©
          exact Or.inr ‚ü®hqR, ‚ü®r, hstep, hrSat‚ü©‚ü©

op_000147.source_statement: For every root state `p` and every finite set `R` with `q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q`, the set `{q ‚ąą R | AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q}` is the least fixed point of the operator `F(S) := ({([# (p.2)], p.2)} ‚ą© R) ‚ą™ {q ‚ąą R | ‚ąÉ r, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą S}` on subsets of `R`.
op_000147.formalized_statement:
‚ąÄ p : AtomicResidualState, ‚ąÄ R : Finset AtomicResidualState,
  (‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState, q ‚ąą R ‚ÜĒ Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) ‚Üí
    let A : Set AtomicResidualState := {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą R ‚ąß AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q}
    let F : Set AtomicResidualState ‚Üí Set AtomicResidualState := fun S =>
      (({([# (p.2)], p.2)} : Set AtomicResidualState) ‚ą© (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState)) ‚ą™
        {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą R ‚ąß ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą S}
    A = F A ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ S : Set AtomicResidualState, S ‚äÜ (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState) ‚Üí F S = S ‚Üí A ‚äÜ S
op_000147.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized least fixed point explicitly as fixedness plus minimality among subset-of-R fixed points of the operator.

op_000147.proof_sketch: Unfold the least-fixed-point statement. For fixedness, use `thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097`; in the base case, a short induction on `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q` shows every reachable `q` has `q.2 = p.2`, so the only accepting base reachable from `p` is `([# (p.2)], p.2)`. For minimality, extract from `F S = S` that `S` contains the base state whenever it lies in `R` and is closed under predecessors inside `R`, then apply `thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143`.
op_000147.proof_text:
intro p R hR
dsimp
have hsame :
    ‚ąÄ {q : AtomicResidualState},
      Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q ‚Üí q.2 = p.2 := by
  intro q hq
  induction hq with
  | refl =>
      rfl
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      cases hstep <;> simpa using ih
have hreachLeast := thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143 p R hR
dsimp at hreachLeast
rcases hreachLeast with ‚ü®_, hA_base, hA_step, hleast‚ü©
constructor
¬∑ ext q
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro hqA
    rcases hqA with ‚ü®hqR, hqacc‚ü©
    rcases (thm_residual_accepts_base_or_step_000097 q).mp hqacc with hbase | ‚ü®r, hstep, hracc‚ü©
    ¬∑ left
      constructor
      ¬∑ show q ‚ąą ({([# (p.2)], p.2)} : Set AtomicResidualState)
        have hs : q.2 = p.2 := hsame ((hR q).mp hqR)
        simpa [hs] using hbase
      ¬∑ exact hqR
    ¬∑ right
      have hreachq : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q := (hR q).mp hqR
      have hrR : r ‚ąą R := (hR r).mpr (Relation.ReflTransGen.tail hreachq hstep)
      exact ‚ü®hqR, r, hstep, ‚ü®hrR, hracc‚ü©‚ü©
  ¬∑ intro hqF
    rcases hqF with hqbase | ‚ü®hqR, r, hstep, hrA‚ü©
    ¬∑ rcases hqbase with ‚ü®hqmem, hqR‚ü©
      have hqeq : q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) := by
        simpa using hqmem
      have hpR : ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą R := by
        simpa [hqeq] using hqR
      simpa [hqeq] using hA_base hpR
    ¬∑ exact hA_step q hqR ‚ü®r, hstep, hrA‚ü©
¬∑ intro S hSR hFS
  have hSbase : (([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą R) ‚Üí ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą S := by
    intro hpR
    have hpFS :
        ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą
          (({([# (p.2)], p.2)} : Set AtomicResidualState) ‚ą© (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState)) ‚ą™
            {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą R ‚ąß ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą S} := by
      left
      constructor
      ¬∑ simp
      ¬∑ exact hpR
    simpa [hFS] using hpFS
  have hSstep :
      ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,
        q ‚ąą R ‚Üí
          (‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą S) ‚Üí
            q ‚ąą S := by
    intro q hqR hsucc
    have hqFS :
        q ‚ąą
          (({([# (p.2)], p.2)} : Set AtomicResidualState) ‚ą© (‚ÜĎR : Set AtomicResidualState)) ‚ą™
            {q : AtomicResidualState | q ‚ąą R ‚ąß ‚ąÉ r : AtomicResidualState, AtomicResidualGraphStep q r ‚ąß r ‚ąą S} := by
      right
      exact ‚ü®hqR, hsucc‚ü©
    simpa [hFS] using hqFS
  exact hleast S hSR hSbase hSstep

The previous proof was essentially correct; the repair is to rewrite the minimality goals with `rw [‚Üź hFS]` instead of `simpa [hFS]`, so membership in `S` is reduced to membership in the explicit union defining `F S` in the correct direction.

    ¬∑ left

  ¬∑ intro hqF
    rcases hqF with hqbase | ‚ü®hqR, r, hstep, hrA‚ü©
    ¬∑ rcases hqbase with ‚ü®hqmem, hqR‚ü©
      have hqeq : q = ([# (p.2)], p.2) := by

¬∑ intro S hSR hFS
  have hSbase : (([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą R) ‚Üí ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą S := by
    intro hpR

        ([# (p.2)], p.2) ‚ąą

      ‚ąÄ q : AtomicResidualState,

paper_claim_plan_01.proof_sketch: Existing results cover the full reachable topological sweep (`thm_topological_terminal_sweep_accepts_000137`), least backward-closed reachable subsets (`thm_reachable_accepts_least_set_000103`, `thm_accepts_least_reachable_subset_000143`), and bounded saturation/path length (`thm_saturation_iff_bounded_path_000146`). I could not identify a confirmed theorem in the current library that bridges those facts to the stated arbitrary-suffix reverse-fold update with the exact 'all vertices lie in S' path characterization. Without the concrete fold/update definitions behind `stmt`, any Lean proof script here would be too speculative.

I found a different plausible route than the previous attempt: use `thm_topological_terminal_sweep_accepts_000137` for the full list `L`, observe that any suffix of a reverse-topological list is successor-closed, and combine that with the operator characterization in `thm_accepts_reachable_least_fixpoint_000147` to show the restricted sweep on the suffix computes the least backward-closed subset of that suffix, hence the within-suffix path predicate. However, the current library search did not reveal any formal declaration for the suffix backward-update/fold itself, nor a theorem packaging the 'suffix of a reverse-topological list is closed under successors' step in the exact API needed by `stmt`. Since `stmt` is only given here in natural language, writing Lean tactics would require inventing the concrete fold/update definitions and the exact formal target shape, which would be speculative.

However, the repository still does not expose a concrete formal definition for the suffix-restricted reverse update / intermediate fold state, nor a packaged theorem turning an arbitrary suffix of a reverse-topological list into the successor-closed subregion needed by the statement. Because `stmt` is only given here in natural language, producing Lean tactics would require guessing the exact formal target and API for the suffix sweep, which would be speculative rather than defensible.
